text: Precision engine bearing inserts are manufactured with a small portion of the bearing ends extending beyond the  bearing housing or caps. The installation process of these bearings requires sufficient ________.
The piston rod scraper box incorporated in a two-stroke/cycle, crosshead diesel engine serves to  _____________.
options: eliminate the necessity for an oil scraper ring
prevent side thrust and cylinder scoring
prevent sludge and dirty oil from entering the crankcase
scrape oil and carbon deposits off the cylinder walls

text: The main advantage of unit injectors (combined fuel pump and injector) over other fuel  injection systems is _____________.
options: the lack of high pressure fuel lines
the lessened chance of fuel leaks into the engine sump

text: When may the crankcase ventilation pipes or oil drain pipes of two or more engines be connected?
options: Propulsion engines under 1000 shaft horsepower may share a common crankcase vent provided the oil drains remain  separate.
In most cases it is desirable and cost effective for  propulsion engines to share a common crankcase ventilation and monitoring system.
No interconnection may be made between the crankcase ventilation pipes or oil drain pipes.
None of the above are correct.

text: On most modern diesel engines, the main and connecting rod bearings receive their lubricating oil by ____________.
Lubricating oil is supplied to the crankpin bearings in a marine diesel engine by _____________.
The camshaft drive is designed to maintain proper camshaft speed relative to crankshaft speed. In maintaining this relationship, the camshaft drive causes the camshaft to  rotate at _____________.
options: one-half crankshaft speed in a two-stroke cycle diesel  engine
crankshaft speed in a two-stroke cycle diesel engine
two times crankshaft speed in a two-stroke cycle diesel  engine
one-fourth times crankshaft speed in a four-stroke cycle diesel engine

text: The opening of an exhaust valve on a modern, large, low- speed, main propulsion diesel engine, may be actuated by _____________.
options: direct action of the main piston moving down

text: The method of piston cooling in which oil is delivered  through the connecting rod to a compartment within the  piston, then distributed by the motion of the pistons, and allowed to drain to the crankcase via one or more holes or pipes, is termed ________
In a large, slow-speed, main propulsion diesel engine, which of the parts listed is under tension when the engine is  running?
Passages are drilled in the crankshafts of diesel engines to provide lubricating oil to the _____________.
The main function of tie rods in the construction of large, low speed diesel engines is to ____________.
options: stiffen the bedplate in way of the main bearings to increase the engine's longitudinal strength
accept most of the tensile loading that results from the firing forces developed during operation
mount the engine frame securely to the hull to prevent shaft coupling misalignment
connect the crosshead solidly to the piston rod

text: Fuel injection pumps using the port and helix metering  principle requires the use of a ___________.
gudgeonpin bearings are difficult to lubricate because of  their oscillating motion and _____________.
options: the reciprocating motion of the piston
their position in the lubrication system

text: Engines having a bore exceeding 250 mm, but not exceeding 300 mm are to have at least _________.
options: three compression rings per piston and the minimum of two oil scraper rings
one intake and one exhaust valve per cylinder provided no other means of scavenging is used
one explosion relief valve in way of each alternate crank throw, with a minimum of two valves
one crankshaft except in cases where an opposed piston  design is required

text: Shaker, circulation, and spray are the three general methods used in _____________.

A two stroke diesel engine exhaust temperaturewill be lower than a four stroke diesel engine of the same displacement because the ___________.  I. scavenging air is cooling the exhaust gases  II. exhaust cycle time is longer
Where is the fuel delivery check valve located in a jerk pump fuel injection system?
options: On the suction side of the delivery pump
In the injection pump housing discharge
On the inlet side of the spray valves

text: Which of the air intake systems listed will result in the lowest specific fuel consumption?
Camshafts are usually driven by timing gears or ___________.
The arrangement and shape of the cams on a diesel engine camshaft directly control which of the listed groups of  operating conditions?
options: Firing order, valve timing, and valve lift
Fuel consumption, efficiency, and cylinder pressure
Scavenge pressure, compression ratio, and exhaust pressure

text: A port-and-helix fuel injection pump having upper and lower plunger helixes is designed to _____________.
options: vary fuel delivery and return pressure
vary the beginning and ending of injection

text: Some fuel injection systems utilize port-and-helix metering. Which of the following statement describes a system "timed for port closing"?
options: Injection has a variable beginning and a constant ending.
Injection is metered by an external delivery valve.
Injection has a constant beginning and variable ending.
Injection will not occur until the helix closes the delivery valve.

text: A crankshaft whose center of gravity coincides with its  center line is said to be __________.
The small end of the connecting rod is attached to the  piston with a _____________.
options: sliding wedge the pressure at not more than _________. 0.2 bar

text: The sealing surfaces of a diesel engine piston ring are  considered to be the faces in contact with the cylinder  wall, in addition to the ring groove ___________.
How many crankcase relief valves are required for a 325mm bore, eight cylinder in-line engine?
In which of the following areas of a crosshead engine is a permanently connected fire extinguishing system required?
options: Exhaust manifolds in excess of eight inches in diameter.
Crankcases having a gross volume in excess of 21 cubic feet.
Turbocharger inlet piping in excess of twelve inches in  diameter.
Scavenging spaces in open connection to the cylinders.

text: The valve cam slope angle determines the _____________.
options: opening and closing points of the valve
opening and closing rate of the valve
amount of time the valve remains open

text: Carbon deposits building up, in, and around the injection nozzle tip are least likely to occur when using which of the listed types of fuel injector nozzles?
Which of the following statements describes a fuel injection pump marked "timed for port closing"?
options: Injection has a constant beginning and variable ending.
The pump stroke determines the amount of fuel injected.
Fuel is metered by the pump's delivery valve.
Timing reference marks should be changed.

text: An individual injection pump is designed for variable  beginning and constant ending of injection. For diesel  engines operating at constant speeds, the start of injection will _____________.
options: always occur at top dead center

text: The amount of fuel injected in a particular time, or degree, of crankshaft rotation is termed _____________.
At dead center, the centerline of the connecting rod  usually coincides with the _____________.
Connecting rods in a diesel engine are used to connect the _____________.
The purpose of a heat dam used in some diesel engine cast iron pistons is to ____________.
options: concentrate all heat in the piston crown
increase the distance of travel for heat from the crown to the top ring groove
ensure that all heat in the piston crown is conducted to the top ring
provide a short direct path for heat to flow from the crown to the top ring

text: A diesel engine with a full speed of 1000 RPM drives a  propeller at 300 RPM. What is the speed reduction ratio?
Why is the ring belt narrower in diameter than the skirt of a piston designed for a diesel engine?
options: To allow for greater expansion due to higher operating temperature.
To seal the cylinder against leakage of combustion gases.
To provide an additional surface for oil cooling.
To provide additional strength for the crown and lower  structure.

text: The diameter of a piston is usually less at the crown than at the skirt, in order to ______________.
options: facilitate the installation of piston rings
allow for the expansion of the piston during operation
prevent crankcase vapors from entering the combustion  chamber
reduce wearing of the upper cylinder liner

text: The fuel injection pumps on a diesel engine are controlled by a linkage system attached to the _____________.
The ability of a fuel particle to travel into the combustion chamber before burning is called _____________.
The function of the window cast into the housing of an  individual jerk pump is to ____________.
options: allow the pump to be timed to the engine
check for sludge on the pump barrel
check that the fuel return passages are clear
set up the fuel rack calibration in cubic millimeters

text: Turbulence in a diesel engine cylinder is of major   importance in providing _____________.
Cavitation erosion in the cooling water system of a diesel engine usually occurs at the pump impeller, and on the  waterside of the _____________.
Movement of the pump control rack in a fuel injection system using individual plunger-type pumps _____________.
options: changes the position of the fuel inlet ports
changes the length of the pump stroke
varies the quantity of fuel delivered
varies the compression of the delivery valve spring

text: Diesel engines are classified as reciprocating internal  combustion engines because they ______________.
options: use energy from fuel burned outside their cylinders
burn fuel in a combustion chamber that moves back and forth
burn fuel in a chamber where its energy moves a piston back and forth
use a continuous combustion process to impart rotary motion to the pistons

text: Which of the listed design features is most common to a  two- stroke/cycle low-speed main propulsion diesel engines?
Which statement about diesel engine combustion is true?
options: Combustion does not begin until the piston starts down  on the power stroke.
Maximum combustion pressure is reached before TDC.
Turbulence in the cylinder causes a delay in ignition.
Maximum cylinder firing pressure is not developed   until the piston passes TDC.

text: Why are some diesel engine cylinder liners plated on the wearing surface with porous chromium?
options: The chromium will not wear out the piston rings.
Chromium eliminates the need for oil scraper rings.
The chromium strengthens the liners in the way of the  scavenging air ports.
Pores in the plating aid in maintaining the lube oil film.

text: What type of fitting is to be used on diesel engine fuel injection line piping?
Theoretical perfect combustion in a diesel engine yields by-products of _____________.
The piston gudgeon pin used in some diesel engine pistons is prevented from contacting the cylinder wall by a __________.
The load is always placed on the lower half of the main  bearings in a/an ____________.
The main operating characteristic of diesel engines which distinguishes them from other internal combustion engines is the _____________.
Which term describes piston pins having bearing surfaces in both the piston bosses and connecting rod eye?
Piston cooling fins are located _____________.
options: on top of the piston crown
at the base of the piston skirt
inside the cylinder liner cooling water jacket

text: Besides the use of piston cooling fins to assist in cooling,they also provide extra strength for the piston ___________.
A thrust bearing is used with a propulsion diesel engine to _____________.
options: control axial movement of the crankshaft
transmit engine thrust to the propeller shaft
absorb vibrations in the propeller shafting
prevent propeller thrust from being transmitted to the hull

text: Which of the following statements pertains to propulsion engines with bores exceeding 200 mm?
options: There shall be a means to display the cooling water outlet temperature of each cylinder.
All engines connected to controllable pitch propellers shall be of the direct reversible type.
The engines will be fitted with a means to display the  exhaust gas temperature of each cylinder.
All of the above are correct.

text: What is the function of the main thrust bearing?
options: Prevents lateral movement of the slow speed gear.
Transmits propeller thrust to the hull.
Prevents main reduction gear axial movement.

text: The function of diesel engine piston compression rings is to ____________.
options: transmit heat from the piston to the cylinder liner
remove oil from the cylinder combustion space

text: Modern marine diesel engines equipped with mechanical fuel injection operate on a combustion phase within the cycle which is _________.
options: a combination of constant volume and constant pressure
a combination of constant temperature and constant pressure

text: The purpose of counterboring the top of the cylinder liner, extending down to the top point of travel of the top  compression ring, is to _____________.
options: prevent wear of the liner from occurring
prevent wear of the liner from forming a ridge at the upper level of ring travel
decrease compression ratio for easier starting

text: Exhaust gas pyrometers are useful for ____________.
Compared to other fuel injection systems, unit injectors operate with virtually no _____________.
A properly honed diesel engine cylinder liner will ________.
The minimum fuel oil delivery pressure required for   efficient injection depends primarily on the ______________.
options: maximum pressure in the engine cylinders during injection
quantity of the fuel to be injected
duration of the ignition delay period

text: The two strokes of a two-stroke/cycle diesel engine are ____________.
Where engine bores exceed 230 mm, a bursting disc or flame arrestor is fitted ____________.
options: at the supply inlet to the starting air manifold for  non-reversing engines
on the exhaust manifold prior to the inlet of the   turbocharger
on all devices subject to the by-products of combustion or lubrication system vapors
in way of the control valve of each cylinder for direct  reversing engines having a main starting manifold

text: The main function of piston compression rings is to _______.
options: reduce friction losses in the engine
seal the space between the piston and the liner
limit upward flow of lube oil into the combustion space

text: Diesel engine piston ring gaps can be straight or angle cut. In comparison, the angle cut ring ____________.
Which of the listed designs is effectively used to provide the turbulence necessary for proper combustion in a diesel engine cylinder?
The most rapid period of fuel combustion in a diesel engine cylinder should begin just before the piston reaches top dead center and ____________.
options: when fuel injection has been completed
when fuel vaporization has been completed
should efficiently continue through the after burning period in all properly designed engines
should be completed shortly after passing top dead center

text: Oil control rings function to _____________.
options: adjusting fuel racks to maintain equal loading between  cylinders
adjusting the load limit setting of the governor at idle conditions

text: In the construction of a diesel engine, what is the purpose of end plates?
options: To provide accessibility to the cylinder liners.
To add rigidity to the block and a surface for attaching other parts.
To add stability to the engine block.
To make a surface for the base.

text: What is commonly used to create turbulence in a diesel engine combustion system?
To shut down a diesel engine after it exceeds the set  maximum speed, which type of device listed should be used?
To function properly, oil control rings used on a diesel engine piston must distribute sufficient oil to all parts of the cylinder wall and must also _____________.
options: prevent any lubricant from reaching the compression rings
prevent excessive lubrication from reaching the combustion space
provide metal-to-metal contact to seal the cylinder against blow-by
assure a positive means of scraping carbon accumulation from the cylinder

text: In some modern large diesel engines, which of the followingis used as the support for the main bearings?
In an internal combustion engine, which of the devices  listed will force the compression rings to seal the   compression gases in the space above the piston?
options: Gas pressure acting against the back of the ring

text: The size of the exhaust valve opening is ____________.
options: most critical in a four-stroke/cycle diesel engine
most critical in a two-stroke/cycle diesel engine
most critical in a four-stroke/cycle diesel engine if it is turbocharged
of equal importance in a two-stroke/cycle diesel engine as in a four- stroke/cycle diesel engine

text: Which of the general advantages listed does the electrical pyrometer have over the mechanical pyrometer?
options: When heated, it will move proportional to the amount the metal has lengthened or expanded.
The pointer associated with the pyrometer scale can be made to also measure engine RPM. reduce the amount of lube oil burned in the combustion  chamber
It can be utilized in exhaust manifolds and heat exchangers interchangeably. scrape excess lube oil from the liner on the downstroke
It can indicate temperature at a distant point from the  source of heat. all of the above

text: The main purpose of the piston oil scraper rings is to _____________.
options: seal the space between the piston and the liner
reduce the amount of lube oil burned in the combustion  chamber
transmit heat from the piston to the cylinder liner
damp out fluctuations of the piston side thrust

text: How many power strokes per crankshaft revolution are there in an eight cylinder, two-stroke/cycle diesel engine?
Turbulence is created in the cylinders of a diesel engine to _____________.
An important design characteristic of an explosion relief valve for a diesel engine is the ability to _________.
options: close quickly in order to prevent an inrush of air
open quickly against crankcase pressure to prevent a  possible implosion
open slowly to permit a gradual reduction of crankcase  pressure
close slowly to permit proper seating of the valve disc and neoprene sealing surfaces

text: Turbulence in the cylinder of a two-stroke/cycle main  propulsion diesel engine is mainly created by _____________.
The theoretical minimum compression ratio necessary to  ensure compression ignition in a direct injection diesel engine is _____________.
Provision is to be made for ventilation of an enclosed  diesel engine crankcase by means of a small ________.
options: fan to develop a slight suction not exceeding 25mm of water
vent line attached to the upper most area of the crankcase near the center of the engine
breather or by means of a slight suction not exceeding 25mm of water

text: In a large slow-speed propulsion diesel engine, the force applied to the crosshead is ______________.
options: against the crosshead during power stroke and away from the crosshead during the compression stroke 1980 to 2070 RPM
against the crosshead during the compression stroke and away from the crosshead during the power stroke 2100 to 2200 RPM
against the crosshead during the power and compression  strokes 2200 to 2300 RPM
away from the crosshead during the power and compression strokes 2300 to 2400 RPM

text: A six cylinder 2-stroke/cycle, single acting diesel engine has a 580 mm bore and a 1700 mm stroke. What indicated  power per cylinder will be developed if the average mean effective pressure is 15.3 kg/cm2 at a speed of 120 RPM?
Diesel engine valve springs function to _____________.
options: keep the valves off their seats until the exhaust stroke is completed
open inlet valves when the air injection cycle begins

text: A six cylinder, two stroke/cycle diesel engine is 83%  efficient and has a cylinder constant of 0.998 while  operating with a mean effective pressure of 15 kg/cm2 at a speed of 100 RPM. What is the metric brake horse power developed?
Between the periods of injection and ignition of the fuel, a diesel engine crankshaft rotates through the ____________.
The purpose of an interference angle in a diesel engineexhaust valve is to ________.  I. work in conjunction with valve rotators to rotate   the valve  II. seat the valve quickly
One function of diesel engine lubricating oil is to _______.
options: induce carbon formation on cylinder walls
improve fuel penetration in the combustion space
form a friction reducing film between mating surfaces

text: In a diesel engine, exhaust valves open before the intakeports are uncovered to ___________.  I. reduce pumping losses  II. reduce back pressure
The amount of fuel injected into a diesel engine cylinder by a unit injector, is controlled by _____________.
options: the firing pressure in the cylinder
a metering helix inside the pump
varying the physical length of the plunger stroke
varying the clearance between the injector cam and the  injector rocker arm

text: A diesel engine operating at a light load, when compared to operating at heavy load has a fuel/air ratio that is   __________.
options: less said to be __________.   I. staticly balanced       II. dynamically balanced II only

text: Using a diesel engine indicator P-V diagram, the cylinder mean effective pressure is calculated to be 21.3 kg/cm2. What is the scale of the spring used on the indicator if the diagram area is 18.46 cm2 with a length of 13 cm?
A built-up exhaust valve is one in which _____________.
options: the stem and heads are made of different material
the self-centering action comes from motion of the valve stem in the guide
a replaceable valve disk is welded to the head

text: A seven cylinder, 2-stroke/cycle, single acting diesel  engine has a 750 mm bore and a 2000 mm stroke. What  indicated power will be developed if the average mean  effective pressure is 14.8 kg/cm2 at a speed of 96 RPM?
The cylinder liner forming the cylinder wall and the inside of the water jacket is called a _____________.
The purpose of an interference angle in a diesel engineexhaust valve is to ________.  I. seat the valve quickly  II. break up seat deposits
Open combustion chambers are designed to _____________.
Critical speeds occurring within the operating speed range of a main propulsion diesel engine may be changed, or have their damaging effects reduced by a/an _____________.
Which bearing half will receive the greatest load in a  two- stroke/cycle diesel engine?
options: All bearing halves share an equal load.
Upper half of the main bearing.
Lower half of the piston pin bearing in the connecting rod.
Lower half of the connecting rod bearing at the crankshaft end of the rod.

text: A safety cover differs from other diesel engine access doors in that it is fitted with a __________.
In an opposed piston engine, which of the following events would happen if the lower crank lead were reduced from 12‚ to 0‚?
options: The exhaust ports would open before the scavenging ports.
The scavenging ports would open before the exhaust ports.
Neither the exhaust nor the scavenging ports would open.
The exhaust and scavenging ports would open simultaneously.

text: The valve stem expansion associated with engine warm-up is allowed for by the ____________.
A dry-type spark arrestor removes sparks from a diesel  engine exhaust by _____________.
options: increasing the linear velocity of the exhaust gases
changing directions of exhaust gas flow
decreasing the temperature of the exhaust gases
accelerating the exhaust gas through a reduced size orifice

text: When two cams of the same diameter, one with tangential flanks and the other with convex flanks are compared, the cam with tangential flanks will cause___________.
The average pressure exerted on a piston during each power stroke is termed _____________.
Wet-type exhaust silencers, used with some diesel engines, have which of the following design features in common?
options: The silencer is equipped with a water seal.
The exhaust gases are not mixed with cooling water.
The internal baffles break up the exhaust gas pulsation.
The exhaust noise is completely eliminated.

text: In a diesel engine, after ignition of the fuel occurs, but before the piston reaches TDC, there is little change in the cylinder _____________.
Main propulsion diesel engines having a bore exceeding 300 mm are to have at least _________.
options: two independent means of starting the engine
two engine driven lube oil pumps capable of parallel  operation
one (explosion relief) valve at the position of each main crank throw
five air starting valves to permit the admission of starting air at any crank angle

text: A controllable pitch propeller on a diesel driven vessel eliminates the need for ____________.
Mist detectors used on large low-speed main propulsion  diesel engines monitor and check for the presence of  ____________.
Which of the following relationships should occur between the temperature developed in a combustion space, and the compression ratio of the engine?
options: Higher compression ratios create higher temperature.
Higher temperatures create higher compression ratios.
Lower temperatures create higher compression ratios.
Higher compression ratios create lower temperatures.

text: Which of the bearing types listed is commonly used for main bearings in small internal combustion engines?
The most common instrument used to measure diesel engine exhaust pressure is the _____________.
Which of the following types of engines have a combustion chamber located between a cylinder head and the crown of a piston?
The greatest turbulence in a diesel engine cylinder is  created by the _____________.
options: degree of penetration of the fuel oil droplets

text: The exhaust system for a diesel engine is usually designed to remove exhaust gases and to _____________.
options: remove the emission of exhaust smoke pollutants

text: Most practical diesel engines today operate on a cycle which is a combination of the Diesel and Otto cycles. In this process, compression ignition _____________.
options: begins on a constant volume basis
begins on a constant pressure basis
ends on a constant volume basis
begins and ends on a constant volume basis

text: In an opposed piston engine, the term "crank lead" refers to _____________.
options: one crankshaft turning faster than the other
the two crankshafts turning in different directions
the piston in one cylinder reaching inner dead center  several crankshaft degrees before the other piston
the piston in one cylinder reaching dead center when the other reaches outer dead center

text: Which of the events listed does NOT occur during the instant the piston just reaches top dead center?
A main propulsion diesel engine crankshaft bearing lacking sufficient "crush", will _____________.
options: unburned fuel vapors in the scavenge air receiver
lube oil vapors in the crank case
lube oil vapors in the engine room

text: A unit type fuel injector is used on a diesel engine to _____________.
options: produce the proper fuel oil pressure

text: The closing of the exhaust valves used on a modern, large, low- speed, main propulsion diesel engine may be directly provided by _____________.
A diesel engine crankcase ventilation system ______________.
options: determines the level of combustible gases
provides inert gas generation in crankcase

text: The bearings used to support the crankshaft are generally called __________.
Internal combustion engine crankcase vent outlets must be equipped with ______.
Cylinder lubrication oil for low speed main propulsion  diesel engines is admitted to each cylinder during   _____________.
Which of the following listed construction details of  internal combustion engines is required?
options: A warning notice to caution against the opening of a hot crankcase for a specified period of time after shut down.
The use of end block construction for engines developing over 1000 brake horsepower.
Removable cylinder liners must be used for engines   developing over 1000 brake horsepower.
All engines shall be provided with an exhaust gas pressure monitoring system.

text: The term "proper metering", as applied to a diesel fuel  injection system, can be best defined as _____________.
options: delivering the same quantity of fuel to each cylinder for each power stroke according to engine load
maintaining the metering adjustment for a reasonable period under all load conditions
timing fuel injection to obtain maximum power and good fuel economy
distributing the fuel to all parts of the combustion chamber for proper combustion

text: The rate of fuel injection in a diesel engine cylinder  depends primarily on _____________.
options: the size of the holes in the fuel nozzle
timing of the pump be lubricated more easily than with sufficient crush
supply pressure to the pump tend to rotate with the journal
have its back forced against the seat under load

text: The intake valves in a diesel engine are reseated by _____________.
A characteristic of a bearing material which permits small dirt particles to become embedded in it's surface is ________________.
options: desirable, as it will prevent damage to the journal surface
desirable, as it will assist in keeping the lube oil filters clean
undesirable, since the embedded particles will score the journal
undesirable, since the particles will interfere with lube oil flow

text: Most fuel injection nozzles are opened by _____________.
options: timing gears keyed to the crankshaft

text: Injection pressure in a common rail fuel system is   controlled by _____________.
options: varying the fuel pump piston stroke
varying the injector needle valve clearance

text: What is to be installed on an internal combustion engine if its cylinder bore exceeds 200mm?
What is the purpose of the "window" installed in the housing of an individual jerk pump?
options: To allow the pump to be timed to the engine.
To check for sludge on the pump barrel.
To check that fuel oil return passages are clear.
To set up the fuel rack calibration in cubic millimeters.

text: Which statement regarding the arrangement and location of explosion relief valves used on an internal combustion  engines is true_____________?
options: They may be omitted on all engines having a cylinder bore of nine inches or less
They may be omitted provided the engine utilizes a crankcase monitoring system
The type of engine and operating cycle must be considered by the designer
Minimizing the danger from emission of flame is a key consideration

text: One function of the fuel pump delivery check valve is to _____________.
options: prevent carbon deposits from forming on the injector nozzle
help the injector needle reseat without dribbling at the nozzle holes
provide a prolonged pressure drop in the high pressure steel piping to the injector
ensure a fuel leakoff between the plunger and barrel which provides lubrication for relative movement

text: Fuel injection systems meter fuel, atomize fuel, and _____________.
options: create turbulence in the combustion chamber
inject fuel at the proper time

text: The valve cam slope angle determines the _____________.
options: acceleration rate of valve opening and closing
diameter of intake and exhaust valves

text: Cylinders diameters greater than 230 mm require additional safety devices when the scavenging spaces are openly  connected to the cylinders. Which of the following devices will be used to protect such spaces?
options: Stacked plate type inlet check valves

text: Fuel injectors used in heavy fuel oil systems are usually provided with cooling to reduce _____________.
Increasing the load on an engine using a double-helix type injection pump varies the effective stroke of the pump to start _____________.
The duration of fuel injection developed by an individual port-and- helix fuel injection pump, is determined by the ____________.
When the lower edge of the spiral begins to uncover the  release port in a jerk pump, the _____________.
options: pumping continues until the plunger travels its full stroke
effective pumping stroke of the plunger ends
pressure drops slowly until the full stroke is attained
plunger rotates to the zero delivery position until the next stroke

text: Which of the following will occur when the lower edge of the spiral, on the plunger of a jerk pump, uncovers the spill port?
options: The pressure drops and fuel delivery stops.
The plunger rotates to the no fuel position.
The barrel rotates to the zero effective stroke position.

text: Which of the components listed is NOT found in a single  acting engine?
Cast iron pistons used in large propulsion diesel engines are constructed with ____________.
options: the skirt being tapered and smaller than the crown
the skirt being tapered and larger than the crown
the crown being tapered and smaller than the skirt

text: Successful combustion inside the cylinders of a diesel  engine is dependent upon _____________.
options: good mixing of fuel and air

text: Engine displacement is equal to the cylinder _____________.
options: area times the stroke, times the number of cylinders
volume times the stroke, times the number of cylinders

text: A loop or cross scavenged engine utilizes the motion of its pistons and a turbocharger to provide scavenging air. Which of the listed mechanical designs prevents the air under the pistons from being pumped back through the scavenge ports during the piston
The possibility of damage from operating a diesel engine at critical speeds is reduced by the use of ______________.
options: a cast iron bed plate with good flexible qualities

text: The linear motion of a diesel engine piston is converted to the rotary motion required to drive gears, propeller shafts, and generators by the _____________.
The plunger in a jerk pump is rotated until the release port is uncovered. If the port remains uncovered all of the  time, which of the listed operations will occur?
options: The maximum effective stroke will be attained.
The fuel delivered to the cylinder will be excessive.
The injection nozzle will overheat and carbonize.

text: The amount of fuel delivered by a helical plunger fuel  injection pump is controlled by _____________.
In a diesel engine, pistons are attached to the crankshaft by _____________.
In diesel engines designed with a crosshead, the motion of the piston rod can be described as _____________.
options: reciprocating at the piston pin, rotary at the crank pin
reciprocating at the crank pin, rotary at the piston pin

text: Which of the following manufacturing methods is recommended for diesel engine fuel injection line piping?
Which of the combustion parameters listed is used in a  diesel engine, but NOT related to the injection system?
options: Effective stroke increased by finer atomization
Penetration nonexistent in the precombustion chamber system

text: What is required for crosshead type engines that have a scavenging space in open connection to the cylinder?
options: The air flow from the scavenging space must always be  protected by plate type check valves and under no   circumstance may other devices be used.
A suitable gasket for the interface of both manifolds is necessary to prevent recirculation of scavenging gases,  while additionally minimizing exhaust gas leakage.
The scavenging space is to be permanently connected to an approved fire extinguishing system, entirely separate from the fire extinguishing system of the engine room.
The required equipment for a crosshead type engine is totally dependent upon manufacturer's ability to placate market demands

text: In a diesel engine mechanical-type fuel pump, the delivery check valve is opened by ____________.
Differential needle valves used in fuel injectors are  directly closed by _____________.
Injection lag can be caused by ____________.
options: improper timing of the intake valves

text: A diesel engine with a combustion chamber located between the crowns of two pistons is known as a/an _______________.
An exhaust pipe from a internal combustion engine may not need to be insulated when _________.
options: it is of the water jacketed type
it is used as an emergency generator

text: In diesel engines, the four basic events (intake,   compression, power and exhaust) are performed once in  _______________.

text: Which of the following conditions is NOT an indication of microbial contamination?

options: the pressure drop across the filter
increase flow rate from the filter

text: In diesel engineering practice, the term used to express the ignition quality of a particular fuel is _____________.
Which of the following statements is true concerning the cetane number rating of diesel fuel?
options: The cetane number is obtained by comparing the fuel with cetane, a colorless liquid hydrocarbon.
The higher the cetane number, the shorter the ignition lag.
The highest cetane number of fuel is 100.

text: Most type(s) of microbiological growths in fuel are __________.
Which of the following statements describes the unchecked growth of microbiological organisms within a fuel system?
options: The dying bacteria will cause a coating to be formed on the sides of the tank thereby decreasing corrosion.
The fuel in the tank will loose its fluidity, solidify, and be the cause of an expensive removal process.
Corrosion of various metal components will occur due to the formation of hydrogen sulfide gas.
All of the above are correct.

text: An indication of microbial contamination is ______.
options: usually discovered during normally scheduled strainer  cleaning

text: The desirable properties of a marine fuel oil should include _____________.
options: high flash point and high viscosity
low flash point and high viscosity
low heating value and high sulphur content
high heating value and low sulphur content

text: Which of the listed factors will indicate the most about the ability of a fuel to ignite in a diesel engine?
options: Cetane number large amounts of water to be present

text: Which of the substances listed is satisfactorily removed from the fuel by a centrifugal oil purifier?
Fuel oil having a low cetane rating could result in _______.
With respect to diesel fuel, the ease with which a cold  engine will start is dependent upon the _____________.
options: high heating value of the fuel
amount of carbon residue after combustion
internal flow resistance in the injectors

text: Why are heavy fuels not usually prone to the problems of microbiological infection?
options: Heavy fuels are subjected to better refining processes which prevent the formation of these growths.
Most heavy fuels contain chemicals which prevent the growth of fungi and other bacteria.
Microbiological infection does not affect marine fuel but rather the personnel who are involved with the handling, storage and purification of the fuel.
The necessary nutrients that the organisms feed on are  in a more complex form and not available for microbial  degradation.

text: Fuel oil strainers should be made of ____________.  I. copper  II. brass
When fuel oil heaters are required for main engine   operation, ____________.
options: each heater shall have the capacity to supply the main  engine at full power
at least two heaters of approximately equal size are to be installed
the system shall be designed to permit series or parallel operation

text: One cause of diesel engine fuel ignition delay is _______.
options: mechanical flexibility in the pump mechanism
ignition quality of the fuel oil

text: A substance found in residual fuels which tends to cause exhaust valve corrosion and grooving, is ____________.
To minimize corrosion, fuel oil strainer disks, spacers and scraper blades are made of _____________.
options: monel metal or stainless steel octane number of 45

text: Injection lag in a diesel engine may be caused by _________.
options: a higher cetane number of fuel oil
the diesel fuel used having a high viscosity
mechanical rigidity in the lube pump mechanism
a decrease in the fuel pump delivery pressure

text: Automatically fired auxiliary boilers use fuel oil strainer arrangements of either the simplex type or _____________.
The fuel oil strainers located in the fuel oil service  system of an automatic auxiliary heating boiler are either the duplex type or the _______________.
Proper filtering and straining of diesel fuel is important because the _____________.
options: fuel oil pump will overspeed if dirt is not removed
fuel oil transfer pumps cannot tolerate small amounts of grit in the oil
fuel injectors may be damaged by foreign particles in the fuel oil
dirty fuel will clog the intake air filter

text: If a diesel engine runs out of fuel, you can expect trouble from _____________.
options: fuel dilution of the lube oil

text: The cetane rating of diesel fuel is an indication of the ______________.
When cleaning a duplex strainer, it is important for _____________.
options: the pressure to be bled prior to opening the compartment cover
the lube oil to be allowed to cool before removing the  basket
the cleaning fluid used to be more viscous than the lube oil
a new cover gasket to be installed when reassembling the unit

text: The metal edge type filters used in diesel engine fuel oil and lube oil systems are normally cleaned in place by  _____________.
options: back flushing the system and draining the filter
opening the drain plug and blowing through the filter
manually operating a built-in scraper and draining the  filter
flushing with any approved solvent then draining the system

text: Which of the following conditions is NOT an indication of microbial contamination of the fuel supply? _____________. I. sludge from the fuel oil settling tanks and centrifuge II. water that has been collected in the settling tank.
Diesel engine injection lag is caused by _____________.
Piping from booster pumps to injection systems are to be at least ________.
Which of the following represents the significance the fuel oil cetane number?
options: The cetane number has no affect on injection lag.
The cetane number is an indication of the fuel's viscosity.
Ignition lag is reduced with fuels having a high cetane  number.
The cetane number is of little significance in the   combustion process.

text: Which of the following statements is true concerning the cetane number of diesel fuel?
options: The cetane number affects the amount of injection lag.
The cetane number is of little significance in the   combustion process.
Ignition lag is reduced with fuels having a high cetane  number.
The cetane number is an indication of the fuel's viscosity.

text: The cetane number rates fuels for diesel engines according to its _____________.
The ignition quality of a fuel oil is an important   operational consideration because it _____________.
options: indicates the amount of abrasive material in the fuel
affects the starting ability of a cold engine
determines the amount of fuel penetration
affects the compression ratio of an operating engine

text: Which chemical listed is utilized to prevent and correct most microbial infections occurring within fuel storage  systems?
Proper housekeeping to prevent the formation of    microbiological growths within a fuel system includes the prevention of water accumulations and the use of _______.
An increased pressure differential between the inlet and outlet of a strainer usually indicates the strainer is  _____________.
Small amounts of moisture are necessary to trigger the  growth of microbiological organisms found in some marine fuels. Some sources of water contamination are _________.
The major cause of problems occurring with fuel injection equipment is _____________.
options: incorrect replacement of barrels and plungers of jerk pumps

text: Air in the fuel lines to the fuel injection nozzles of a diesel engine will result in _____________.
Fuel oil day tanks for diesel engines must be checked and cleaned at regular intervals in order to remove ___________.
Clogged diesel engine fuel oil filters can cause __________,
Generating tubes in waste heat boilers are finned to ____________.
options: increase the rate of heat transfer

text: A failure to any component of a flame safeguard control for an automatically fired auxiliary boiler, will result in  ______________.
The flame safeguard control system of a large automatic  auxiliary boiler will provide fuel shut off in the case of high ____________.
Prior to lighting off a cold automatically fired auxiliary boiler, you should _____________.
Which of the following statements describes the results of excessive microbiological growths within a fuel system?
options: All excessive amounts of growth will cause the main engines of the vessel to stall due to the inability to supply the proper quantities of fuel to satisfy the existing load.
The deposits produced by these growths form blockages and flow restrictions ultimately leading to improper atomization of the fuel into the cylinders.
Eventually the growth of these organisms will deplete the supply of food available to them, which in turn will cause their demise.
If continual growth is permitted, a sweet odor similar to that associated with baking will be noticed when system  components are opened for inspection.

text: The microbiological growths that affect fuel supplies can easily be transported from one location to another by  _________.
The major cause of fuel pump and injection system problems is _____________.
Fuel oil is regularly transferred to the day tank in order to _____________.
options: allow impurities to settle out of the fuel
allow air to escape from the fuel
make fuel available for immediate use

text: It is desirable for an auxiliary boiler safety valve to pop open and reseat quickly to __________.
options: give warning that excessive boiler pressure has been reached
prevent wire drawing of the disc and seat

text: The amount of oil consumed by a return flow-type fuel  atomization system, fitted with both supply and return  meters, can be determined by the ____________.
options: sum of the supply and return meter readings
difference between the supply and return meter readings

text: If the fire goes out in an automatically fired auxiliary boiler and the burner continues to supply fuel, there is a potential danger of ______________.
When an  automatically fired boiler has a flameout, which of the following actions should occur FIRST?
options: close the air cock once fires are lit
crack the steam stop to assure protective steam flow

text: Which of the following actions takes place in the control circuit of an automatically fired auxiliary boiler when the desired steam pressure is obtained?
options: A temperature sensing device opens the circuit breaker in the burner motor.
The high limit control secures power to the entire oil  firing system.
The stack relay actuates the low limit control which breaks the ignition circuit.
The stack relay secures power to the high voltage side of the ignition transformer.

text: Which of the following conditions could cause the feed pump for an auxiliary boiler to lose suction?
options: Pump recirculating line being open too much

text: The purpose of a temperature sensing device installed in the stack of a small automatically fired auxiliary steam boiler is to secure the oil burner _______________.
options: in the event of a flame failure
in the event of a stack fire
when the water level reaches the crown sheet
when the feed pump discharge pressure drops to a preset minimum

text: Variations in the amount of fuel oil burned in a return flow type burner, are controlled by the ____________.
options: back pressure in the fuel oil return line

text: A burner responsible for producing black smoke in an  automatic auxiliary boiler, would be caused by a __________.
A pulsating flame, accompanied by a burner developing black smoke in an auxiliary boiler, is an indication that the _____________.
options: fuel oil pressure is too low

text: Failure of the feed pump to deliver feedwater to an   auxiliary boiler could be caused by _______________.
options: grounded probes in the water level control

text: The diesel engine exhaust gas bypass, as fitted with some waste heat boilers, is installed to ____________.
options: prevent engine back pressure at heavy loads
increase total engine efficiency at low loads
prevent boiler corrosion at low engine loads
improve engine fuel consumption at any load

text: A waste heat boiler is installed on some diesel propelled vessels to _____________.
options: The fuel oil pump should stop.

text: Which of the listed sequence of events occurs when an  automatic auxiliary boiler is prepurged?
options: The damper on the inlet side of the furnace is moved to the open position for a given number of seconds and then moved to the closed position.
The damper on the inlet side of the furnace is moved to the open position for a given number of seconds and then moved to the low fire position.
The damper is moved to the closed position for a given  number of seconds and then moved to the low fire position.
The damper in the uptakes is moved to the wide open position for a given number of seconds and then moved to the low  firing rate position.

text: Fins are installed on the generating tube surfaces in waste heat boilers to ____________.
options: prevent soot fires in the exhaust system
prevent exhaust gas erosion of the tubes
increase the velocity of exhaust gas flow
increase the rate of heat transfer

text: Subtracting the return flow meter reading from the supply flow meter reading on a boiler equipped with a return flow fuel oil system, determines the amount of oil ____________.
When there is a flame failure in an automatically fired  auxiliary boiler, the ____________.
Bottom blow valves are installed on auxiliary water-tube boilers to _____________.
options: remove suspended and precipitated solids from the boiler water
completely drain the boiler in an emergency situation
prevent hardened scale deposits in the water drum
remove floating impurities from the oiler water surface

text: The solenoid valves in the fuel oil supply line to an  automatically fired auxiliary boiler, are automatically  closed by ______________.
If the fuel/air ratio in an automatically fired auxiliary boiler is insufficient, the result could lead to __________.
High stack temperature occurring in an auxiliary boiler  could be a result of ____________.
The maximum pressure developed by a waste heat boiler is determined by the main engine exhaust ____________.
Bottom blow valves are installed on auxiliary water-tube boilers to _____________.
options: completely drain the boiler in an emergency
prevent sludge from forming in the steam drum
remove floating impurities from the boiler water surface
remove settled solids from the water drum

text: With which of the following types of diesel engine   arrangements is a waste heat boiler most likely to produce the maximum steam pressure, temperature, and flow   conditions?
Which of the following methods is typically employed in the design of waste heat boilers to obtain maximum heat   transfer, while maintaining low overall weight?
options: Feedwater is preheated in a separately fired economizer.
An external superheater unit is located above the boiler in the gas passages.
An unfired exhaust gas preheater is added to increase the heat transfer rate.
Steel fins are installed on the generating tube surfaces to increase the effective surface area.

text: A variable capacity, pressure atomizing, fuel oil burner functions to ____________.
options: provide a wide range of combustion
provide a constant fuel return pressure

text: In an auxiliary boiler steam and water system, the highest pressure will be in the ____________.
Large steam drums are not required in the design of a  coil-type auxiliary water-tube boiler because _____________.
options: steam and water are separated in the accumulator   (fclearance chamber)
the heat of combustion is sufficient to remove all moisture from the steam
the volume of steam is small at low pressures
automatic burner cycling controls steam volume and quality

text: Downcomers installed on auxiliary package boilers are  protected from direct contact with hot gases by ___________.
Why should the main steam stop valve of an auxiliary boiler be eased off its seat and then gently closed before lighting off?
options: To examine the valve stem for scars or nicks.
To check for a tight bonnet seal. gas temperature
To ensure that the valve will not be seized shut when hot. pressure
To check the valve packing. timing

text: When an auxiliary boiler is panting and emitting black  smoke, you should _____________.
options: decrease the fuel oil supply pressure

text: The purpose of the separating nozzle in the accumulator of a water- tube, coil-type, steam generator is to separate  _____________.
options: dry steam from the steam and water mixture

text: Within the cycle of a forced ciculation auxiliary water-tube boiler, part of the water flashes into steam, and the remaining hot water is ____________
options: collected in the lower portion of the steam accumulator  for recirculation back to the heating coil or water tank
returned to the lower drum via downcomers due to density difference for reheating
passes through the domestic heating system return line  steam traps to the auxiliary feed supply tank
automatically dumped into auxiliary feed heater and   reheated by auxiliary exhaust back pressure

text: The firing range of a variable capacity, return flow-type fuel atomizer is regulated to meet steam demand by varying the ____________.
Fins are installed on the fireside of the water-tubes used in waste heat boilers to ____________.
options: decrease the velocity of gases flowing past the tubes
increase the rate of heat transfer

text: A horizontal fire-tube package boiler, incorporating a  two-pass design, is basically constructed with ____________.
options: the rear of the boiler being used as a combustion chamber
extensive fireside baffling used to its fullest, enabling increased heat transfer rates
removable spiral metal gas retarders being inserted in the tubes to decrease the gas velocity to a minimum, thus  extracting the maximum caloric heat value

text: Waste heat boilers may be equipped with vents on the feed water heater heads to ____________.
A distorted furnace in a fire-tube auxiliary boiler may be the result of ____________.
options: firing for extended periods in the low fire mode provide a means of draining the boiler
overheating, due to waterside deposits warn the engineer of low water level
varying the water level above the crown sheet cool the crown sheet at high firing rates
carrying excessive alkalinity in the boiler water open the burners' electrical firing circuits

text: A feed pump for an auxiliary boiler might lose suction if the ____________.
After   undergoing extensive repairs, an auxiliary boiler, with a maximum allowable working pressure of 10 kg/cm2, should be hydrostatically tested at a pressure of   ______________.
The principal purpose of refractory and insulation   installed in the firebox of an auxiliary boiler is to  ____________.
options: prevent slag accumulation on the corbels
direct the force draft into the space between the inner and outer casings, to maintain a pressure seal
protect the inner casing and reduce heat loss
prevent flame impingement on the generating tube bank

text: Excessive vibration of an automatically fired auxiliary  boiler can be caused by _____________.
options: air or water in the furnace

text: An exhaust gas bypass is installed on a waste heat boiler in order to ____________.
options: recycle exhaust gas to the turbocharger
bypass a portion of the exhaust gas at peak loads for better efficiency
bypass exhaust gas at high loads to prevent excessive back pressure
minimize moisture condensation in the boiler gas passages at low loads

text: A properly adjusted safety valve for an auxiliary boiler will ____________.
options: attain maximum lift when it pops below its set pressure
open with a sharp, clear pop at its set pressure
close sharply when the pressure drops to its set pressure
operate most effectively when it has zero blowdown

text: Constant capacity, pressure atomizing, fuel burners designed to meet a wide variation in steaming loads on an auxiliary boiler, are _____________.
options: automatically supplied with warmer air on demand
automatically supplied with more fuel on demand
equipped with standard variable capacity atomizers
cycled on and off in response to steam demand

text: The gauge glass on a coil-type auxiliary boiler is connected to the ______________.

In comparison to straight flow mechanical atomizers, return flow atomizers provide relatively uniform atomization over a wide firing range due to the ____________. atomize when using a steam atomizer in an  auxiliary boiler?
options: Expansion of the steam in the furnace.
Expansion of the steam in the whirling chamber.
Expansion of the steam in the orifice plate.

text: The purpose of the programmed purge cycle on an    automatically fired auxiliary boiler is to _______________.
options: cool the furnace to prevent preignition
remove explosive vapours from the furnace
provide sufficient air in the furnace to allow ignition of the fuel

text: When preparing to light off a cold boiler equipped with a return flow fuel oil system, the recirculating valve directs the flow of oil ____________.
options: directly to the fuel oil heater inlet for further warm-up
back to the fuel oil settler for further filtration
back to the suction side of the service pump

text: The purpose of designing some waste heat boilers with  sinuous fire tubes, is to ____________.
options: increase exhaust gas velocity through the boiler
reduce accumulations of carbon deposits on the heat transfer surfaces
eliminate exhaust gas pulsations and noise
increase the rate of heat transfer to the waterside

text: The amount of fuel oil atomized by a return flow oil burner is directly controlled by the ____________.
In the event of a flame failure in an auxiliary water-tube boiler, you must ______________.
options: relight the boiler immediately to prevent loss of steam  pressure
relight the fire off the brickwork as long as the bricks are cherry red
purge the furnace of any combustible gases before attempting to relight the fire
speed up the feed pump to prevent dry firing when the burner flame is reestablished

text: An auxiliary boiler is equipped with a return flow fuel  atomization system, which uses a/an _____________.
The constant capacity, pressure atomizing, fuel oil burners designed to meet a wide variation in the steaming loads of an auxiliary boiler, are ________________.
options: automatically cycled on and off in response to demand
automatically supplied with more fuel on demand
equipped with standard variable capacity atomizers
equipped with fuel nozzles having variable orifices

text: Excessive vibration from an auxiliary boiler could be caused by ____________.
Which of the following statements concerning the operation of a coil- type forced circulation auxiliary water-tube  boiler is correct? at high firing rates
options: oil supply pressure not having to be reduced at low loads to the point where poor atomization occurs
return flow atomizer being designed for best combustion at low firing rates
rotational motion imparted by the tangential slots being greater in the return flow atomizer

text: Which of the following statements concerning fire-tube  boilers is correct?
options: Combustion gases flow through the tubes.

text: Which of the listed problems will happen when the water  level of a fire-tube type auxiliary boiler approaches the crown sheet?
Fins are installed on the fireside of the water-tubes, used in waste heat boilers, to ____________.
options: decrease the velocity of gases flowing past the tubes
reduce the accumulation of soot deposits on the tubes
increase the heat transfer surface area

text: When a waste heat boiler is installed in the exhaust of a main propulsion diesel engine, the exhaust gas bypass would be used ____________.
options: at high loads to prevent overheating
at low loads to prevent corrosion in the boiler
during periods of high steam demand

text: Which of the following items is necessary for all waste heat boiler installations, regardless of design or manufacturer?
options: A separate means to prevent feedwater contamination

text: When lighting off an auxiliary boiler, which of the   problems listed could cause the burners to sputter?
Which of the following statements describes how the fueloil enters the whirling chambers of the sprayer plates usedin a auxiliary boiler return flow fuel oil system?
options: Through the sprayer plate drilled passages.
Through tangential slots in the sprayer plate.
Through baffles in the orifice plate.

text: The PRIMARY function of a waste heat boiler is to _________.
options: recover heat which otherwise would be lost

text: A SECONDARY function of a waste heat boiler is to _________.
options: reduce engine back pressure Steam is generated in the heating coils and is force fed to an accumulator.
increase engine cycle efficiency Unevaporated boiler water collects in the bottom of the accumulator.
increase turbocharger efficiency Moisture is removed from generated steam in a radiant superheater.

text: Which of the following statements about a coil-type forced circulation auxiliary water-tube boiler is correct?
options: Steam is generated in the fclearance chamber.
Steam is recirculated to the heating coils.
Response to steam demand is comparatively rapid.
Unevaporated feedwater drains to the bilge.

text: Excessive return oil pressure from a variable capacity  return flow fuel oil burner system on an automatic auxiliary boiler, will cause ____________.
An auxiliary boiler equipped with a return flow fuel  atomization system, has a _____________.
The amount of oil atomized by the return flow variable  capacity atomizer, used with some automatically fired  boilers, is controlled by the _______________.
options: amount of air admitted to the atomizer
oil pressure in the oil return line
quantity of oil delivered by the service pump
proportioning device in the atomizer fuel valve

text: Throttling a burner air register on an auxiliary boiler  could result in ____________.
Diesel engine waste heat boiler construction is usually of the fire- tube or _____________.
The variation in the amount of fuel oil burned in the  operation of an auxiliary boiler, utilizing a return flow type atomization system, is a function of the _____________.
Auxiliary boilers can be classified as ________________.
In a coil-type forced circulation auxiliary water-tube  boiler _____________.
options: steam is recirculated through heating coils in the boiler relieve excess fuel oil pressure during the "off" fire cycle
hot water fclearancees to steam in the fclearance chamber admit water to the dry pipe
unevaporated feedwater is lost through the atmospheric vent throttle the forced draft fan output for proper combustion
response to steam demand is slower than in a fire-tube  boiler reduce excess steam pressure in the boiler

text: The safety valve installed on a coil-type auxiliary boiler is located on the ____________.
Auxiliary boilers are divided into several classifications, one of which is ______________.
In a coil-type forced circulation auxiliary water-tube  boiler, ____________.
options: steam demand response is comparatively rapid
steam is recirculated through heating coils in the boiler
unevaporated feedwater is discharged through the skim tube

text: The principal purpose of refractory and insulation   installed in the firebox of an auxiliary boiler is to  ____________.
options: prevent flame impingement on the generating tube bank
direct the force draft into the space between the inner and outer casings, to maintain a pressure seal
protect the inner casing and reduce heat loss
prevent slag accumulation on the corbels

text: Before any work is to be carried out on a burner in an  automatically fired auxiliary boiler, you should always  _____________.
options: block all control system relays closed
allow the boiler to cool completely
close all manually operated fuel valves
lock all safety interlock switches closed

text: How is the concentration of dissolved oxygen in the   feedwater of an auxiliary boiler maintained at acceptable limits?
options: Feedwater is cycled through a DC heater.
Oxygen is liberated in the three-stages of feedwater  preheating.
Oxygen is liberated by maintaining the highest practical feedwater temperature.

text: A burner producing black smoke in an automatic auxiliary boiler, would be caused by a/an ______________.
The water in an auxiliary boiler should be tested for  chloride content to determine ______________.
The procedures recommended for auxiliary boilers having high salinity include ____________. ____________.
Auxiliary boilers are divided into several classifications, one of which is _____________.
The tube sheets installed in a fire-tube auxiliary boiler are normally connected by ____________.
Which type of pump is typically used to supply fuel to a unit type auxiliary boiler?
In a coil-type auxiliary water-tube circulation boiler  _____________.
options: unevaporated feedwater collects in the bottom of the fclearance chamber
all generated steam is recirculated through heating coils in the boiler
heated water fclearancees to steam in the boiler heating coils
response to steam demand is slower than in a fire-tube  boiler

text: Which of the following procedures decreases the total  dissolved solids concentration in the water of an auxiliary boiler?
A sprayer plate used in a return flow fuel oil atomizer is correctly installed if the oil __________.
options: passes through the whirling chamber before passing through the tangential slots
passes through the tangential slots before passing through the whirling chamber
leaves the burner as a straight stream until mixed with the primary flow of combustion air
leaves the burner as a straight stream until mixed with the swirling atomizing steam

text: The water in an auxiliary boiler should be chemically tested daily for alkalinity and _________________.
For proper operation, auxiliary boiler feedwater must have which of the following characteristics?
The concentration of dissolved solids in the boiler water of an auxiliary boiler could increase as a result of  ___________.
options: securing the boiler and giving it a bottom blow

text: If a tube ruptures in a water-tube auxiliary boiler due to low water, you should ____________.
options: secure the fires and maintain feedwater to boiler to keep up the water level
not secure the fires until water level falls out of sight in the gauge glass
secure both the fires and the feed inlet valve
secure the fires when the pressure drops to 50% of the  maximum allowable working pressure

text: A coil-type automatically fired auxiliary boiler is to be laid up wet for an indefinite period. The boiler water  should be treated to ensure that ____________.
options: the manufacturer's recommended pH is maintained
there is no excess of oxygen scavenging chemicals
sludge formation cannot occur in the steam separator
waterside blowdown will not be required

text: If an operating auxiliary boiler has a water pH reading of 7, you should ______________.
options: treat the water with caustic soda
treat the water with chemical scavengers
reduce the water alkalinity to recommended readings

text: Heavy soot accumulations in an auxiliary boiler could be caused by _____________.
Failure of the burner flame in an automatic auxiliary boiler would probably be a result of ______________.
options: full fuel pressure at the nozzle

text: The water in an operating auxiliary boiler should be tested for alkalinity and chloride content each __________.
The most common cause of scale formation in an auxiliary boiler is ______________.
options: concentrations of calcium sulfate in the boiler water
improper treatment of the feedwater with calcium sulfate

text: Why should the main steam stop valve of an auxiliary boiler be eased off its seat and then gently closed before lighting off?
options: To examine the valve stem for scars or nicks.
To check for a tight bonnet seal.
To ensure that the valve will not be seized shut when hot.

text: Which of the following actions should normally be taken during each watch when the auxiliary boiler is in operation?
options: Lift the safety valves by hand
Inspect and clean burner oil solenoid valves

text: Flame failure in an operating automatically fired auxiliary boiler can result from a _____________.
When checking for the presence of sulphite in the feedwater of an auxiliary boiler, you are in essence checking   ____________.
options: to ensure the compound additions are adequate for   controlling dissolved oxygen
to ensure the compound additions are adequate for control of pH
the hardness of the makeup feed water
to ensure the automatic or manual blowdown rate and   frequency is adequate for control of total dissolved solids

text: A safety valve on an auxiliary boiler simmers constantly and can not be stopped by several quick blow-offs using the hand relieving gear. The problem may be __________.
The water in a steaming auxiliary boiler should be tested daily for ______________.
Which of the following actions should be taken when an  auxiliary boiler is operation?
options: Lift the relief valves by hand.
Inspect and clean all solenoid valves.
Inspect for oil and water leaks.

text: Improper maintenance of an automatic auxiliary boiler oil burner could result in ______________.
In a fire-tube auxiliary boiler, you should expect to find the thickest scale on the waterside of the ____________.
If the feed pump for an auxiliary boiler fails to deliver the feed water to the boiler, the cause may be ____________.
options: high steam pressure in the boiler
steam demand exceeding feed pump capacity

text: Which of the following actions should normally be taken  during each watch when the auxiliary boiler is operation?
options: Inspect and clean burner fuel oil solenoid valves
Lift the safety valves by hand

text: An automatically fired auxiliary boiler with carbon deposits formed on its burner electrodes, will experience _________.
Oxygen corrosion in auxiliary boilers is prevented by  treating the boiler feed tank with either sodium   ____________.
options: faulty steam pressure signal to the trial for ignition  circuit

text: A firebox explosion in an automatically fired auxiliary  boiler may be the result of ______________.
options: a faulty transformer in the ignition circuit

text: After lighting off a cold, automatically fired, auxiliary boiler, as steam begins to form, you should ___________.
options: give the boiler a bottom blow

text: If poor combustion occurs in an auxiliary boiler due to an air damper linkage being out of adjustment, you would adjust the linkage and then _____________.
options: test the high and low fire solenoids
check the photocell window for carbon deposits
check the burner ignition electrode gap

text: The pH value of water in a diesel engine closed cooling  water system should be maintained between _____________.
The boiler water alkalinity in a coil-type auxiliary boiler should be maintained at the pH recommended by the boiler manufacturer to _________________.
options: reduce corrosion in the heating coil
prevent clogging and erosion in the coil

text: The amount of chloride content in the water of an auxiliary boiler can be reduced by ____________.
In readying an auxiliary water-tube boiler for a routine hydrostatic test, which of the following procedures should be undertaken prior to filling the boiler with fresh water?
options: The safety valve escape piping should be disconnected from the valve body and a blank inserted.
The boiler vent valves should be opened.
All handhole/manhole covers should be tightened up as much as possible to preclude any leaks.

text: The concentration of total dissolved solids in the water of an auxiliary boiler can increase as a result of __________.
Before an auxiliary boiler is shutdown for an extended  period of time, the water in the boiler should have a pH value of ____________.
Why should handhole gaskets not be allowed to leak on an auxiliary boiler?
options: Water circulation in the boiler will be disrupted.
The gasket and its seating surface may become wire drawn.
The gasket material will become hardened.
Scale and sediment will form on the gasket.

text: During maintenance inspections of a fire tube auxiliary  boiler, you should check for _____________.
options: weaking of the tubes at the tube sheet

text: Before any work is to be carried out on a burner in an  automatically fired auxiliary boiler, you should always  _____________.
options: allow the boiler to cool completely
close all manually operated fuel valves
lock all safety interlock switches closed
block all control system relays closed

text: A dirty atomizer sprayer plate in the burner of an auxiliary boiler, would be indicated by ____________.
A bypass line provided around a waste heat auxiliary boiler in a diesel engine exhaust system, may be used to avoid  boiler ____________.
Which of the following actions should normally be taken  during each watch when the auxiliary boiler is in operation?
options: Clean the flame scanner photocell window.

text: If a large difference exists between the compressor   discharge pressure and the pressure corresponding to the existing condensing temperature in a refrigeration system, you should ____________.

adjust the high pressure cutout to maintain the desired  pressure
open the hot gas bypass temporarily to reduce capacity

text: Air entering an air-cooled refrigeration system is indicated by ____________.

options: vapor generator could be caused by _____________. a minor amount of air in the system
normal oil circulation with the refrigerant
the expansion valve being stuck in the open position

text: Crankcase sweating in a refrigeration system is caused by _____________.

text: When handling contaminated oil from a hermetically sealed refrigeration compressor unit that has burned out its motor, you should _____________.

options: formation of flash gas in the liquid line

can be quickly removed by simply shutting off fluid flow to the coils Has no affect on the system.

text: If you heard a "crackling" sound occuring within a   centrifugal pump, the most probable source of the noise  would be the ______________.

due to the inability of this type of pump to lift water to cover the suction (eye) of the impeller
to initially unload the pump by having its head pressure equal to discharge pressure
in order to overcome the potential energy of water in the discharge line

text: In a direct expansion type multi-box refrigeration system, the compressor is set up to cycle on and off by the   _____________.
The static suction lift of a pump is the difference in  elevation between the _____________.
options: centerline of the pump and the level of the liquid in the suction well when the source of liquid is below the pump
centerline of the pump and the suction liquid level when the source of liquid is above the pump
centerline of the pump and the level of the discharge liquid
liquid levels of the suction and discharge

text: Which of the listed devices is used to control the admission of steam to the steam cylinder of a reciprocating pump?

options: A power end and fluid end

text: If a centrifugal pump vibrates and is noisy when operating, the cause could be _____________.
Excessive lost motion in the valve mechanism of a duplex reciprocating pump will cause the _____________.
options: pump to short stroke continuously at both ends of the stroke for both cylinders

text: The seal piping obtains liquid from the discharge side of the pump and directs the liquid to the _____________.
When securing a steam reciprocating pump, which of the  valves listed should remain open?
