options: Prior to each fuel discharge operation.

text: You suspect a problem with the centrifugal fire pump on your ship, and begin to slowly close the discharge valve. Your suspicion is confirmed when the ______________.
options: discharge valve is closed off and there is no change in the discharge pressure
discharge valve is 80% closed and the relief valve lifts
suction valve is closed off, and the discharge pressure  begins to decrease
suction valve is closed off, yet the relief valve does not lift

text: Which of the following statements represents the advantage of rotary pumps as compared to reciprocating pumps?
options: The high discharge pressure of the rotary pumps permits a larger volume of fluid per unit time than the reciprocating pump.
Rotary pumps are capable of pumping more fluid than reciprocating pumps of the same weight.
Rotary pumps occupy only one-half the space of    reciprocating pumps.
Rotary pumps eliminate discharge slippage of the pumped  liquid, while this does not hold true for a reciprocating pump.

text: A distinguishing feature of an eductor, when compared to other pumps, is the ______________.
options: discharge end being smaller than the suction end
ease at which the wearing rings may be changed

text: Lantern rings are provided on centrifugal pumps to ________.
options: allow illuminated inspection of shaft and packing
provide a passage for the stuffing box sealing liquid
adjust leakage at the shaft gland
allow for distortion of the wearing rings

text: Which of the following terms is used to identify the  pressure of the liquid entering a pump?
One of the main functions of wearing rings, as used in a centrifugal pumps, is to _____________.
options: maintain radial alignment between the pump impeller and  casing
absorb all impeller shaft end thrust
prevent water leakage to the atmosphere
prevent wear of the pump casing and/or impeller

text: Which of the pumping systems listed for use aboard ship will most likely use a propeller type pump?
On many modern tankers, which of the following devices is used to reduce cargo pump leakage to the pump room bilge?
Which of the following intervals do Regulations require the remote cutout for the fuel oil service pumps to be tested?
options: At each fire and boat drill

text: If a centrifugal pump is driven by a constant speed prime mover and produces a flow rate less than the designed  specifications, the cause could be _____________.
options: suction absolute pressure greatly exceeds corresponding  vapor pressure of liquid being pumped 45° apart

text: What would be the result of throttling the suction valve tothe point where the flow was less than that recommended by a centrifugal pump manufacture?  I. The discharge head would be reduced.  II. The pump would begin to cavitate.

Mechanical shaft seals used on water service pumps require lubrication of the seal faces to minimize deposits of  foreign matter on those surfaces. Which of the following pressures and lubricants are required?
A liquid is being transferred by a centrifugal pump. As the liquid passes through the volute, its velocity decreases and ______________.
Which of the following statements is true regarding a  positive displacement rotary pump?
options: Theoretically, there is no limit to shut off head pressure.
The discharge side should be protected against excessive pressure build up via a relief valve.

text: If a centrifugal pump develops insufficient discharge  pressure as a result of worn wearing rings, which of the following corrective actions is required?
options: Throttle in on the discharge valve.
Always replace the wearing rings and the impeller as a set.
Throttle in on the suction valve.

text: When adding packing to a seal involving motion, you must be careful to prevent ____________.
options: deterioration of the packing due to excessive tightening

text: What would be the result of throttling the suction valve tothe point where the flow was less than that recommended by a centrifugal pump manufacturer?  I. The designed discharge head would be reduced.  II. The packing life would be greatly reduced.
A double suction centrifugal pump impeller has been   installed in the reverse direction, and will ______________.
options: have a greater head capacity I only
operate with increased impeller efficiency II only
discharge through the suction side of the pump casing Both I and II
require more power to turn the shaft Neither I nor II

text: What would be the probable cause if a pump were unable tomaintain necessary discharge pressure to a system?  I. failure of the internal parts of the pump  II. failure of the system relief valve
Reduced capacity, accompanied by vibration and noise at the suction of a centrifugal pump, results from cavitation in the fluid being pumped. Cavitation describes the formation of _____________.

loosened, and then retightened until they are only finger tight

text: A centrifugal pump gradually develops insufficient discharge pressure. What corrective action is required?
options: Throttle in on the discharge valve.
Replace the wearing rings, shaft, and impeller.
Throttle in on the suction valve.

text: If a centrifugal pump sealing line becomes restricted while operating under a negative suction head (suction lift), the inner end of the stuffing box will be ___________.

under pressure and fluid will tend to leak into the pump
sealed by the seal cage or pump lantern ring

text: Heavy wear in a particular area of the inner circumference of a packing ring may be caused by ____________.

The shaft sleeves have recently been replaced on a   centrifugal salt water service pump. The packing gland is now drawn tightly up against the casing. Despite the use of standard procedures to tighten the packing gland, water  continues to pour out along
options: the sleeves "O" ring seals were not installed
suction head pressure has become excessive
the sealing water flow to the stuffing box is blocked
only two sections of the lantern ring were installed

text: If a centrifugal pump were continually operated with the discharge valve closed, the _____________.
options: motor controller overload would open capacity will increase
pump would eventually overheat capacity will decrease
relief valve would continuously cycle open suction pressure will increase
motor would overheat suction pressure will decrease

text: Pitting in the suction areas of a centrifugal pump bronze impeller is usually caused by ____________.

One of the many problems occurring with centrifugal pump shaft packing is the excessive wear on the rings nearest the packing gland. If the rings nearest the impeller remain in good condition during the same operating period, the wear is most likely cause
options: insufficient lubrication of the lantern ring
packing rings rotating in the stuffing box
overtightening the packing in one adjustment
some rings being cut too short

text: If it were necessary for you to frequently replace the  packing on the main condensate pump, the trouble could be the result of ______________.
options: running at low speed for a long time

text: A twin-screw, rotary, positive displacement pump is provided with timing gears. If it were possible to change the lead (pitch) of the screws, which of the following statements would represent the true effect on the pump characteristics?
options: The longer the lead, the lower the flow rate.
The shorter the lead, the higher the pressure potential.
The longer the lead, the lower the axial velocity.
The shorter the lead, the higher the inlet pressure   requirements.

text: The discharge rate or capacity of a centrifugal pump will vary directly as the ____________.
options: impeller efficiency for large changes in its size

text: Theoretically, a double suction impeller is in hydraulic axial balance. In actuality this balance is rarely achieved due to _________________.
options: an unbalanced force exerted from the direction of the  impeller nearest the driving motor
unequal or nonuniform flow to the suction eyes of the  impeller housing
excessive sealing water flow to the stuffing box
flexible shaft design which causes rapid wear of the  outboard shaft bearing

text: To operate a centrifugal fire pump at reduced capacity, you should ___________________.

The centrifugal pump component responsible for converting the mechanical energy of the liquid being pumped, to that of kinetic energy is the ____________. condensate pump, the trouble could be the result of ___________.
options: salt water contamination of the condensate

text: Charring or glazing of the inner circumference of the  packing rings in a centrifugal pump, is caused by _________.
options: failure to seat the packing rings

text: When new, a rotary pump is designed to have 0.005 mm clearance between the rotors and the pump casing in order to develop a 180 LPM flow rate at 21bar. After one year of operation, the clearance has increased to 0.020mm, with the discharge pressure
options: less than 180 LPM because of increased clearance
180 LPM because the pressure is unchanged
greater than 180 LPM because of reduced slippage
greater than 180 LPM because of increased efficiency

text: When the danger of freezing exists, all steam driven  reciprocating pumps and deck equipment should be __________.
options: cycled at least once a day

text: Which of the following valve arrangements permits the reciprocating pump liquid piston to take suction from the suction chamber, and simultaneously discharge through the discharge chamber during the up and down strokes?
options: Two sets of suction and discharge valves in the liquid  cylinder.
Two sets of valves, one set each for suction and discharge located in the water chest.
One set of valves in the water chest and another set in the liquid cylinder.
Two relief valves, one on each of the liquid cylinder.

text: The discharge head of a centrifugal pump will vary directly with the ______________.
options: impeller efficiency for large changes in the size of the impeller

text: Centrifugal pumps, used to handle hot liquids, must have a minimum flow through them under all operating conditions. This flow serves to ____________.
options: prevent overheating and damage to pump rotating parts due to rubbing
maintain the net positive suction head of the pump

purge the pump of noncondensable vapors and gases

text: Sealing lines provide sealing liquid flow to the stuffing box of a centrifugal pump ____________.
options: to help lubricate the shaft packing
to limit the outflow of seawater from the stuffing box of a pump provided with a high suction head

text: The total static head of a system resisting the operation of a centrifugal pump is the difference in elevation between the __________________.
When repacking a stuffing box fitted with a lantern ring, which of the following precautions should be observed?
options: Replace all of the packing rings.
Block off the sealing water connection.
Be certain that all packing is fitted loosely to the shaft.
For good lubrication, apply a fine grade lapping compound between the shaft and packing.

text: Most pump manufacturers recommended that the discharge  piping for centrifugal pumps be ____________.

the same size as the pump discharge nozzle
one size smaller than the pump discharge nozzle
installed with a short radius elbow at the pump

text: When installing a new rotary pump, the suction piping should ___________________.

be at least one size larger than the pump suction connection

air binding in the discharge ports

text: Permanent centrifugal pump shaft damage due to erosion, corrosion, and wear at the stuffing box is usually prevented by ________________.

options: mechanical seals discharge liquid level and the pump centerline

do nothing, as this type of pump utilizes O-rings in lieu of packing
open the steam chest and pry the packing loose with a  scraper

double the liquid velocity through the pump when compared to a single volute

machine the worn wearing ring and refit

text: Which of the following conditions would prevent a steam reciprocating pump from delivering its rated capacity?

text: If a centrifugal pump operating with a positive suction head vibrates, or becomes noisy, the cause could be ____________.

options: Vapor pockets formed in the suction flow stream.
Heavy fluid in the flow stream.

text: Water leaking from a pump packing gland is kept away from the bearing housing by the use of ______________.

options: seal air from entering along the shaft
distribute the sealing liquid within the stuffing box

cube of the diameter of the impeller
impeller efficiency for large changes in the size of the impeller

Isolate the discharge side from the suction side.

text: The primary function of a centrifugal pump volute is to _____________.

ensure that the lantern ring is aligned with the sealing supply opening

text: Which of the listed pumps is NOT a positive displacement pump?

options: stem horizontal or pointing downward below the pipe

text: If you heard a "crackling" noise in a centrifugal pump, the most probable cause of the problem would be _____________.

decrease to where the pump no longer develops flow

change the pump to a "close coupled" arrangement

text: Excessive air leakage into the suction side of a   centrifugal pump would be indicated by which of the   following operational problems?

The pump packing gland overheats after short operating  periods.
The motor overcurrent protective device continually shuts the pump down.

low tolerance for entrained air and gases
low range of flows and pressures

options: They should always be started with the discharge valve  closed.

text: One of the many troubles occurring with centrifugal pump shaft packing is the excessive wear on the rings nearest the packing gland, while the rings nearest the impeller remain in good condition. This wear may be caused by ____________.
options: air entrained in the fluid pumped
packing rings rotating in the stuffing box
some packing rings being cut too short

along the discharge side of the housing

text: Which of the following conditions would be hazardous if you were using two centrifugal pumps to discharge a flammable liquid?

Each pump operating at a different speed and discharging into a common line.
Both pumps operating at the same speed taking suction  from a common line.
Each pump operating at a different speed and taking   suction from a common line.

text: Increasing the speed of a centrifugal pump will result in an increase in its capacity. Another means of increasing the capacity of a centrifugal pump is to increase the _________.
options: diameter of the discharge piping, with all other factors remaining the same
diameter of the suction piping, with all other factors  remaining the same

text: A centrifugal pump, normally operated under a positive  suction head, has its impeller replaced by one with a larger suction eye. What effect will this change have on the pump?
options: The pump motor will electrically trip off the line.

The pump will deliver less gallons per minute at the same RPM.

text: A centrifugal pump operating against a closed discharge  valve has a/an ______________.

options: A priming pump is always required to flood the impeller  suction.
They should always be started with the sealing line valves closed.

text: Reduced capacity, accompanied by vibration and noise at the suction of a centrifugal pump is a result of ____________.
The function of seal cages, or lantern rings installed in the centrifugal pump stuffing boxes, is to ______________.
options: seal air from entering along the shaft
distribute the sealing liquid within the stuffing box

text: If a centrifugal pump is being driven by a steam turbine and the discharge valve is closed off, the pump will __________.

options: only need to start a second pump and operate it in parallel
start a second pump and place it on line, close the discharge valve on the original pump and watch for a rise in the discharge pressure

text: A centrifugal pump produces flow with a resulting discharge head by energy conversion. It is typical for the energy conversion to follow the order of ______________.

mechanical energy to potential energy to kinetic energy
potential energy to mechanical energy to kinetic energy
kinetic energy to mechanical energy to potential energy

text: The simplest method to use for determining if a centrifugal pump is operating as designed, is to ____________.
options: closely observe the pump discharge temperature
close off the discharge valve, and watch for a rise in  pressure
momentarily close off the suction valve, and watch for a rise in pressure
use a clamp on ammeter and compare the readings to past records

text: If two centrifugal pumps, driven by two independent electric motors, operating at unequal speeds are discharging an  inflammable liquid through a common discharge line, the  higher speed pump may cause the slower pump to ___________.

When operating with a negative suction head, which of the following types of pumps will require priming?

options: suction head is high, and is handling a cool, debris free liquid

options: takes suction from a low temperature pressurized tank

text: Two individual centrifugal pump impellers of the same  diameter, width, and rotating speed are to be compared, one is cast with straight vanes, the other with curved vanes. Which of the following statements is correct?

The straight vane impeller has the potential for producing a greater discharge pressure.

text: Which of the following statements about pump operations is NOT a correct statement?
options: In order for a fluid to be pumped, a fluid must be made to flow.
A substance must be cold in order for it to be pumped.
The kinetic energy imparted by the pump can be used to force a liquid through a hydraulic system.
A pump must receive its energy from an external source.

text: Which pump installation would require an external source of sealing water?
options: Main circulator pump jacket water cooling

text: When replacing packing rings in the stuffing boxes of reciprocating pump piston rods, the ends of the rings should  be cut _________________.

options: Check for parallel alignment of the sleeve radial face to the sleeve bore.

text: A pump shaft that is bent or distorted should normally be ____________.

text: Which of the units of measurement listed below are used to express positive and negative suction heads when calculating pump head or hydraulic horsepower?
options: Inches of mercury for positive head and feet of water for negative head
Feet of water for positive head and inches of mercury for negative head
Inches of mercury for both positive and negative heads
Feet of water for both positive and negative heads

text: The static suction head of a pump is the _____________.

distance the suction liquid level is below the center line of the pump

text: To maintain the design discharge pressure of a centrifugal pump, the clearance between the ____________.

text: Which of the following problems will occur when the suction valve is throttled on a centrifugal pump not designed to operate in this manner?

text: Which of the following statements represents the main  difference between a stuffing box gland and a mechanical seal for sealing the shaft of a centrifugal pump?

a single stepped impeller mounted in a progressively staged casing
a large radial clearance between the impeller and casing to prevent overheating due to friction
an increase in the discharge velocity of the liquid with a corresponding decrease in pressure through the stages

text: An increase in rotor clearances in a rotary pump will _________________.
Positive displacement, helical gear pumps are well suited for pumping oil because _____________.
options: stuffing boxes eliminate the leakage problems usually  associated with other gear pumps
it is not necessary to closely maintain design clearances with this pump
they are essentially self-priming and produce a high suction lift
these pumps are designed with extreme tooth angles

text: How does the viscosity of a fluid being pumped affect the operation of a spur gear pump?
options: The viscosity of the fluid is relatively unimportant in the pump operation.
The pump can only be used for fluids of high viscosity.
The pump can only be used for light oils.
The pump cannot handle fluids more viscous than lubricating oil.

text: A spur gear pump should be operated with the discharge  valves _____________.

options: stuffing boxes eliminate the leakage problems usually  associated with other gear pumps
helical gear pumps are essentially self-priming and produce high suction lift
closely maintained design clearances in this pump is not necessary
helical gear pumps are designed with extreme tooth angles

text: A centrifugal pump with a double volute casing operated at greater than design capacity, will ________.

text: In most horizontally split centrifugal pump casings, wearing rings are prevented from rotating by _____________.

text: When a centrifugal pump is operating with a positive suction head, the inner end of the stuffing box is _____________.
options: under a vacuum and air tends to leak into the pump through the shaft stuffing box
under a pressure and fluid tends to leak from the pump  along the shaft and through the stuffing box

sealed by the seal cage or pump lantern ring

text: Pump efficiency may be lost as a result of _______________.

a leak in a gasket on the suction side of the pump

text: Which of the following actions should be taken if during a routine maintenance inspection of a centrifugal pump,  localized scoring on a pump shaft sleeve is detected?

text: Why is the discharge pressure of a herringbone gear pump steadier than the discharge pressure of a simple spur gear pump?
options: Clearance between the gear teeth and casing is smaller.

The herringbone gear pump has four spur gears instead of two.

text: Lead wrapped flexible packing, rated for temperatures of up to 232°C and shaft speeds up to 1000 RPM, will not be  suitable for use with _________________.
