options: loss of moisture in the air charge in the receiver
frequent lifting of the intercooler relief valve using one dowel pin pressed into the side of the ring, then fitting the pin into a hole drilled into the side of the casing ring grove

an abnormally low intercooler pressure having the casing "crush" them in place using an extended lip formed on one half of the ring and using the upper casing half to trap the lip when the casing is reassembled

text: Which of the following statements is correct with regards to the operation of a centrifugal cargo pump?
options: Oil is discharged from the center of the impeller through the outlet.
Gravity causes the oil to flow toward the discharge.
The self-priming feature of the centrifugal pump enables it to draw its own suction as it starts.
The discharge capacity varies directly with the speed of the impeller.

text: Centrifugal pumps are designed with sealing lines _________.
options: to prevent the fluid being pumped from pouring out of the stuffing box when a high suction head is available
to prevent air from entering the pump casing along the shaft

text: To prevent overheating and scoring of the shaft after  repacking the stuffing box, which of the following   procedures should be carried out?
options: Tighten the packing in small increments while the pump is operating.
Lubricate the packing with cylinder oil before installing new turns of packing.
Tightening the gland in all the way and then backing it off slightly.
Operating the pump slowly and applying oil freely to the shaft until the packing is properly seated.

text: Which of the following statements is true regarding   mechanical seals?
options: They may be used in lieu of conventional packing glands for any service other than saltwater.
They are not suitable for use on fuel oil transfer pumps.
They are normally lubricated and cooled by the fluid being pumped.
Once placed into service, leakage between the dynamic seal surfaces may be reduced by monthly adjustment of the spring compression.

text: Which of the listed parts is used in a lobe-type rotary pump to reduce the wear of the lobe edges?
Increasing the moisture content of conditioned air is known as _____________.
A viscous film of oil collected between the valve face and seat of a low pressure reciprocating air compressor will ______________.
options: prevent the valve from wire drawing
retard the opening and closing of the valve
have no effect on compressor operation

text: The purpose of an air compressor unloading device is to _____________.
options: drain water from the air receiver
delay the compression process until the motor is up to speed

text: Air blowing from the intake air filter of an operating air compressor indicates ___________.
options: pulsations in the air distribution system
overloading of the air distribution system

text: In a low pressure air compressor, the loss of volumetric efficiency normally results from _______________.
options: heating of the air leaving the cylinders
constant enlargement of the clearance expansion volume

text: In order to distribute the side pressures over a wide area of the cylinder walls and liners, which of the listed types of pistons are used in modern low pressure air compressors?
Which of the following problems would be the probable cause for the faulty operation of a reciprocating air compressor suction valve?
options: Carbon build up in the piston ring belt.
Faulty operation of a cylinder unloader.
Compressor operation in an area of high relative humidity.

text: If an electric motor driven air compressor fails to start, it may be due to a _______________.
If the drive belts on an air compressor were squealing, you should fix them by ____________.
The piston displacement rate of a reciprocating air   compressor can be modified by changing the _____________.
Intake valves installed on most reciprocating low pressure air compressors are actuated by the pressure differential between the air in the cylinder and the pressure __________.
options: of the air on the external side of the valve
of the push rod and rocker arm assembly
sensed by the receiver low pressure limit switch
sensed by the intercooler diaphragm valve

text: A knocking sound emitted from an unloaded air compressor is probably caused by _____________.
Over pressurization of an air compressor intercooler is  prevented by the _____________.
One method of unloading a low pressure air compressor at start-up is accomplished by ____________.
options: holding the L.P. discharge valve open
the use of a precharged accumulator
using a temporarily opened cylinder clearance pocket
temporarily discharging to the air receiver

text: To obtain maximum efficiency, two stage air compressors are usually ____________.
options: operated in an unloaded condition at all times

text: Which of the listed operating conditions would have the  greatest effect on the volumetric efficiency of an operating reciprocating air compressor?
options: The density of the air entering the compressor.
The temperature of the air entering the compressor
The designed rotating speed of the compressor.

text: Which of the following problems can result in below normal pressure in the intercooler of an operating low pressure air compressor?
options: Leaking intake valves on the high pressure cylinder
Leaking discharge valves on the low pressure cylinder

text: Excessive lube oil consumption in a reciprocating air  compressor is an indication of _____________.
options: defects in the high pressure unloader

text: On small, low pressure, air compressors, the cylinders are usually lubricated by the _____________.
options: internal cooling passages in the crankshafts and connecting rods

text: Air compressor receivers should be "blown down" at least _____________.
When the compressed air reservoir is placed in line with an air compressor and is used as an aftercooler, the reservoir must be ___________.
options: fitted with a moisture trap at the inlet

text: The cylinders and intercoolers of most low pressure air compressors are cooled by _____________.
An air compressor is equipped with an intercooler and an aftercooler to _____________.
options: inject water vapor into the compressed air
prevent overheating of first stage valves
reduce the compressed air charge density

text: If the capacity of a reciprocating air compressor gradually drops off, the cause could be ____________.
Reciprocating air compressor bearing failure may result from _________________.
Which of the following frequent maintenance procedures is required of compressed air receivers?
options: A close watch on the temperature.
Constant cleaning to remove oil and dirt.
Constant testing of the relief valves.

text: An air compressor is equipped with an intercooler to _____________.          I. increase the efficiency of the compressor II. reduce the cylinder operating temperature

According to Regulations , all pressure vessels other than unfired steam boilers shall be protected by pressure-relieving devices that prevent the pressure from rising more than __________ above the maximum allowable working pressure.
One of the main differences between a propeller pump and a centrifugal pump is the absence of a ______________.
options: volute in the centrifugal type pump
volute in the propeller type pump
velocity nozzle in the centrifugal type pump
reciprocating piston in the centrifugal type pump

text: Why should a person performing maintenance on an air compressor wire and tag the system valves closed?
options: To protect the operator performing the maintenance.
Each of the above is correct.

text: Intercooling of a multistage air compressor provides the advantages of reducing the work of compression on the succeeding stages, and ____________.
options: condensing part of the original water vapor content

text: The safety heads of most large reciprocating compressors are held in place by _____________.
options: discharge pressure in the safety return line

text: A squealing sound occurring from within an operating  reciprocating air compressor is an indication of __________.
If there is a sudden drop in the capacity of a reciprocating air compressor, you should check for ____________.
options: worn piston rings or cylinder liners
a defective pressure switch or pilot valve

text: Operating a reciprocating air compressor without an air  filter will cause ____________.
options: carbon deposits on valves and pistons
serious wear on valves and pistons

text: The heat of compression is partially removed from compressed air by ____________.
If a reciprocating air compressor has a knock occurring in frequency with its operating RPM, the cause is probably  ____________.

A compressor operating with an accumulation of dust andgrease on the surfaces of an intercooler would result in_________.  I. a decrease in volumetric efficiency of the   compressor  II. higher volume of air to be discharged to the   receiver
If the intercooler of a low pressure air compressor becomes fouled either internally or externally, the _______________.
options: normal running time will be decreased

text: If the foundation bolts of a reciprocating air compressor are loose, which of the conditions below will occur?
When one belt of a multiple V-belt compressor drive requires replacement, all of the belts must be replaced, because _____________.
options: the old belts would otherwise carry all the load
new belts are shorter than old belts
new belts have a tendency to slip

text: A squealing sound generated by an operating reciprocating air compressor would indicate _________________.
options: loose drive belts Defective pressure switch
badly leaking valves Leaking suction unloader
compressor interstage leakage Compressor starting against full load
tight drive belts Compressor starting without any load

text: If a ship service air compressor failed to unload, the ________________.
options: air receiver pressure would be excessively low
circuit breaker may open on starting

text: Leaking valves in a low pressure, reciprocating, air  compressor can result from _______________.
options: operating the compressor at excessive speed
uneven piston stroke in the compressor

text: Excessive lube oil consumption by a reciprocating air  compressor can be caused by ____________.
options: using a slightly dirty air filter
using oil having an excessive viscosity
carrying the oil level higher than normal

text: After disassembly, the safest way to remove carbon deposits from air compressor inlet and discharge valves is to use _____________.
A dirty intercooler on the ship service air compressor will result in ________________.
A dirty intercooler on an air compressor will cause _______.
options: damage to the unloader operating diaphragm
an increase in current flow to the motor
high pressure in the receiver and refrig-a-filter
an excessive consumption of crankcase oil

text: If an air compressor is used to supply compressed air to outlets throughout the engine room and on deck of a vessel, the system is known as the _______________.
When one belt of a multiple V-belt drive requires replacing, it will be necessary to _____________.
options: ensure the proper belt dressing is applied
ensure the seasoned belts are reinstalled in their proper sequence
season the new belt prior to installation

text: Which of the following statements describes the function of an air compressor intake filter?
options: Protects against suction valve float. bypassing the discharge to suction
Provides a positive pressure on the air inlet valves. cylinders can be unloaded during starting and stopping by _____________. fitting depressors which hold the suction valve plates on their seats
step unloading the cylinders in a multi-cylinder machine

text: Aftercoolers are used with air compressors to _____________.
options: reduce the temperature of compressed air
decrease the density of compressed air
dampen pressure pulses in the discharge air
ensure complete expansion of the compressed air

text: If a ship service air compressor operating in intermittent service were failing to unload, the ____________.
options: compressor would stop abruptly when power was secured
air receiver indicated pressure would be excessively low
compressor would require more than the normal amount of lubrication

text: Which of the following problems could cause air to blow out through the inlet air filter of a running compressor?
If an air compressor is used to supply air primarily to the combustion control system and other pneumatic controllers, the entire system is known as the __________.
Slipping drive belts on a ship service air compressor is a probable symptom of ___________________.
Air line lubricators are used in compressed air systems to lubricate ______________.
options: tools and equipment served by compressed air

text: Which of the following statements represents the path of air flow passing through a typical two stage, low pressure,  reciprocating, air compressor?
options: Intercooler, L.P. cylinder, H.P. cylinder, and air cleaner
L.P. cylinder, air cleaner, intercooler, and H.P. cylinder
Air cleaner, L.P. cylinder, H.P. cylinder, and intercooler
Air cleaner, L.P. cylinder, intercooler, and H.P. cylinder

text: A knocking sound from one cylinder of an operating air  compressor indicates ____________.
options: a defective or broken high pressure unloader

text: An important point of consideration when replacing a dry type intake filter on an air compressor is to ____________.
options: use the same wetting oil on the element as is used in the compressor lubrication system
install a smaller size filter to allow for expansion  of the element
Protects against the damaging effects of airborne solid  particles.

text: In addition to a pressure gage and a relief valve, an air receiver should be fitted with a _____________.

If you have a duplex double acting reciprocating pump making 160 strokes/minute, with a 6" diameter cylinder, a 9" stroke and operating with 85% volumetric efficiency, what is the capacity of this pump?

A compressor operating with an accumulation of dust andgrease on the surfaces of an intercooler would result in_________.  I. a high consumption of lube oil  II. higher than normal air pressure in the receiver
A V-belt that is too tight will cause excessive wear of both the belt and the _____________.
For any given volume of compressed air produced by a  multistage air compressor, interstage cooling will ________.
options: increase the power required for compression
decrease the power required for compression
allow isothermal compression of the air charge
allow the compressed air volume to remain constant

text: If the intercooler relief valve lifts while an air   compressor is operating under load, you should check for ___________________.
options: a defective pressure switch or pilot valve
a leak in the intercooler piping
leakage through the low pressure unloader control diaphragm

text: Leaking valves in an air compressor can be a result of _______________.
The pressure developed in the high pressure cylinder of a reciprocating air compressor, in order to assure an output near the end of its compression stroke, is ____________.
options: the same as the line discharge pressure

text: The air charge leaving an intercooler, or aftercooler of an air compressor can be expected to be ___________.
options: select the proper size filter so that air flow is not  restricted

text: A reciprocating air compressor is running roughly and  vibrating excessively, indicating that the ____________.

A compressor operating with an accumulation of dust andgrease on the surfaces of an intercooler would result in_________.  I. the motor to run warmer than usual  II. lower density air discharged to the receiver
The unloading of an air compressor may be provided by_________.  I. holding the intake valve off of its seat  II. temporarily relieving the the intercooler to the   atmosphere
Operating a reciprocating air compressor without an air  intake filter can result in a/an ___________.
options: deposit of carbon on the valves

text: If a ship service air compressor failed to unload the ___________________.
options: air receiver pressure would be excessively low

text: A leaking suction valve in the second stage of a two stage, high pressure, air compressor can cause excessively high ________________.
Condensate must be drained periodically from the air  compressor receivers to prevent _____________.
The volumetric efficiency of a reciprocating air compressor, is the ratio of the _________________.
options: adiabatic work of compression to the indicated horsepower
work of isothermal compression to the brake horsepower of the motor
air indicated horsepower from indicator cards to the brake horsepower input
actual amount of air discharged by the compressor, to the theoretical volume swept by the movement of the compressor piston

text: The unloading system on an air compressor will ____________.
options: increase compressor discharge pressure on demand
increase compressor operating speed as necessary

text: Air compressor cylinder unloaders enable the compressor to _____________.
options: vary their speed according to temperature and load
start and come up to speed before air compression begins
change speed according to overload demands

text: If one drive belt on an air compressor is found to be  worn you should _____________.
Carbon deposits forming on the discharge valves of an air compressor are caused by oil deterioration under high  pressure. The first step in reducing these deposits would be to _____________.
Unloading of a low pressure, reciprocating, air compressor at start- up can be accomplished by ______________.
options: holding the L.P. discharge valve open
the use of a precharged accumulator
using a permanently enlarged clearance expansion volume
temporarily discharging back to the compressor intake

text: On a medium pressure, differential piston, reciprocating air compressor, lubrication of the pistons/cylinders is usually accomplished _____________.
options: lube oil flow from the bearing lube oil system

text: Which of the listed valve types is typically used as inlet and discharge valves on modern low pressure air compressors?
According to Regulations , a welded  repair to be made to a ships service air receiver, other than an emergency repair at sea, must be ____________.
options: inspected and tested by the chief engineer before it may be returned to service
completed and then tested at least twice prior to notifying the Administration to ask for a surveyor
made with a backing strip to ensure full penetration
made only with the prior approval of the Administration

text: One function of the air receiver in a compressed air system is to _____________.
options: dry the air discharged from the intercooler
receive exhaust air from pneumatic accessories
remove all traces of oil from the air

text: An unloader is installed on an air compressor to __________.
options: bypass the high pressure stage to the low pressure stage on 100% of the air compressors in service
reduce the compressor frictional load when starting

text: Regulations specifically prohibit seats or disks in pressure vessel relief valves to be made of  ________________.
If the electric motor driving an air compressor fails to start, the cause may be a _____________.
Air compressors are equipped with unloading systems for the purpose of _____________.
options: removing all but the frictional load during starting
relieving intercoolers of high temperature buildups
providing high starting torque in the drive motor
seating valve plates during compressor shutdown

text: Excessively low air pressure occurring in the intercooler of a reciprocating air compressor is caused by _______________.
options: leaky discharge valves on the LP cylinder
leaky discharge valves on the HP cylinder

text: Broken valve strips in an operating low pressure, air  compressor will cause ____________.
options: oil contamination in the compressed air
no immediate loss of the compressed air capacity

text: The function of the springs used with channel or plate-type valves for reciprocating air compressors is to ____________.
options: open the valves during downward strokes
provide positive closing of the valves
reduce air intake and exhaust pulsations

text: The major difference between the discharge and suction  valves installed in most low pressure, reciprocating air compressors is that ___________.
options: one valve seats upwards, while the other seats downwards
the reed valves used on the discharge are made substantially thicker and heavier than the suction valves
the suction valve springs exert a greater tension than the discharge valve springs
the discharge valve springs exert a greater tension than the suction valve springs

text: Cylinder inlet valve failure in a low pressure air   compressor can be caused by _______________.
options: carbon buildup on the pistons from excessive lubrication
compressor overspeeding due to mechanical failure in  the unloader
valve pounding due to valve covers being excessively tight
sticking valves due to carbon buildup on the seats

text: An air compressor can be unloaded at start-up by __________.
options: relieving the intercooler pressure to the atmosphere
remove all but the frictional load during starting

text: Which of the following statements is true concerning the overall efficiency of air compressors?
options: High pressure compressors are more efficient than low  pressure compressors.
Mechanical efficiency divided by compressor efficiency will equal the overall efficiency of the compressor unit.
Two stage compressors are generally more efficient than  single stage compressors.
The volumetric efficiency is decreased in multistage  compressors.

text: Which of the following statements is true concerning V-belt drives for reciprocating air compressors?
options: Belts generally stretch slightly during the first few months of use.
An excessively tight belt will overload the bearings.
A loose belt will slip on the motor pulley and wear rapidly.

text: Condensate must be drained from the intercooler and   aftercoolers of an air compressor because _____________.
options: the cooling effect of the condensate reduces the   compressor's efficiency
a danger of explosion exists whenever water is present in a compressor
water causes erratic operation of pneumatic components
the volumetric capacity of the first stage is reduced if water remains

text: According to Regulations, if a pressure vessel, such as a ships service air tank, has defects which may  impair its safety, the tank shall be ____________.
options: hydrostatically tested at a pressure equal to the design pressure of the tank
hydrostatically tested at a pressure equal to 1 1/2 times the maximum allowable working pressure of the tank
pneumatically tested at a pressure equal to the working  pressure of the tank
pneumatically tested at a pressure equal to 1 1/2 times the design pressure of the tank

text: Regulations require safety and relief valves for air service to be provided with a substantial lifting device, capable of lifting the disk from its seat when the pressure in the vessel is ________________.
options: 50% of that at which the valve is set to blow
75% of that at which the valve is set to blow
110% of that at which the valve is set to blow
125% of that at which the valve is set to blow

text: Which of the following statements is correct when comparing the cylinder diameters of a two-stage reciprocating air  compressor?
options: The low pressure cylinder will be smaller than the high  pressure cylinder.
The high pressure cylinder will be smaller than the low  pressure cylinder.
The high and low pressure cylinders will be of equal size.
The low pressure cylinder will be smaller than the high  pressure cylinder but the piston stroke will be greater.

text: Which combination of the main shaft segments listed below, that are located furthest from the main engine, are   connected by the inboard stern tube shaft coupling?
options: Line shaft and stern-tube shaft space

text: Differential pistons used in some reciprocating air   compressors, serve to provide _____________.
options: a means of distributing side pressures over a wider area of each cylinder
a means of unloading during start-up
more than one stage of compression by each piston
a variable compression ratio enabling the output to be  varied to suit any load condition

text: The function of an unloader on a two-cylinder, two-stage medium pressure, air compressor is to _____________.
options: remove all but the friction load on the compressor motor when starting
prevent water accumulation in air lines

text: The clearance volume for a single stage compressor is  defined as the space created between the _____________.
options: top of the piston and bottom side of head, with the piston at BDC
piston and head, including the space around the piston to the top of the upper ring and under the valves, with the piston at TDC
bottom of the piston and bottom side of the head at TDC, regardless of upper ring location and valve placement
top of the piston and bottom side of the head at TDC as  compared to that which exists between the top of the piston and bottom side of head at BDC

text: One cause of leaky valves in a low pressure air compressor may be attributed to _________________.
options: running with an air filter element different from that  required by the original manufacturer's specifications
excessive operating hours without carrying out preventive maintenance

text: Compared to poppet and other mechanically operated valves, which of the following advantages is gained by using a thin plate (feather) low lift type valve in an low pressure air compressor?
options: They operate with a minimum of noise.
They are simple and easy to replace.

text: If all of the air charge has been lost from a ship service air receiver, the compressor mechanical unloading system will ____________.
options: fail to work, and the drive motor will trip the circuit  breaker as the compressor will be overloaded
fail to work, but the compressor having no load will start with little or no difficulty
Still function normally, but the safety interlock will  stop the drive motor

text: Which of the following statements describes the purpose of the split inflatable seal installed aft of the primary seal assembly for the propeller shaft?
options: To serve as a seal when adding packing to the stuffing box.
To allow repair or replacement of the primary seal elements when the ship is waterborne.

text: The exposed portion of the outboard propeller shaft is  protected against seawater corrosion by ________________.
options: a covering of plastic, rubber, or shrunk-on composition  sleeve
a layer of oxidation formed when the metal of the shaft is exposed to seawater

text: One of the determining factors regulating the time interval for drawing a vessel's tailshaft depends upon the design to reduce stress concentrations in accordance with Class  standards. Which construction method meets this design  criteria?
options: Stress relief grooves at the aft end of the propeller and forward end of the liner
Keyway cut as to give a sharp rise from the bottom of the keyway to the shaft surface

text: An excessive amount of water is prevented from entering a vessel using a water lubricated stern tube bearing by the use of the ___________.
If water were a semisolid, the linear distance a propeller would advance in one revolution is the _____________.
The lignum vitae in a stern tube bearing is normally  lubricated with _____________.
Which of the statements listed accurately applies to  controllable pitch propeller systems?
options: A pitch indicator is to be fitted on the navigation bridge for vessels 400 gross tons and above.
Independent remote control of pitch is to be provided at or near the oil distribution box. Tests of its operation are to be performed in the presence of the Surveyor.
After installation in the vessel, the complete piping system is to be subjected to a hydrostatic test equal to 1.25 times the design pressure.
The arrangement of piping is to be such that a single  failure in one part of the piping or pump unit will not  impair the integrity of the remaining parts of the system.

text: Compared to a constant pitch propeller, a controllable pitch propeller _____________.
options: more efficiently uses available engine power
operates at a lower efficiency at a fixed speed
produces the same torque at lower engine power
develops its rated power at a lower speed

text: The conical steel or composition cone installed on a  propeller, known as a fairwater cone, provides which of the following benefits?
options: To provide a ready means for the entry of cooling water.

text: Water leaking through the stern tube stuffing box is used to accomplish which of the following actions?

Some vessels are equipped with a water lubricated stern  tube. When at sea, operating under normal conditions, the water service valve from the ship's saltwater system to the bearing should be ______________.
options: closed, and no leakage permitted across the shaft packing
closed, and only slight leakage permitted across the shaft packing
open, and only slight leakage permitted across the shaft packing
open, and no leakage permitted across the shaft packing

text: The device used to retain the packing, that keeps water from entering the ship through the opening where the propeller shaft passes through the hull, is called a ____________.
Excessive propeller vibration may occur as a result of _________________.
options: high water pressure under the hull

text: Unusual hull, or propeller shaft vibrations can be caused by _____________.
options: excessive engine speed in shallow water
slight overheating of the line shaft spring bearings
high engine speed in deep water

text: Which of the following statements is correct concerning requirements for propellers?
options: A propeller may not be changed with one of a different pitch unless stress evaluations are supplied and permission is granted by a Marine Surveyor.
When steel propellers are used, zinc anodes are to be fitted on the aftermost strut bearing housing and on the forward most section of the rudder assembly.
The exposed steel of the shaft is to be protected from the action of the water by filling all spaces between the cap, hub and shaft with a suitable material.
Ultrasonic examinations of the propeller may be performed in lieu of required drydocking periods, provided certified  copies are distributed to the proper regulatory bodies.

text: Which of the components listed is used to connect several sections of line shafting?
options: Bolts fitted through flange type couplings forged integral with the shaft sections.

text: When the rotating shaft frequency and the natural vibrating frequency become synchronized at a particular speed, that speed is known as the _____________.
Keyless propeller hubs secured to tailshafts by "pilgrim nuts", are removed by _____________.
In order to prevent overheating of the packing in the stern tube stuffing box, _____________.
options: the gland is properly adjusted to permit a slight leakage of sea water
cooling water is supplied from the fresh water cooling  system
the gland nuts must be tightly taken up to prevent any water leakage

text: Following the withdrawal of the tail shaft, whichnon-destructive test could be used to locate cracks?  I. liquid penetrant dye  II. magnetic flux
A piece is missing from one blade of a four-bladed propeller and could result in ____________.
When the tailshaft is drawn from a vessel in drydock, which of the following inspections is required to be carried out?
options: The propeller hub taper and shaft keyway should be inspected for cracks or corrosion.
The stern bearing alignment with the stern frame should be checked.
The interior of the stern tube should be inspected for  leaks.
The shaft liner should be removed and inspected for cracks.

text: When a vessel is underway, a small amount of water is  allowed to leak through the water lubricated stern tube  stuffing box in order to ___________________.
options: flush out silt and mud which can accumulate from shallow water
ensure positive coolant flow through the strut bearings
prevent seizing of the rubber strips in the bearing bushing

text: Propeller pitch speed minus ship speed divided by the  propeller pitch speed is termed ______________________.
Vessel propellers are classified as being right hand or left hand. A right hand propeller turns clockwise when viewed from _______________.
Which of the following statements best describes an oil  lubricated stern tube bearing installation?
options: It receives its oil supply from a branch line of the main lube oil system.
No shaft liner is needed in the area of the babbitted  bearing surface.

text: When the propeller blades are integral with the hub, the propeller is called a _____________.
Any unusual, or new vibration in the hull or propeller  shafting can be an indication of _____________.
By which of the following means is force efficiently  provided to vary the pitch of the blades on a modern  controllable pitch propeller?
Inspection of a vessel's propeller during drydocking should include ____________.
options: noting the condition of the fairwater
logging inspection results in the official deck log

text: In a water cooled stern tube, a slight leakage of water  across the packing gland is provided to ____________.
options: flush all dirt and grit from the gland
flush all dirt and grit from the bearing staves
keep the stern tube fair water cool

text: The conical steel or composition cone installed on a  propeller, known as a fairwater cone, provides which of the following benefits?
Which of the devices listed prevents water from entering a ship's hull via the propulsion shaft?
options: Stern tube packing or mechanical shaft seal

text: Which of the following nondestructive testing methods can be used to detect a suspected subsurface defect in a tailshaft liner?
It is necessary to test the hydraulic system of a   controllable pitch propeller. According to regulation what  should be done?
options: The vessel must be drydocked and the propeller removed when performing this test.
The system pressure should be prevented from exceeding the maximum allowable operating pressure set forth by the manufacturer. prevent blowing the outer oil seal.
For precise regulation of the bearing temperature, the  system is required to have its own oil cooler.

text: Which of the following actions, pertaining to saltwater  lubricated stern tube stuffing boxes, is usually observed when the ship is expected to be in port for an extended  period?
options: The stuffing box is continually flushed.
The drain connection is left opened.
The stuffing box gland is tightened.
The packing is adjusted for greater cooling or replaced.

text: A bronzed sleeve is secured to the propeller tailshaft by _______________.
When the rotating shaft frequency and the natural vibrating frequency become synchronized at a particular speed, that speed is known as the _____________.
Stern tube and strut bearings lined with hardwood or rubber composition materials are lubricated with _____________.
A vessel has been equipped with an oil lubricated stern  bearing and is undergoing a routine drydocking. Which of the following inspections should be made when the tailshaft has been drawn?
options: The overall condition of the bearing surface should be  examined.
The shaft liner should be inspected for cracks and tested to insure a tight fit.
The stern tube should be tested for cracks with dye   penetrant.

text: The purpose of the propeller fairwater cone is to ________.
options: lock the propeller nut in position

text: Regulations require a single tailshaft with water lubricated tailshaft bearings, stress-relieved keyway, and fabricated from materials resistant to corrosion by sea water, to be drawn and examined once in every  ___________.
options: 8 years All necessary repairs must approved by the Chief Engineer of the vessel.

text: After packing is added to the stern tube stuffing box,which of the following procedures should be observed toinsure even tightening of the gland?
options: Listen for a smooth, regular sound.
Measure the distance between the gland and the stuffing box with a rule.
Judge by feeling the gland for an increase in heat as it is being tightened up.
Open the drain connection to allow seawater to flow in and make the distance even.

text: Which of the following statements represents an operating characteristic of neoprene or rubber stern tube bearings?
options: No stern tube packing is required.
The bearing is lubricated by seawater.
Propeller shaft vibration will not damage these bearings.
Neoprene bearings are less abrasion resistant than bronze bearings.

text: Which of the following statements describes the advantage of the mechanical shaft sealing system over the stuffing box and packing method of shaft sealing for propulsion shafting?
options: It eliminates the repairing or renewing of the shaft sleeve.
It is fully automatic in operation.
It allows for the removal and reinstallation of all parts without dismantling the shafts.
Each of the above is correct.

text: The stern tube flushing connection can be used to _________,
options: assist in flushing out all packing strips when renewing  packing underway
provide a positive flow of water through the stern tube for lubricating, cooling, and flushing when required
flush out brackish water resulting from extended in port operation
serve as the only source of supply of water for the stern tube bearing

text: One of the determining factors regulating the time interval for drawing a vessel's tailshaft depends upon the design to reduce stress concentrations. Which of the following  factors, in part, would be considered to meet this design criteria?
options: Stress relief grooves at the forward end of the propeller and aft end of the liner.
Keyway is to be cut so as to give a sharp rise from the  bottom of the keyway to the shaft surface.

text: The tailshaft is usually supported by the ____________.

On a vessel using a water lubricated tailshaft bearing, a single tailshaft without a special keyway, and not   constructed of materials resistant to corrosion by seawater, the tailshaft must be drawn and examined twice within any ____________.
To anneal a copper gasket, you should heat the gasket ______________.
options: cherry red and quench in water
and let it cool slowly in the air

text: Which of the following propeller materials will not require stress relief treatment after minor repairs, straightening of the tips or the thinning of the blade edges?
The probability of sulfide stress cracking in the presence of hydrogen sulfide is greatest for ____________.
When using the circular magnetic particle method to nondestructively test a weldment, the best results are  obtained when the ____________.
options: current is flowing at right angles to the discontinuity
magnetic field is parallel to the discontinuity
magnetic field is at right angles to the discontinuity
defect is located outside the parallel flux path of the  prods

text: In radiographic inspections, the standard test piece  included in every radiograph provides an effective check on the overall quality and bears a numerical relation to the thickness of the part being tested. This piece is  called a/an ____________.
The internal force of a material, which tends to resist deformation when subjected to external forces, is known as ___________________.
Copper sheet metal that is to be used as a gasket, is  usually annealed by heating it to a cherry red color, and then _____________.
When metal is tempered, it becomes _____________.
The energy associated with molecules is known as __________.
Copper is sometimes used for fluid power lines because it ______________.
options: has good resistance to high temperatures
withstands heavy vibration under heavy system loads

text: In certain circumstances, weldments are preheated before each pass to _____________.
options: increase the temperature gradient in the weld area
allow the use of smaller welding rods
eliminate the need for post heating

text: Which of the listed effects represents the purpose of heat treating steel?
Which of the listed methods used for testing a metal,  measures the ability of a metal to absorb the energy of a load rapidly applied to the member?

Which of the nondestructive testing methods can be easily used yet with good accuracy to detect, locate, identify, and measure defects in welds and base metals; regardless of  material type and structure?
Which nondestructive testing method is suitable for use in detecting and identifying a diameter subsurface void in a six inch diameter stainless steel shaft?
Reheating a hardened component to a temperature lower than the hardening temperature and then cooling it is known as _____________.
Ferrous metals are metals containing ___________.
A bourdon tube-type steam pressure gage is fitted with a siphon loop to prevent damage from ____________.
Which of the following guidelines is considered to reflect good design practices of shipboard steam heating systems?
options: Provide constant steam service for equipment operating throughout the year.
Provide either a common or individual reducing station for each group of units requiring the same operating pressure.
Run all piping so it may be drained by gravity.

text: Regarding the low pressure evaporator steam control orifice in the live steam supply line, the steam at the outlet of the orifice if not properly conditioned will be developed as _______________.
Which of the following statements concerning the operation of steam pressure reducing valves is correct?
options: Reducing station relief valves must be lifted by hand at least once a watch.
The cut out valve at the outlet of the reducing valve should be throttled when in operation.
Reducing valves should be warmed-up and drained before they are adjusted.
Reducing valves are absolutely reliable and require no  routine maintenance.

text: Mechanical energy in transition is referred to as _________.
The rate of heat transfer from a hot region to a cold region is affected most by the ____________.
The difference between the pressure at a point being  measured and that of a perfect vacuum is known as _________.
Steam supply piping to deck machinery is insulated in order to _____________.
options: maintain good turbulent free steam flow through the pipe
reduce heat loss from the system

text: The definition of superheat refers to the amount of heatnecessary to raise the temperature of a fluid and appliesto _________.  I. vapors  II. liquids
The action of water being circulated within a natural  circulation boiler, is an example of what form of heat  transfer?
If the fixed orifice in the evaporator steam supply line were to be removed, the steam pressure reducing valvelocated upstream would _____________.
options: be unable to maintain steam flow unless the proper range spring were to be installed
be suitable for maintaining the required steam flow under all load conditions
handle the operation satisfactorily as the control orifice develops an impedance to the steam flow

text: A vapor under pressure stays in contact with, and at the same temperature as the liquid from which it was generated. The vapor and liquid in this situation are, therefore, said to be in a/an ____________.
The basic function of a steam trap is to ____________.
options: regulate the flow of steam leaving a heater
limit the flow of steam leaving a heater until it has given up its latent heat of condensation
limit the flow of steam leaving a heater until it has given up its latent heat of fusion

text: Which of the following forms of energy is usually associated with relatively large bodies or objects?
The steam supplied to operate a steam driven reciprocating pump is usually obtained from the _____________.
options: desuperheated steam line via a reducing station
main steam line via a reducing station

text: Medium temperature hot water systems have been used in place of small auxiliary boilers producing 7bar steam with ____________.
By which of the listed methods may heat be transferred from one body to another?
Some auxiliaries are not designed to handle auxiliary steam at boiler pressure. Which of the devices listed is usually fitted in the branch line to deliver steam at the correct pressure?
The movement of steam piping, as a result of changes in temperature, is best compensated for by the use of _____________.
The sum of the sensible heat and the latent heat of any  substance is known as ____________.
A suitable location for the installation of an impulse type steam trap would be in the ____________.
options: horizontal run of drain line from the lowest point at the outlet of the heat exchanger
steam supply line to a feedwater heater
drain line from a high point in the main steam line   expansion loop
vacuum drag line from the atmospheric drain tank to the main condenser

text: Heat which brings about a change in the physical state of a substance without a change in temperature, is called _____________.
How many BTUs must be added to one pound of water at 32°Fto raise the temperature to 212°F?
Heat may be transferred by _____________.
Insulation on deck steam piping must be installed securely as _____________.
options: loose insulation contributes to steam leaks
deck steam lines are subject to vibration
steam driven machinery cannot run with loose insulation
loose insulation prevents normal pipe expansion

text: Which of the following forms of energy concerns the forces which bind atoms together in a molecule?
Which unit of measurement is used to measure mechanical potential energy?
Steam traps should be installed at the lowest outlet point of the heat exchanger in a horizontal run of pipe. Which of the following installation considerations should also be maintained?
options: They should be installed with their main axis horizontal.
They should be installed unpainted and wrapped in an  insulation blanket.
A bypass around the trap is unnecessary if the steam input pressure never exceeds 0.7kg/cm2
They should remain uninsulated and unpainted.

text: A Bourdon tube pressure gage is protected from the effects of the steam entering the pressure element by a/an _______.
options: exposed, uninsulated coil in the line leading to the gage

text: A portion of the cargo of an LNG carrier boils off during each voyage. How is the cargo boil off normally handled?
options: Compressed, condensed, and return to the cargo tanks.
Mixed with nitrogen and recirculated through the primary barrier.

text: The operation of a thermostatic steam trap depends upon the ______________.
options: action of a float within a chamber
difference in movement of two dissimilar bimetallic strips not fastened together
fact that hot water under pressure tends to flash into steam when its vapor pressure is rapidly reduced
change in the amount of vapor produced within an enclosed chamber

text: The heat required to change a substance from a liquid to a gas without experiencing a temperature change, is defined as the latent heat of _____________.
Thermostatic steam pressure reducing valves are used in the fuel oil service system to control the ______________.
options: double bottom fuel oil tank temperature
pressure of the fuel supplied to the burners
attemperator steam flow in the heater discharge circuit

text: Power is defined as the ____________.
options: amount of force needed to overcome friction
distance through which an object is moved

text: Which of the following statements represents the   definition of energy?
options: Energy is work that has been accomplished.
Energy is the capacity for producing an effect.

text: Which of the following terms could be applied to the  simple definition "the energy of motion"?
Thermal energy in transition is also referred to as _______________.
Which of the following modes of heat transfer does NOT  require any physical contact between a warmer and a cooler substance?
Which of the following statements concerning energy is correct?
options: Energy can be created or destroyed.
leather or neoprene diaphragm in the gage line
spring loaded bellows in the gage line

text: The boiling point of water in an open container at sea level is 100°C. If the pressure on the open container is   decreased, the boiling point will _____________.
Which of the following best describes the thermodynamic effect on the pressure and volume relationship with regards to saturated gases?
How can you determine the date that the pressure vessels on your ship will require a hydrostatic test or internal  inspection?
options: Check the pressure vessel block on the Certificate of  Inspection.
Check the pressure vessel addenda to the vessel's SOLAS certificate.

text: A leaking steam trap located in the return lines from a  heating system is indicated by excessive _____________.
A pounding noise occurring in one cylinder of a steam reciprocating general service pump could be caused by ________________.
options: lower than normal supply steam temperature
lower than normal supply steam pressure
improper adjustment of steam cushion valves
loss of air charge in the pump discharge plenum chamber

text: Latent heat can be defined as the heat which must be added to a substance in order to change it from a _____________.
A major controlling factor in the location of the condensate return piping from a heating system is/are ____________.
options: the requirement for a gravity return
the requirement for short laterals run
the requirement to minimize conflict with headroom and other services

text: Water hammer in a steam heating system can be caused by ____________.
options: filling the auxiliary boiler with cold water
steam admitted to a cold pipe
filling the auxiliary boiler with hot water
draining a soot blower line before cracking the steam supply valve

text: Which of the listed types steam traps operates on the  principle that hot water flashes to steam when its vapor pressure is rapidly reduced?
options: The total quantity of energy in the universe is always the same.

text: The difference between the pressure at a point being  measured and that of a perfect vacuum is known as _________.
Pigtails, or siphons, are used to protect Bourdon tube-type gages from the direct exposure to steam by ________________.
options: changing the direction of the steam flow
bleeding off a portion of the steam

text: A turbine exhaust pressure of 1.013 bar abs. is equal to _____________.
Admitting steam to an improperly drained heating system  could result in _____________.
options: high velocity slugs of water in motion in the system

text: A Bourdon tube-type steam pressure gage is fitted with a siphon loop to prevent damage from the direct effects of ____________.
Which of the listed reciprocating pump parts control the position of the pilot slide valve?
options: Movement of the main piston through the steam cylinder

text: The heat identified by only a change in temperature is known as ____________.
The operation of a thermostatic steam trap depends upon the ____________.
options: tendency of hot water to flash into steam
flow characteristics of a liquid as it passes through an orifice

text: Which of the statements listed concerning heat transfer is correct?
options: Heat is always transferred at a constant rate.
Heat transfer rate increases as temperature difference  increases.

text: Which of the following conditions must be carried outbefore the superheating of saturated vapor can occur?
options: The evaporation rate must be increased.
The flow of liquid to the unfired pressure vessel must be increased.
The vapor must be removed from contact with the liquid from which it was generated.
The operating pressure must be raised.

text: The latent heat of water vapor in air is dependent upon the _____________.
Which of the listed statements describes specific heat?
options: The amount of heat required to change the temperature of one pound of a material, one degree C.
The amount of heat required to change the temperature of one pound of ice, one degree C.
The amount of heat required to change the temperature of one pound of butter, one degree C.

text: Which of the following guidelines is considered to reflect good design practices for shipboard steam heating systems?
options: Provide orifice-type bypasses for all traps and automatic valves.
Provide a dirt pocket and strainer ahead of the steam trap on a unit heater return.
Provide all units with a dirt trap and gate valve in the supply and a check valve on the return.
Wherever possible install vertical runs for condensate  piping.

text: Which of the listed forms of heat transfer, if any, is  illustrated by the flow of combustion gases through a  boiler?
options: Both natural convection and forced convection

text: Which of the following forms of energy is demonstrated  through the process of combustion?
The basic function of a steam trap includes which of the following processes?
options: Regulate the flow of condensate from a heater.
Regulate the flow of steam from a heater.
Regulate the flow of steam to a heater.
Regulate the flow of condensate to a heater.

text: Which of the listed methods of heat transfer takes place when two substances of different temperatures are in  physical contact with each other?
options: Each of the above The high temperature region is known as a heat sink.

text: What is the cooling water source for most recovery unit  condensing coils aboard ship?
options: condenser water vapor at the outlet

text: According to regulations, a boiler combustion  control system may be tested at __________.
options: a pressure not less than 1.25 times the maximum allowable working pressure
a pressure not less than 1.5 times the maximum allowable working pressure
the maximum allowable working pressure of the system
the normal operating pressure of the system provided full loading is accomplished

text: If the steam flow entering the evaporator steam supply  orifice has a quality of 95%, which of the following  statements would be true?
options: The steam at the outlet would obtain a higher degree of desuperheat.
The steam at the outlet would be at a higher superheat than when it is supplied at 100% quality.
The steam would obtain a lower absolute pressure than when it is supplied at a quality of 100%.
The entrained moisture will become a totally saturated vapor prior to any superheat being acquired by the steam.

text: If "wet" steam is supplied to the air ejectors of a low  pressure evaporator, the passage of the mixture through the nozzles will ______________.
options: cause the potential level of kinetic energy to be partially converted to thermal energy
cause the existing level of thermal energy to be totally converted to kinetic energy
cause the level of thermal energy to be converted to  potential energy

text: When replacing a steam pressure reducing valve, what  information is required for the selection?
options: Maximum and minimum continuous flow rates (kgs./hr.)

text: What is the potential energy of an object weighing 10 kgs at a height of 10 mtrs?
Before starting a reciprocating, steam-driven pump that has been idle for a period of time, you should _____________.
options: open the steam line root valve

text: Which of the listed temperatures represents the saturation temperature for water at a saturation pressure of 1bar abs. (101.34 kPa)
Which of the following terms would best describe the temperature at which a liquid boils at a given pressure?

A stopper is inserted into the spout of a closed container in which water has been heated to a temperature of 100°C. If additional thermal energy is imparted, what changes will occur to the pressure and temperature inside the container?
options: both pressure and temperature will rise
only a change of state will occur

text: Steam tables can be used to obtain the _______________.
options: values for properties of water and vapor at various   conditions
specific fuel consumption under steady steaming conditions
steam generating capacity of a vessel's boilers
mechanical efficiency of the main unit

text: Energy existing in a system as a result of the relative velocities of two or more objects is termed_______________.
The aft, starboard bilge-well can not be pumped out, and back flushing has not been successful. The next practical solution will be to carry out which of the following  actions?
options: List the ship to port in order to pump out the bilges for the remainder of the voyage.
Place 75 Ltrs of fuel oil tank-wash into the  affected bilge well for one hour to dissolve the stoppage.
Take all necessary steps to properly and safely pump out the affected bilge well, then manually "muck" it out.
Change over and use one of the two remaining bilge pumps to dewater the starboard bilge well.

text: Which of the following actions should be taken FIRST if one bilge well of a multiple suction bilge system is unable to be pumped out?
options: Open the bilge pump for inspection.
Remove each of the suction manifold valves.
Remove only the suction manifold valve to the affected bilge well.
Attempt to pump out another bilge well to determine if the entire system is affected.

text: An independent bilge suction is required for ________.
Which of the following statements indicates compliance with Regulations concerning a vessel's bilge system? principle of the ___________.
If the air temperature in a manifold were found to be 122°F, what will be the equivalent reading on the centigrade scale?
If you determine that a steam reciprocating pump is   operating with too long of stroke, causing the piston nut to strike the cylinder head, you should _____________.
options: completely open the steam cushioning valves
install a smaller nut on the piston rod
adjust the valve gear to manufacturer's specified   measurements

text: The sensible heat of air is dependent upon the ____________.
The heat required to raise the temperature of 1 pound of a substance 1°F is called ________.
During routine maintenance the bilge manifold valves are removed. Prior to resecuring the valve bonnets, the valve ________.
options: disks and seats should be checked and lapped if necessary
bonnet, flange gaskets should be renewed if they were cut or torn
stem packing should be renewed if the packing has hardened, the glands have bottomed out, or the glands cannot be  tightened

text: Accidental flooding of the engine room bilges by the bilge main is prevented by ____________.
options: stop-check valves installed in the bilge suction manifolds
using a positive displacement reciprocating bilge pump
installing eductors in all bilge rose boxes
installing a swing check before each bilge valve

text: The emergency bilge suction valve is typically used _____________.
options: to inject cleaning additives when the bilges are extremely dirty
when the main condenser becomes fouled, in order to provide additional cooling water circulation
to connect the rose box to the independent bilge suction
if the bilges become flooded and they can not be emptied by any other means

text: What can happen to oil based mud when contaminated with salt water? controlled from above the floor plates.
options: The system must be capable of operation under all   practicable conditions after a casualty, whether the vessel is upright or listed.
Peak tanks, chain lockers, and decks over peak tanks may be drained by eductors, ejectors, or hand pumps.

text: Regulations for bilge and ballast  piping require that ________.
options: all oil and water tanks be connected to the bilge system
the bilge pumping system be operable under all practicable conditions
peak tanks and chain lockers have separate electric pumps

text: Referring to Regulations , the emergency bilge system ________.
options: is independent of the main bilge system
has a cross connection to the ballast system
is part of the independent bilge system

text: If one of the bilge manifold valves is prevented from  seating, the ________.
options: bilge-well connected to that valve, plus the second   bilge-well being pumped will be completely emptied
bilge system will lose vacuum, preventing other bilges from being pumped out
aftermost bilge-wells connected to that valve will siphon their contents to the forward bilge-wells

text: The bilge system is unable to pump out the aft starboard engine room bilge-well due to the bilge-well suction being fouled. With two feet of water over the top of the   bilge-well, which of the following actions should be carried out?
options: Send the wiper into the bilge-well with a scoop and pail.
Remove the effected bilge manifold valve and attempt to back flush the line.
Simultaneously operate all available bilge pumps.
Transfer half the contents of a drum of degreaser into the bilge well and pump out the bilge well with the system.

text: Bilge lines led through tanks, without using a pipe tunnel must be ___________.
options: fitted with return valves at the bilge manifolds
coated with coal tar epoxy, or similar corrosion resistant substance
fitted with nonreturn valves at the bilge suctions

text: The bilge system has been performing well; however, the aft starboard engine room bilge-well suddenly fails to be pumped out. Which of the following should be done first to  determine the cause?
options: Open the bilge pump for inspection.
Remove each of the manifold valves.
Remove only the manifold valve to the affected bilge-well.
Attempt to pump out another bilge well to determine if the entire system is affected.

text: The aft starboard bilge well is clogged, and back flushing has not been successful. The next practical solution would be to __________________.
options: change the trim of the ship to port in order to pump out the bilges for the remainder of the voyage
place  75.7 Ltrs (20 gallons) of fuel oil tank wash into the affected bilge well to clear the blockage Small amounts can cause thickening of the oil based fluid as well as a reduction in density.
properly and safely empty the well with a portable pump then manually muck it out Small amounts react with gel particles in the mud causing them to flocculate.
The water will collect at the bottom of the tank and can not be stripped overboard.

text: Regulations  require the emergency bilge system to ________.
options: be independent of the main bilge system
be a part of the independent bilge system
have a cross connection to the ballast system

text: A compound gage is typically installed on the _____________.
options: suction side of a bilge pump
exhaust manifold of an auxiliary diesel
discharge line from an air compressor
chemical feed tank of an evaporator

text: If one of the bilge system manifold valves does not properly seat, the ________.
options: bilge well connected to that valve, plus the second bilge well being pumped will be completely emptied
bilge system will lose vacuum and prevent the other bilges from being pumped out
bilge well aft connected to that valve will siphon its  contents to the forward bilge wells

text: When a horizontal type centrifugal pump is used for dewatering the engine room bilges, you would ________.  I. use a rotary, liquid piston type pump to obtain   a prime  II. partially open the sea suction valve, then   gradually switch over to the require
Which of the equipment listed is most effective in   processing bilge slops for overboard discharge?
options: Assembling the lube oil purifier as a separator and aligning it to the bilge overboard.

text: You are unable to pump out the aft starboard engine room bilge well that is fouled, with one foot of water over the top of the bilge well, what action should be carried out?
options: Send the wiper into the well with only a scoop and pail.
Remove the bilge manifold valve and attempt to back flush the line.
Simultaneously operate all available bilge pumps.
It is only necessary to transfer half the contents of a  drum of degreaser into the bilge well.

text: The process of grinding, shredding, or reducing the size of sewage particles is known as ________.
A P-type trap and drain is used _________.
options: for all lavatory sink installations, as no other drain type is useable
whenever the "gray" water drain system connection is located in the deck
whenever the "gray" water drain system connection is located in a bulkhead
as a condensate drain for superheaters

text: A vessel is in compliance with federal regulations regarding the discharge of sewage by ________.
options: treating sewage in an approved system
pumping the sewage ashore to an approved container

text: The flame screens installed on sewage system tank vents  prevent explosions by ________.
options: allowing the escape of flammable vapors
dissipating the heat of a fire
absorbing any flammable vapors in the vicinity
preventing flammable vapors from entering the tank

text: An S-type trap and drain is used _____________.
options: for all lavatory sink installations, as no other drain type is useable
whenever the "gray" water drain system connection is located in the deck
whenever the "gray" water drain system connection is located in a bulkhead
as a condensate drain for superheaters

text: Traps at plumbing drain connections prevent ________.
Machinery operating features are designed to help conserveenergy. Which of the following results will not contributeto energy conservation?
The general purpose of a heat exchanger is to _____________.
options: eliminate hot air from the condenser
maintain steady pressure in a system
heat, or cool one fluid by means of another fluid
reduce the engine room temperature in tropical climates

text: When a heat exchanger is used as a cooler, excessive cooling water flow should be avoided to prevent _____________.
In sewage treatment, the term "maceration" refers to the process of ________.
options: breaking up solid matter into fine particles
precipitating nondecomposed waste in a collection tank
chemically adjusting the sewage pH to 7.0
eliminating bacterium coli from the sewage

text: The drains of lavatory sinks are fitted with P-type or  S-type traps to _______.
options: reduce water velocity and minimize erosion
provide a cushion of water to reduce the effects of water hammer
provide a seal against sewer gas rising into the compartment

text: A pneumatic pressure tank is installed in a sanitary system to ________.
options: reduce excessive cycling of the sanitary pump
prevent the sanitary pump from losing suction
provide a higher pressure in the system then the pump can deliver
increase water flow through the system

text: The process of grinding or shredding sewage into smaller particles is known as _________.
Traps in plumbing drains are designed to help prevent _____.
options: drain noises in the living quarters
air pockets in the sewer lines
escape of odors into the living quarters

text: The temperature differential occurring between the inlet temperature of the fluid to be cooled and the outlet temperature of the cooling medium in a shell-and-tube heat exchanger is greatest in which of the flow designs listed?
The presence of scale and dirt on the saltwater side of a lube oil cooler is usually indicated by ___________.
options: seawater leaking into the lube oil system

text: Zinc anodes are installed in a marine raw-water cooling  system to _____________.
The floating end of a shell-and-tube type cooler utilizes a packing ring to ____________.
options: prevent leakage past the floating end tube sheet
baffle cooling water so it can be directed on a multiple pass
modify to a mixer type cooler

text: Hot water heating system converters are selected for the heating load required plus about 50% excess capacity to  allow for ____________.
options: unusual high demand on the system
increase capacity during times of peak demand
increase steam and water contact for better efficiency

text: Erosive tube failure in a heat exchanger can result from _____________.
Zinc rods are installed in the refrigeration system   _____________.
When performing a hydrostatic test on a low pressure evaporator to determine the source of suspected air leaks you should use ___________.
options: fresh water at or above the ambient temperature
circulated cold fresh water, while maintaining all normal unit operating pressures
air and a soap solution to test all joints
phosphorescent hydrazine bisodium solution visible under a black light source

text: Which of the following statements is correct concerning heat transfer?
options: Heat is given off from a high temperature region known as a heat sink.
Heat transfer rate is affected most by the size of the heat sink involved.
Heat transfer rate is affected most by the temperature  difference between the heat source and the heat sink.
Heat transfer by radiation will occur only by mass motion of a fluid substance.

text: Which of the following conditions must exist for heat to flow from one object to another?
options: The two objects must be in physical contact.
The two objects must be the same size.
There must be an existing temperature differential.
There must be an existing weight differential.

text: In a shell-and-tube type heat exchanger, the most common cause of decreased performance is _____________.
options: loose tube bundle baffle plates keep heat transfer surfaces shiny and clean
failure of the baffle plate prevent rapid accumulation of marine growth
rupture of the tube bundle provide a protective coating on heat exchanger surfaces
fouling on the seawater side reduce electrolytic action on heat exchanger metals

text: When conducting a hydrostatic test on the distillate cooler shell of a flash type evaporator, liquid appears well inside one of the tubes. In order to correct this, you should  _____________.
options: all of the above would be considered satisfactory as a  temporary repair until permanent repairs may be conducted

text: Which of the copper alloys listed is commonly used in  heat exchangers and possesses a high resistance to   corrosion?
The zinc plates are located in the _____________.
In a shell-and-tube type hydraulic fluid cooler, the amount of heat transferred from the hydraulic fluid to the cooling water depends upon ________________.
options: the temperature of the hydraulic fluid
the flow rate of both the cooling water and the hydraulic fluid
the temperature of the cooling water

text: The rate of heat transfer between the hot and cold fluids passing through a shell-and-tube type heat exchanger will ____________.
options: remain constant along the tube's length
be greatest in a single pass counter flow heat exchanger
remain constant throughout the heat exchanger
vary from section to section through out the heat exchanger

text: An 8-inch, globe-type, stop-check valve has been installed in the lube-oil cooler water outlet, with the flow coming in from the top of the disk. This means that  _____________.
options: the valve will remain completely open
the valve will prevent back flow
the valve will never permit water flow from the lube oil cooler
the valve will allow the lube oil temperature to be 10‚F (5.5°C) cooler than if the valve were properly installed

text: Expansion of the tube bundle in a shell-and-tube type cooler may be provided for by the _______________.
Evaporators and condensers are two forms of _____________.
options: heat exchangers paint and insulate the plates to prevent corrosion
liquid receivers renew the plates at each inspection
storage receptacles replace the zincs if deteriorated by 50%
flow inhibitors file the plates to change the negative value

text: Which of the following statements is true concerning a  radiator type heat exchanger?
options: Raw water is the cooling medium.
Water cooled exhaust elbows remove the heat.
The surge tank must be located a minimum of 4 feet above the highest finned tube.
Air flow provided by a fan is used as the cooling medium.

text: The tendency of a flammable liquid to vaporize is indicated by its ____________.
The lowest temperature required to cause self-sustained combustion of a substance independent of any outside source of ignition is called ____________.
The flash point of bunker fuel is defined as the __________.
options: lowest temperature at which the fuel oil will give off  enough vapor to momentarily flash into flame when ignited
lowest temperature at which the fuel oil will give off  enough vapor to continue to burn once ignited
temperature the fuel oil must be heated to for proper  atomization
temperature at which the fuel oil will burn in a boiler  furnace

text: The lowest temperature at which fuel combustion becomes  self- sustaining in the presence of a source of ignition, is defined as the ____________.
Which of the following statements describes the relationship between viscosity and specific gravity?
options: Liquids with different viscosities will always have the same specific gravity.
Liquids with different viscosities will never have the same specific gravity.

that if subjected to the standard fire test, they would be capable of preventing the passage of flame for one half hour
that if subjected to the standards fire test, they would be capable of preventing the passage of flame or smoke for one hour

text: In order to recognize the amount of anchor chain paid out, specific portions of the chain are color coded and wrapped with wire. The second shot of the chain is painted   __________.

end of the chain shackled to the anchor

end of the anchor chain fastened to the vessel

text: All of the links in the next to the last inboard shot of chain are painted __________.

of approved incombustible materials, but need meet no requirements relative to the passage of flame
that if subjected to a standard fire test, they would be capable of preventing the passage of flame for one half hour
that if subjected to the standard fire test, they would be capable of preventing the passage of flame and smoke for one hour

text: According to  regulation , an "A" class division, bulkhead or deck shall be constructed _________.
options: with approved incombustible materials and made intact from deck to deck and to shell or other boundaries
of approved incombustible materials, but need meet no requirements relative to the passage of flame
that if subjected to the standard fire test, they would be capable of preventing the passage of flame for one half hour
that if subjected to the standard fire test, they would be capable of preventing the passage of flame and smoke for one hour

text: The depth of fuel oil in a double bottom tank is measuredthrough the _____________.

options: not be critical until the vertical angle approaches 15‚
loose practically all of its strength as a support
most likely experience shear stress failure if not   reinforced immediately
not pose any problem, provided there are no cracked welds connecting it to any adjoining strength members

text: In order to recognize the amount of anchor chain paid out, specific portions of the chain are color coded and wrapped with wire. The first shot of chain is painted __________.

red on the detachable link and red on each link to either side of the detachable link

text: A shot of anchor chain is equal to __________.

be examined and approved by the Port State Control Officer of the port in which the vessel is being   constructed

text: The bleeder plug, or docking plug located on a motor vessel double bottom tank is used to ______________.

provide a secondary means of tank sounding frame

empty the tank when in drydock deck longitudinal

text: The duties of a chief engineer upon taking charge of the department include _____________.

taking a complete personal inventory of all engine room  spare parts
determining if any vital engine room equipment is   inoperative

text: When a vessel is drydocked for inspection, which of the  following valves are required to be opened during this  period?
options: High and low suction valves on fuel oil settling tanks
Bottom blow skin valves to the main and auxiliary boilers
Cooling water control valves on refrigeration condensers

separated from accommodation spaces by watertight and/or vaportight bulkheads

options: readily accessible gastight glands shall be provided
compressed air shall be used as the primary means to  discharge cargo
a pressure gauge or manometer shall be installed on the  bulkhead to ensure the gastight seal is maintained
the glands are to be kept under continuous suction with  power ventilation

text: The structural members of the hull extending in a fore and aft direction are called _________.

options: Fire pump and lube oil pump side longitudinals
Lube oil pump and distilling plant intercostals
Distilling plant and shaft alley door ____________. stiffeners

text: If a lifeboat winch allows a lifeboat to descend to the  water at an excessive speed, you should _____________.
options: remove unnecessary weight from the boat
adjust the davit mounted limit switches
engage the motor friction clutch bands

text: Sounding tubes and access openings for fuel oil tanks on cargo vessels are permitted by Regulations   to be located in which of the listed spaces?

options: In the bow of the ship at the waterline.
On the bottom of a fuel or ballast tank under the sounding tube.
Under the counter above the propeller blade tips.

text: The principal purpose of an anchor windlass chain stopper is to _____________.

absorb the brake thrust of the anchor windlass
hold the anchor chain while riding at anchor

hull girder strength in terms of the standard model
designed size of the beams, stiffeners, and shell plating
ICE strength classification of the hull

text: The minimum number of crew members permitted by law to operate your vessel can be determined by checking the _______________.
Expansion tanks used in a hot water heating system may be of the open or closed type. In a closed type system, what  would be the normal temperature range of the water?
Which of the following statements describes the function of an alarm annunciator on an engine room alarm panel?
options: An alarm condition causes a light and siren to come on which remain on until the machinery is secured.
A flashing light comes on, followed by an audible alarm. When an alarm acknowledge button is depressed, the audible alarm is silenced and light stays on.
An alarm condition gives an audible and visual alarm signal, both of which are secured when the alarm acknowledge button is depressed.
An alarm condition causes a flashing light to come on,  followed by an audible alarm. When the alarm acknowledge button is depressed, the warning light is extinguished.

text: With reference to a vessel's structural integrity, the most significant characteristic of a cryogenic liquid is its  ______________.

vapor cloud which reacts violently with saltwater

text: In order to recognize the amount of anchor chain paid out, specific portions of the chain are color coded and wrapped with wire. The third shot of chain should have __________.

three turns of wire wrapped around the stud of the third link on each side of the detachable link
three turns of wire wrapped around the stud of the link on each side of the detachabale link
one turn of wire wrapped around the stud of the third link on each side of the detachable link

text: If high relative humidity is maintained in a cargo hold,there is a significant possibility that ________.  I. there will be an accumulation of static   electricity  II. mold will grow and contaminate the cargo
