If a vessel is not provided with duplicate service systems, which of the systems listed will satisfy the regulations if serviced by two pumps?
When any vessel is drydocked, examination shall be made of the propeller, ___________.

options: of many initial operating tests performed on newly   commissioned boilers
which is used to determine the flash point of various marine fuels
which develops a series of temperature relationships in a test furnace
in which all emergency firefighting and related safety  equipment are tested

text: With regard to the opening and closing of watertight  appliances not fitted with a remote operating control or alarm system, which of the following is the master or person in charge of a MODU required to enter in the logbook?
options: The time required to close the appliances.
The reason for opening or closing each appliance.
The name of the person performing the opening and closing of such appliances.
The fact that the hull indicators functioned or not.

options: Provides a surface for the application of force, or the  installation of machinery.
Provides landing surface for the sounding bob of a tank  sounding tape.

text: Aboard tankers, the term Category "A" Machinery Space, as defined by Regulations  means any spaceincluding trunks and ducts to that space containing  ____________.
options: internal combustion machinery used for main propulsion
one or more oil fired boilers or oil fuel units
internal combustion machinery used for purposes other than main propulsion where the total collective power is at least 500 brake horsepower

text: If auxiliary machinery vital to the main propulsion system is not provided with independent duplicate systems, which of the systems listed would satisfy regulations if serviced by two pumps?
All of the links in the last shot of anchor chain are  painted __________.

options: propeller coupling and flange fasteners by the authorized inspector of the repair facility
keel and stabilizing fins by a registered marine engineer
stern bushing, sea connection and fastenings if deemed  necessary by the class surveyor

text: In ship construction, a large number of watertight bulkheads results in ____________.

decreased capacity to set flooding boundaries

a space in which cargo hose is stowed

text: When installing a new independent fuel tank for the   emergency lighting unit, which of the following statements must be strictly adhered to in accordance with Regulations?
options: The tank must be located on an open deck or in an adequately ventilated metal compartment in which the ambient   temperature never exceeds 150°F.
Iron or steel tanks shall be galvanized on the interior to prevent the formation of rust if any condensation should occur.
The fuel tank should be adequately supported and braced to prevent movement and insulated from the vessel's common ground to insure against static electricity.

text: Machinery driving fuel oil transfer and fuel oil service pumps must be fitted with a remote means of stopping the machinery from ____________.

Main circulating/bilge injection valve metallic shut off valves are welded to nonmetallic hull  materials
All of the above two non-remotely operated metallic valves are installed on either side of the deck or bulkhead regardless of   accessability

text: Regulations  require fresh water tank vents terminating within the machinery space to terminate well above the deep load line, unless the ________.

tanks have no boundaries in common with the hull
vents are fitted with ball float check valves or an   equivalent automatic closure device
tanks are fitted with suitable pressure-vacuum relief valves

text: Who is responsible for completing the station bill or muster list and posting it in a visible area aboard the vessel?

options: made of nylon or other nonmetallic material

text: How often should cargo oil pump relief valves on tank  vessels to be tested?
options: Prior to each cargo discharge operation.

text: A cofferdam is a/an _______________.
options: empty space between tank tops and bilges
cement baffle in a fresh water tank
empty space separating compartments to prevent the contents of one compartment from entering another in case of leakage

text: In a longitudinally framed ship, the longitudinal frames are held in place and supported by athwartship members called ________________.

faulty operation of the brine overboard pump

text: Which of the following descriptions best identifies the  operating principal of a flash-type evaporator?
options: Sea water is heated to boiling temperature while under a vacuum.
Sea water is passed over heated plates in a thin film.
Heated sea water is injected into a vacuum chamber.
Sea water is forced through a heated eductor.

text: One advantage of a triple-effect low pressure submerged tube distilling plant over a double-effect unit is _____________.

options: high temperature distillate can be recirculated to induce additional flashing
scale formation is not a severe problem
cold shocking is more effective in removing scale
water purity is greatly increased at high capacity

text: If the supplied steam pressure to the first effect of a  submerged tube distilling plant is allowed to fluctuate, the distiller _________________.
The temperature of the steam flow to the saltwater feed  heater, in a flash type evaporator can be manipulated by a/an ____________.
options: pressure regulator within the supply orifice
attemporator in the steam supply piping
sensor wired in with the three-way salinity dump valve

text: Excessive scale formation in a distilling plant may result from ____________.

secure the evaporator until the feedwater temperature can be raised to 74°C or more
raise the tripping point at the salinity indicating panel for the three- way valve

text: Where auxiliary steam supply is not used in the process of maintaining vacuum in a distilling unit, the vacuum is  usually attained by ________.

options: increased heat levels throughout the entire unit
decreased priming and lower salinity distillate
first effect scale formation to be loosened
pressure and temperature fluctuations in the entire unit

text: Which of the listed problems could produce a high absolute pressure within a flash type evaporator?
options: a leak in the first stage demister
a cracked distillate pump vent line

text: A salinity indicating system functions on the basic   principle of measuring the _____________.

options: eliminate most of the scale formation in the first effect
increase the heat level in all effects

the condensation of the saltwater feed

slightly higher than the second stage vacuum
slightly lower than the first stage vacuum

options: increased heat levels throughout the entire unit
decreased priming and lower distillate salinity
first effect scale formation to be lessened
pressure and temperature fluctuations in the entire unit

text: When securing a centrifugal distillate pump, which of the listed steps should be carried out FIRST?
options: Close the sealing line valves to the pump.

condensing water droplets on the metal vanes
trapping water droplets in the metallic mesh
abruptly changing the direction of vapor flow

options: salt water feed heater absolute pressure will decrease  significantly
salt water feed heater absolute pressure will increase  significantly

Where would a salinity cell be installed in a double-effect distilling plant?
options: All of the above dump distillate to the makeup feed tank

text: Condensate formed within the first-effect tube nest of a double- effect, low pressure evaporator, is removed by a  _______.

options: steam flow rate to the air ejectors
float controlled level of the feed heater
throughput of the brine overboard pump

text: A fixed orifice plate is generally installed in the live steam supply to an evaporator. One of the functions of this orifice is to control the quantity of the supplied steam. A second function is to ____________.
options: reduce pressure fluctuations from occurring in the steam supply to the salt water feed heater
produce more useful superheated supply steam
guarantee that the steam supply will remain desuperheated
produce a low salt water feed heater shell absolute pressure

options: vacuum drag from the auxiliary condensate system

options: takes suction from the first stage of the evaporator
takes suction from the second stage of the evaporator as well
takes suction from the first stage ejector discharge
is normally idle and used mainly as a standby unit

text: A flash type evaporator is designed to operate in 24°C sea water, if operated in 10°C sea water, ________.
options: the absolute pressures will be slightly below the predicted design absolute pressures
absolute pressure in the evaporator stages will be   higher
the unit will operate at reduced capacity
thermal efficiency will decrease, but brine density will increase

text: Which of the following statements describes the proper  operation of a three-way solenoid trip valve on a flash-type evaporator?
options: The valve must be manually reset.
The valve will trip if the sea water feed temperature drops below 79°C.
The valve is interlocked to trip only on de-energization of the solenoid.
The valve will trip if the distillate is too warm.

text: The purpose of chill shocking an evaporator is to _________.
options: prepare for a hydrostatic test The brine density is improper.
stress relieve the tubes Heat transfer surfaces are being layered with scale.
break loose scale deposits formed on the tubes Impure distillate is being produced.
test for leaks in the tubes Shell vapor pressure is constantly decreasing.

text: In which of the following types of evaporators is the  control of brine density least important?
When securing a centrifugal type distillate pump, which of the listed steps should be carried out first?
options: Close the pressure gage isolation valves.
Secure the casing vapor vent line.
Secure the stuffing box sealing water line.
Trip the three way dump valve.

text: Improper waterside venting of the tube nests in a submerged tube evaporator will cause a/an _____________.
options: increased vacuum in the first effect

text: Indicated high salinity of the distillate discharged from a flash-type distilling plant will be a result of ___________.
options: maintaining the proper distilling plant heat balance
carrying the brine level below normal
venting of the saltwater heater drain pump

text: Irregular feeding or surging of the feedwater supply to a flash evaporator may be attributed to ______________.
options: erratic water flow through the air eductor
a clogged vent line from the air eductor condenser
excessive pressure in the seawater feed heater
a dirty strainer in the saltwater feed pump suction line

text: In a flash evaporator, heated water under pressure is  converted into vapor by suddenly ____________.

options: scale formation on the tube nests
loss in capacity and maintain plant efficiency

text: A fluctuating and unsteady vacuum in an evaporator may be caused by _____________.
options: wet steam entering the air ejector nozzle
pinhole leaks in the evaporator tube nests
rapid scaling on the evaporator tube nests
high water levels in the last effect

text: Which of the listed chemicals can be routinely used to  maintain the hygienic quality of potable water?
options: Hydrochloric acid fluctuations of the absolutepressure tend to cause _____________. scale formation with increased heat transfer
priming with increased salinity of distillate
slight foaming at the distillate feed pump
improved operating conditions at the brine pump

text: A multistage flash evaporator will be unable to produce  consistently pure distillate if the __________________.
options: tubes of the saltwater feed heater are flooded internally

text: Rapid fluctuation of the last effect shell vacuum in a  submerged tube evaporator can cause _____________.
options: air leaks in the tube nest

text: If a higher than normal water level is observed through the inspection port of a flash evaporator, you should suspect ______________.
options: a leak in the feedwater heater

text: In a flash evaporator, scale as a result of higher than  normal temperatures is most likely to occur in the ________.

options: distillate produced in the second stage would flow towards the first stage
evaporator absolute pressure would eventually equalize in both stages to that of the first stage
evaporator absolute pressure could eventually equalize in both stages to that of the second stage

text: Which of the following statements represents the basic  principle of operation of an electrical salinity indicator?
options: Measures the electrical resistance of the water.
Determines the conductivity of the dissolved oxygen.
Measures the voltage of the chloride ions.

text: Vapor is produced in a flash evaporator by introducing _______________.
options: heated water into a high pressure area
heated water into a low pressure area

text: In the operation of a two-stage flash type distilling plant, the rate of scale formation is greatly reduced by _________.
options: operating the unit at its rated capacity
maintaining a relatively high feedwater temperature
the fact that the first-stage regulator keeps the heater shell at a constant pressure
the fact that no boiling occurs on heat transfer surfaces anywhere in the unit

text: When any low pressure distilling plant is operated at less than the designed vacuum, the _____________.
Potable water piping systems installed aboard ships must be _______.
options: disinfected monthly with a chlorine compound
independent of all other piping systems
cadmium lined to prevent internal corrosion
flushed each time potable water is taken onboard

text: A recirculating line is provided in the potable hot water system to __________.
options: maintain uniform inlet conditions for the constant speed supply pumps
maintain flow rates which will eliminate fluid film   formation in the piping
return overflow from the pressure tanks back to the potable water tanks
maintain the desired temperature range throughout the system

text: A solenoid operated distillate bypass valve is installed in the discharge line between a distilling plant and the  potable water tanks to prevent ____________.
options: excessively warm distillate from entering the potable tanks
salty distillate from reaching the potable tanks
overfilling and overflowing the potable tanks
contaminated water from leaving the potable tanks

text: Brine density in a distilling plant is measured with a/an _____________.
In a flash type evaporator, all saltwater headers are vented through individual vent cocks to the _________.
The condensers located in the various stages of a flash  evaporator are cooled by _____________.
Scale accumulation on evaporator heating surfaces will cause ___________.
The first stage feedwater temperature and shell absolute pressure in a multistage flash evaporator is _____________.
options: equal to the second stage feedwater temperature and shell absolute pressure
higher than the second stage feedwater temperature and shell absolute pressure
lower than the feedwater temperature and shell absolute  pressure in the second stage scale formation increases
higher than the second stage feedwater temperature but at a lower shell absolute pressure distillate purity increases

text: Short cycling of the potable water pump is avoided by the proper use of a ________.
options: hydro-pneumatic tank completely filled with compressed air at system operating pressure
charge of air, at 1.4 kg/cm2 (137.9 kPa) above system pressure with the hydro-pneumatic tank one quarter full of water
charge of air, at system pressure, when the hydro-pneumatic tank is half full of water

text: Which of the following components would be necessary for the proper operation of a potable hot water system?
Distilled water from an evaporator may be discharged into a potable water tank _____________.
options: after passing through an activated charcoal filter
after chemical analysis shows it is fit for consumption
through a solenoid operated three-way valve

text: Tube leaks in the distillate cooler of a two stage flash evaporator will result in _____________.
options: contamination of the second stage condenser

text: Why would a flash type distilling unit be more efficient when operated in cooler seawater temperatures?
options: Steam carryover between stages is reduced.
The amount of available flash steam is decreased.
Feedwater flow from the feedwater heater is increased.

text: The steam coils in a high pressure evaporator used for  saltwater service should be descaled with _____________.
If the rated distillate production of a submerged tube type evaporator cannot be maintained with the supplied maximum steam flow rate, the evaporator ______________.
In a two stage flash evaporator, operating conditions in the second stage, as compared to the first stage, are _________.
options: higher temperature and higher absolute pressure steam trap
higher temperature and lower absolute pressure butterfly valve
lower temperature and higher absolute pressure loop seal
lower temperature and lower absolute pressure adjustable controller

text: Salinity cells are strategically installed in distillingunits to indicate the ___________.  I. purity of the distillate produced  II. quality of the distillate produced
