In a flash-type evaporator, an electrical salinity cell  would be installed in the _____________.
options: distillate outlet from the distillate cooler
distillate inlet to the distillate cooler
condensate drains from the distiller feedwater heater

text: The demisters installed in a flash-type evaporator serve to ____________.
options: deaerate the first and second effect distillate
remove small water droplets entrained in the flashed vapor

text: The inability to maintain proper vacuum in a submerged tube evaporator can be caused by _____________.
options: fluctuating steam pressure to the air ejector nozzle
improper venting of the evaporator tube nests
air leaks in the evaporator tube nests
high water level in the evaporator shell

text: The size of the loop seal orifice for a flash-type   evaporator is important for maintaining the control of ________.
options: the vacuum differential developed between both stages
the absolute pressure differential developed between both stages
the absolute pressure developed in the salt water feed  heater shell
maintaining distillate flow between the first and second stage in order to avoid dry seal operation of the loop

text: If the distillate temperature from a flash type evaporator falls below 71°C _________.
options: the distillate may be directed to the storage tanks
the automatic three-way solenoid dump valve should direct distillate to the bilge
priming will occur in the first-effect

text: Which of the listed conditions will cause the feedwater, that has not flashed to vapor in the first-stage of a flash evaporator, to flow into the second-stage?
options: Difference in brine density between first and second stages 2/32nds

text: Potable and nonpotable water systems aboard ship _________.
options: may be temporarily connected with a removable spool
are permanently connected through a double stop valve
may never be cross-connected by any means
may be connected if they are used only for wash water

text: Partially clogged or scored nozzles in a two stage air  ejector unit of a flash distilling plant could result in a ____________.
options: fluctuating vacuum in the flash chamber
high water level in the air ejector after condenser
malfunctioning check valve in the air ejector discharge

text: Small droplets of water entrained in the flashed vapor  produced in a flash-type evaporator, are removed by the  _________________.
In a double-effect distilling plant, the brine particles remaining entrained with the vapor produced in the   evaporator first effect, are separated by ____________.
One function of the vapor feed heater in a double-effect distilling unit is to _____________.
options: condense part of the vapor given off in the first-effect
preheat distillate circulating to the second-effect tube nest
cool the incoming first-effect evaporator feed
condense all the distillate in the second-effect tube nest

text: The heated feedwater entering any flash chamber of a flash- type evaporator will _____________.
options: vaporize, with the unflashed water remaining at the   temperature at which it entered the flash chamber
vaporize, with the unflashed water equalizing to the  saturation conditions existing in the flash chamber
vaporize, with the remaining water at a temperature greater than it entered the flash chamber
boil, allowing steam bubbles to rise through the brine at the bottom of the flash chamber

text: On a multistage flash-type evaporator, the flash chamber is ______________.
options: combined as part of the salt water feed heater
the open area above the brine levels in the first and second stages
combined as part of the distillate cooler
another term used to describe the vapor feed heater

text: When a flash evaporator is being operated in extremely cold water, you may need to throttle the seawater supply to  ________.
options: prevent cold shocking the evaporator low pressure steam admitted to the feedwater heater
maintain the feedwater temperature above the required  minimum input temperature heat exchange in the first-stage feedbox
avoid flooding the evaporator shell vaporization in the first-stage flash chamber
increase the evaporator distilling rate heat exchange in each stage distiller condenser

text: The efficiency of a flash type evaporator can be increased by _________.
options: decreasing the absolute pressure of each stage
increasing the saltwater feed heater temperature
increasing the pressure at the spray pipe

text: In the operation of a flash type evaporator equipped with air ejectors, the air and noncondensable gases are evacuated directly from the _____________.
Less scale formation occurs in a flash evaporator than in a submerged tube evaporator because ____________.
options: the distillate produced has greater purity
no boiling occurs on heat transfer surfaces
evaporation occurs at a higher rate
the incoming feed is at a higher temperature

text: The thickest deposit of scale in a flash evaporator is mostlikely to occur in the tubes of the ____________.
In a two stage flash distilling plant, which of the pumps listed is vented to the shell of the second stage in order to remove vapor from the pump suction?
Why will a two stage flash type distilling plant tend to operate more efficiently when operating in cold seawater?
options: The colder seawater passing through the condenser tube  bundles of the various stages increases evaporator vacuum.
The amount of heated feedwater discharged from the feedwater heater aftercondenser is increased.
Fewer noncondensable gases are created by the flow of colder seawater.
More seawater is allowed to pass through the first stage overflow weir.

text: One advantage of a flash distilling plant when compared to a submerged tube distiller is _____________.
options: greater distillate purity through high temperature   evaporation
cold shocking for scale removal is not required
less internal corrosion because of lower brine density
less feedwater is required for equal plant capacity

text: Which of the following statements is correct relative to distillation plant operation?
options: Distillation is the process of boiling seawater to produce vapor which is condensed into fresh water. increases brine density
Evaporation is the second part of the distillation process where brine is removed. increases distillate salinity
Brine is the result of condensed sea water vapor. reduces metal corrosion
Distillate is the product resulting from the evaporation of fresh water vapor. reduces heat transfer

text: In a two-stage flash evaporator, the sea water feed   temperature is increased as it passes through the _________.
The quantity and density of brine discharged from the last effect of the distilling plant should be as constant as  possible to ________________.
options: prevent scale accumulation on heat exchange surfaces
maintain a constant distillate outlet temperature
ensure distillate quality and distilling plant efficiency
prevent any salinity in the distillate produced

text: In a flash type evaporator, the brine overboard pump is  vented directly to the ______________.
The advantages of flash type evaporators, as compared to submerged tube type evaporators, include _____________.
options: less internal corrosion because of lower brine density
higher temperature evaporation for lower salinity of the distillate produced
less scale formation in a flash evaporator
less feedwater required for a flash evaporator

text: Which of the following conditions is most likely to cause water hammer in the potable water system?
options: The hydro-pneumatic tank being properly charged.
The starting of the potable water pump.
A low water level in the potable water storage tank.
Rapid closing of a spring closed basin faucet.

text: Which of the following statements represents an advantage of a triple-effect submerged tube evaporator compared to a  single- effect submerged tube evaporator?
options: Lower consumption of steam per pound of distillate produced
Less complex to maintain and operate

text: Potable and non-potable water systems aboard vessel ______.
options: may be temporarily interconnected with a removable spool
are permanently interconnected through a double stop valve
may never be interconnected by any means
may be connected if they are used only for wash water

text: Air leaking into a flash type distilling plant could occur through ___________.
options: gasketed joints fill the unit with saltwater
valve stems fill the unit with descaling compound
gage glass packing completely drain the unit
all of the above tightly seal the unit to exclude air

text: The rate of scale formation in a flash type distilling plant is greatly reduced by _____________.
options: operating the unit at its rated capacity
maintaining a relatively high feedwater temperature
water flashing into vapor rather than boiling on the heat transfer surfaces
operating the first-stage regulator to maintain a constant shell pressure

text: An excessively high brine level in a flash evaporator can be caused by ___________.
If the distilling plant first stage absolute pressure is higher than normal, while the second stage absolutepressure remains satisfactory, the probable fault is due to________.
options: loss of the distillate loop seal between the stages
an air leak through a vacuum gage line in the first stage

text: An automatic solenoid dump valve should be set to trip if the evaporator produces distillate with salt content  exceeding ___________.

If a hole were to form in the division plate between stages of a flash type evaporator _________.  I. vaporization of the feed water would continue at  second stage designed saturation conditions   II. all of the distillate produced would be certain to  b
Priming in a submerged tube evaporator can be caused by high water level and _____________.
Evaporator priming can be caused by high water level,  fluctuating steam pressure, or ____________.
options: a brine overflow weir set too low

text: Which of the following statements is true concerning any evaporator?
options: High conductivity of the distillate indicates distillate salinity is excessive. a needle gun
Increasing the absolute pressure of the shell will increase the distiller's capacity. soap and water
Mesh separators are used in evaporators to filter the  distillate. service should be descaled with _____________. a wirebrush

text: Feedwater supplied to a flash type distilling plant will flash to vapor in the first-stage due to the flash chamber _________________.
options: pressure being lower than the saturation pressure   corresponding to the feedwater temperature
temperature being higher than the evaporation temperature of the supplied feedwater
heat exchange surfaces being directly in the path of the entering feedwater
orifices finely atomizing the heated feedwater entering the flash chamber

text: A constant pressure range is maintained in the potable water system of many vessels by using ________.
options: an air cushion in the hydro-pneumatic tank
a pressure regulator in the pressure tank discharge piping

text: Salinity cells are strategically installed in distillingunits to indicate the ___________.  I. location of a saltwater leak  II. size of the leak
In the operation of a two-stage flash-type distilling plant, which of the pumps listed should be vented to the second stage shell in order to remove vapors from the pump suction?

If a hole were to form in the division plate between stages of a flash type evaporator _________.  I. vaporization of the feed water would continue   II. efficiency of the unit would only be moderately affected
Carryover in a flash type distilling plant can be a result of ___________.
options: faulty operation of the brine overboard pump
a pressure drop through the loop seal

text: When a flash-type evaporator is to be secured for an  extended period of time, the entire unit should be drained, cleaned, and ____________.
A salinity indicator is used to determine the _____________.
In the production of freshwater from seawater through a  process of heating and cooling, the cooling phase of  production is usually called _____________.
The process of boiling seawater in order to separate it into freshwater vapor and brine is usually defined as __________.
In a two-stage flash-type distilling plant, the mesh-type vapor separators function to __________________.
options: direct flash steam into the cooler

text: The quantity of heating steam supplied to the feedwater  heater of a two-stage flash type distilling plant is held relatively constant by a/an __________.
An unsteady vacuum in a submerged tube evaporator may be caused by _____________.
options: air leaks in the tube nests
improper venting of the tube nests
inadequate steam quality to the air ejector nozzles
high water level in the shell

text: Why is a self-sealing pump generally not satisfactory for service as a brine overboard pump?
options: Hot brine tends to form scale on the impeller peripheral edge.
Brine density at 1.5/32 cannot be maintained.
Hot concentrated brine tends to flash, leaving scale  deposits to clog the sealing line and gland seal ring.
Hot brine tends to clog the brine pump suction line   strainer.

text: For optimum noncondensable gas removal from a low pressure evaporator, the air ejector suction is usually taken  directly from _____________.
options: the salt water feed heater shell
anywhere on the second stage shell of the evaporator
the shell of the first stage distilling condenser
the shell of the second stage distilling condenser

text: The purpose of the flash chamber in a soloshell double  effect distilling unit is to _____________.
options: raise the temperature of the evaporator feedwater
prevent flashing at the suction of the brine pump
allow steam expansion within the first effect tube nest
collect and combine distillate formed at different   pressures

text: A three-way solenoid operated dump valve installed on a  flash-type evaporator unit, will divert impure distillate to the bilge _________.
options: only if the cell selector switch is positioned to monitor the first stage distillate outlet
only if the cell selector switch is positioned to monitor the final stage distillate outlet
regardless of the cell selector switch position
only if power to the salinity panel is interrupted

text: The mesh type steam separators located in a two-stage flash distilling plant, function to _____________.
In a double-effect distilling plant, the evaporator feed remaining entrained in the first-effect vapor, is removed by __________________.
In a flash type distilling plant, a screen is fitted over the suction connection to the brine overboard pump in  order to prevent ______________.
options: excessively large salt crystals from damaging the pump
scale from passing into the brine pump and clogging impeller passages
electrolysis from occurring in the pump

text: On which of the following heated surfaces of a flash type evaporator would you be more likely to find soft scale  formation?
Steam passing through the control orifice installed in the live steam supply to a low pressure evaporator will develop significant superheat as a result of _____________.
The purpose of a three-way, solenoid, dump valve on an evaporator is to ___________.
options: prevent excessively saline distillate from entering the  freshwater system
allow the evaporator's first- and second-effects to be  drained with one valve

text: A solenoid-operated distillate three-way valve is installedin the discharge line between the distilling plant and the potable water tanks. This valve will trip and dump the distillate discharge if the _____________.
options: distillate salinity is excessive the continued use of a drain pump
distillate temperature is excessive gravitating the condensate to the atmospheric drain tank
potable water tank has been filled with raw water vacuum drag to the auxiliary or main condenser
potable water tank has become contaminated the use of a loop seal to the combined low pressure feed heater

text: The vacuum maintained in the salt water feed heater shell of a flash- type evaporator is generally accomplished by  ____________.
options: a direct unimpeded connection between second stage and salt water feed heater
either by vacuum drag or an external line from the first stage via a fixed orifice
the fixed orifice provided in the steam supply line

text: In a flash distilling unit, evaporator feed (seawater) first absorbs heat in the ___________.
The second stage feedwater temperature and shell absolute pressure in a multistage, flash type, distilling plant is _____________.
options: higher than the first stage feedwater temperature and  absolute shell pressure
lower than the first stage feedwater temperature and  absolute shell pressure
the same as the first stage feedwater temperature and  absolute shell pressure
not related to the feedwater temperature and absolute shell pressure

text: Short cycling of the potable water system's pump is   prevented by using ________.

A flash type distilling plant is more efficient than a high pressure evaporator as _________.  I. vaporization of the feed water occurs in a vacuum II. boiling of feed water occurs only once on the tube surfaces
If the brine level of a double effect soloshell evaporator is above the sight glass, which action should be taken?
options: Do nothing as this is the normal operating level.
The feed rate should be reduced and the brine discharge  valve opened slightly.
The brine overflow weir should be raised to allow greater outflow.
The brine section should be drained down a minimum of 150mm below the seawater heater bundle.

text: The use of a needle valve in a piping system is recommended when requiring _____________.
Piping cross-sections ranging from 3mm to 300mm in diameter, are sized by ____________.
options: tubes of the salt water feed heater
tubes of the air ejector condenser

text: Under normal operating conditions, the highest temperature and pressure conditions existing in a flash-type evaporator will occur in the _______________.
In a two-stage flash-type evaporator, excess brine in the first stage automatically passes _____________.
options: directly to the second stage feed heater
directly overboard through the brine cooler
into the second stage flash chamber
into the second stage vapor condenser

text: Rapid fluctuations in the shell pressure in a submerged tube distilling unit will cause ___________.
options: increased heat levels in the entire unit
decreased priming and high salinity distillate
first effect scale formation to be reduced
priming in the last effect shell

text: When any low pressure distilling plant is operated with less than the designed vacuum, the _____________.
The primary reason low pressure evaporators produce   distillate more efficiently, and with less scale formation, than high pressure evaporators is ____________.
options: evaporation is accomplished in a vacuum
due to the higher temperature of the incoming feed
due to the latent heat of evaporation principle
evaporation in a submerged medium produces more distillate

text: The designation "schedule 80 extra strong " refers to ____________.
On tankers using manually operated tank valves, the deck hand wheel indicator registers the ____________.
options: exact lift position of the tank valve disk, through 100% of its operation

text: Which of the following descriptions could be applied to  short lengths of pipe called nipples?
options: Fully threaded, half threaded, long, and short
Standard, extra-strong, double extra-strong, and schedule 80

text: Which of the valves listed will be cycled from fully open to fully closed when the handle is turned 90°?
As routine maintenance, the bilge manifold valves are  periodically removed and examined. Prior to resecuring the valve bonnets, the valve _____________.
options: disks and seats should be checked and lapped if necessary
bonnet flange gaskets should be renewed if they were cut or torn
stem packing should be renewed if the packing has hardened

text: A gate valve installed in a piping system should be used _______________.
options: in either fully closed or fully opened positions
only with the stem facing down
to throttle the flow of liquid

text: Which of the listed materials have been used to manufacture piping available in standard weight, extra strong, and  double extra strong wall thickness?
If used for regulating fluid flow through a piping system, which of the valves listed could be damaged?
When pipe is properly screwed into a tapped hole, it will give the correct fit when ______________.
options: half of the threads are covered
all but two or three threads are covered

text: Excessive leakage and premature failure of valve packing is a result of ____________.
options: jamming a valve in the closed position
low pressure fluid flow through the pipeline

text: To connect two lengths of pipe previously set in place, you should use a pipe _____________.
An arrow stamped on the valve body of a water regulating valve indicates the ______________.
options: approximate number of turns the tank valve has been opened
level of oil in the tank

text: Fittings used to close the ends of pipe are called "pipe _____________".
The valve best suited for throttling gas or liquid flow in a pipeline is the _____________.
On tank vessels equipped with power operated cargo tank  valves, the type of power operator most commonly used is ______________________.
Compared to globe and angle valves, gate valves ___________.
options: operate with little or no pressure drop when fully opened
cannot be used for service requiring infrequent operation

text: Which of the problems listed could happen if you attempted to force open a valve "frozen" in position by using a wrench on the handwheel?
Which of the valves listed below is best suited for   throttling service?
Piping cross-sections over 300mm in diameter are sized by the __________.
Which of the pipe materials listed has the best resistance to internal and external corrosion?
When in the completely opened position, which of the listed types of valve will offer the least resistance to flow?
Fabric type packing, such as flax or hemp, is best suited for _____________.
When assembling a run of liquid service piping, which of the following factors should be considered?
options: How the type of fittings used will affect the flow.
What type of gasket material should be used.
What radius should be used for each bend in the run.

text: Small leaks in gaskets should be stopped immediately after installation because _____________.
options: the leak will probably worsen in time
the gasket may not seal itself for several hours
the leak will result in severe flange distortion
small leaks cause the pipeline to creep

text: An inverted gate valve (stem pointing downward) may be  subject to wear sooner than a similar valve installed in the upright position because ______________.
options: the gate's full weight is upon the stem
the gland stud nuts are under more pressure
the packing will not properly adhere to the stem

text: The best gasket material to use in cargo fuel oil lines is _____________________.
Gate valves should not be used for throttling as __________.
options: the pressure drop will be excessive
the installation of an equalizing line will be necessary
wire drawing of the disc will result

text: Which of the fittings listed should be used for the   installation of piping to permit removal of the pump for servicing?
Which of the following statements represents the proper  relative direction of flow through a globe valve?
options: Direction of flow through the valve is unimportant.
Direction of flow through the valve depends upon the type of seat design used in the valve.
Direction of flow should be from below the seat.
Direction of flow should be from above the seat.

text: One revolution at the handwheel of a reach rod operated  valve will cause the valve stem to rotate ____________.
Which of the valves listed below permits flow in only one direction?
On tankers with manually operated tank valves, the type of valve most commonly used is the _____________.
options: check valve packing valves with badly grooved stems

text: Before disconnecting a joint in a pipeline, you should ________________.
options: determine the size of the gasket
hang a bucket under the joint
have a first aid kit on hand
be sure no pressure exists in the line

text: The seat of a butterfly valve will most likely be   constructed of _____________.
On vessels using reach rods to manually control the tank valves, one turn of the handwheel on deck will produce how many turns of the valve stem at the valve?
The threaded pipe fitting called "street elbows" have _______________.
A pipe coupling is a fitting having ______________.
options: outside threads on one end and inside threads on one end

text: Which of the gasket materials and/or types listed is typically used on high pressure steam flange joints?
Which of the following statements is correct regarding  grades of pipe?
options: Stainless steel pipe is manufactured in four general grades.
The term "extra strong" is normally associated with schedule 160 steel pipe.
Copper pipe is manufactured in two common grades.
Brass pipe is manufactured in three common grades.

text: Gate valves should not be used for throttling as __________.
options: the pressure drop will be excessive
the installation of an equalizing line will be necessary
wire drawing of the disc will result

text: Allowances may be made for the expansion and contraction in piping by the use of expansion joints or _____________.
options: bends or loops in the line

text: Valves used in the machinery space piping systems, and constructed with threaded valve stems, must be ___________.
options: direction of opening and closing is unimportant union rings

text: Which of the valves listed should be used either in the  fully opened or the fully closed position?
Joints in pipelines must be properly aligned before they are connected because ______________.
options: excessive strain on the joints will result if they are  misaligned
the pipe will be completely blocked by even the slightest amount of misalignment
condensate accumulates rapidly when flanges are not properly aligned

text: Which of the following conditions can develop if a valve with a badly scored valve stem is repacked?
options: Leaking and premature failure of the packing.
The valve disk will become eroded.
The valve seat will sustain damage due to fretting.

text: Gate valves should not be used for throttling as __________.
options: the pressure drop will be excessive
the installation of an equalizing line will be necessary
cutting of the disc will result

text: A gate valve installed in a pipeline should be ____________.
options: used in either the fully closed or fully opened position
used to throttle or regulate the flow of liquid

text: On tankers using hydraulic tank valve actuation, which of the listed valve types has the highest tendency to develop leaks relative to the valve, and results in more frequent maintenance?
The handle of a butterfly valve must be ___________________.
options: parallel to the axis of flow when the valve is closed Over compression of the packing
forty-five degrees to the axis of flow when the valve is fully open Bending of the valve stem
either at 0° or 90° but never at any other angle regardless of flow rate Rapid corrosion of the valve disc
parallel to the flow when in the fully open position Cracking of gaskets upstream of the valve

text: Before making up a flanged joint, you should _____________.
options: be certain that the flanges line up squarely
cut grooves in the flange face with a chisel
heat the pipeline to expand the bolt holes
have a second spare gasket on hand

text: The valve which is most suited for regulating the flow  through a pipeline is a _____________.
If you were uncertain as to what type of gasket material to install in a pipeline, you should ____________.
options: turn the old gasket over and install it again
check the ship's plans or manufacturer's instructions
leave the old gasket in and cover it with Permatex®
make up the joint without a gasket until you can check with the chief

text: A swing check valve is used in a pipeline to _____________.
options: maintain a preset pressure on the line
closely regulate the amount of flow
relieve excessive pressure on the line
allow flow in one direction only

text: Piping is sized by its nominal _________________.
options: inside diameter regardless of other parameters
outside diameter regardless of other parameters
inside diameter from 3mm to 300mm, and over 300mm by its outside diameter
inside diameter from 3mm to 600mm, and over 600mm by its outside diameter

text: A "close nipple" is a piece of pipe that is _____________.
options: shorter than the nipple outside diameter

text: An electric motor driven (torque producing) remotely  controlled, valve actuator is installed on the high sea suction with the "red" indicator light illuminated. When the "open" push button is depressed, the "green" indicator light comes on momentarily an
options: Alternately depress the "close" and "open" buttons to get the valve open.
Manually engage the actuator handwheel and "break" the valve disk from its seat, then push the "open" button.
Secure power, tag out system and make repairs because the valve stem has sheared.
Secure the breaker to this device, then switch the motor leads and push the "open" button.

text: Which of the following statements concerning butterfly  valves is correct?
options: Special tools are required for lapping or grinding. Raised face flange
The butterfly valve should never be used in a freshwater system. Socket joint
To close the valve, it is only necessary to turn the handle a quarter of a turn. Walseal joint
It is impossible to throttle flow with a butterfly valve. Lap joint (van stone) flange

text: On tank vessels, which of the listed valve types is most commonly used in conjunction with hydraulically actuated controls?
When replacing a gasket in a six-bolt flanged joint, in what order should the bolts be tightened?
options: Each bolt should be installed and tightened in    consecutive order in the clockwise direction.
Each bolt should be installed and tightened in    consecutive order in the counterclockwise direction.
Install and tighten bolts 1, 3, 4, 2, 5, and 6, in that  order.
Install and tighten bolts in pairs which are opposite each other in the flange (1-4, 2-5, and 3-6).

text: The action necessary to transfer the steering control from the wheelhouse to local control in order to use the steering gear room trick wheel, is to _____________.
options: align the trick wheel to the rudder angle position
set the six-way control valve in the trick wheel position
open the power transfer switch before engaging the trick wheel
always place the rudder in the midship position to engage the trick wheel

text: If one hydraulic pump of an electro-hydraulic steering  unit fails, the vessel's steering can be initially and best maintained by using the _____________.
When the helm demands a 20‚ right rudder movement from an electro-hydraulic steering gear, which of the listed actions will be the FIRST action to happen when this rudder position is attained?
options: The steering service pump motor is stopped.
The follow-up gear takes the pump off stroke.
The cylinder relief valves bypass oil to the suction side of the pump.

text: Which of the devices listed, when used on an    electro-hydraulic steering gear, keeps the movement of the rudder closely in step with the steering wheel?
The follow-up gear on an electro-hydraulic steering gear ____________.
options: relieves excessive fluid pressure. cross connection to an alternate power supply
takes the pump off stroke when the desired rudder angle is attained. cycling all connected valves once

allows for rudder movement faster than the movement of the ship's wheel. opened against spring force, to have an energy storage system. That energy storage system shall be capable of ____________. operating any valve independently
closing the boiler quick-closing fuel valves

text: Which of the problems listed represents the major difficulty encountered with thermal expansion valve operation?
options: Lube oil passing through the system.
Moisture or foreign matter collecting at the valve seat and orifice.
Variable spring tension caused by the changing temperature.

text: A non-rising stem gate valve should _____________.
options: be completely opened with the handwheel locked hard at the end of the last opening turn
be opened to the end of the last opening turn, then rotate the handwheel in the closing direction by approximately 1/4 of a turn
be opened by 1/2 of the total number of turns available from full closed to full open
be opened as much as needed, regardless of how little the gate has been moved from its seat

text: As the designated rudder angle is being achieved, as a  result of the original command input, the steering gear  follow-up mechanism is ____________.
options: in motion, providing a null input
not in motion, thus providing a null input
in motion, providing an input to place the main pump on  maximum stroke
in motion, providing an input to place the main pump at null stroke

text: When there is no movement of the rams on an     electro-hydraulic steering gear, the tilting box of the  running pump is _____________.
options: on the purge and vent stroke

text: In an electro-hydraulic steering system, rudder movement is maintained in close synchronization with the steering wheel position by means of the _____________.
Your vessel is departing at 1800 hours on a voyage of more than 48 hours duration. Regulations require that the steering gear be examined and tested no earlier than ____________.
Which of the following statements concerning the operation of a single-acting hydraulic ram is correct?
options: Hydraulic force is applied simultaneously in two directions against the ram by directional ports.
The single-acting ram is both extended and retracted by  means of hydraulic force.
The single-acting ram is not retracted by means of hydraulic force.

text: Which of the following types of hydraulic pumps would be used in a steering system?
Air trapped in the hydraulic fluid of a steering system  should be indicated by ____________.
When the steering wheel is turned, oil is directed to the steering gear rams by _____________.
options: modulating the oil flow with the six-way valve
moving the automatic pressure differential valve
moving the receiving telemotor which regulates the two-way valve
varying the eccentricity of a floating ring or angle of a tilting box

text: A solenoid, direct-acting, three-position, spring-centered, directional control valve is used in a hydraulic system to control a linear actuator. With the actuator under load there is no movement. However, when the load is removed the actuator can be cycl
options: One or both of the centering springs has broken and the  spool has jammed in the valve body.
One or both of the solenoid coils has sustained an open.
The pump coupling is damaged and the pump is unable to  attain its rated speed of rotation.
The pump coupling has sheared and the motor is overspeeding.

text: On an electro-hydraulic steering gear, which of the listed devices will keep the rudder from overtraveling the bridge signal?
Air trapped in the hydraulic fluid of a steering system may be indicated by ____________.
options: a constantly occurring improper rudder response

text: In an electro-hydraulic steering system, rudder shock is limited by _____________.
When the steering gear is in operation, you should ________.
options: check hydraulic oil levels every hour
check for excessive oil leakage from rams
drain water from telemotor cylinders each watch

text: If a severe leak develops in the electro-hydraulic steering gear, which of the listed conditions could result?
options: Jamming of the six-way valve motion.

text: When air becomes trapped in the hydraulic fluid of a  steering system, the _____________.
While inspecting the steering system at sea, you should  check for _____________.
options: air bubbles in the sight glass
overtravel in the rudder angle indicator

text: The function of the hydraulic telemotor transmitter used in an electro-hydraulic steering gear system is to ___________.
options: transmit the rudder angle to the bridge indicator
prevent the control linkage from striking the stops when hard over
automatically purge all entrained air from the system
send hydraulic signals to the receiving unit

text: When the helm angle position is changed, the series ofcorresponding events of the steering gear will include__________.  I. length of time the steering gear pump remains on   stroke is proportional to helm angle input  II. system pressure being higher for
When the helm is turned on the navigation bridge, which of the listed actions will be the FIRST response in the  steering room on a ship equipped with an electro-hydraulic steering gear?
options: The pumps go to full stroke.
The six-way valve aligns itself with the running pump.
Both port and starboard cables are energized.
The synchronous receiver turns, duplicating the helm motion.

text: Most hydraulic steering gears are fitted with relief valves which _____________.
options: function when the rudder is amidships
relieve excess whip pressure from the hydraulic oil system
protect the piping assembly from external rudder shock

text: Rudder motion is prevented from exceeding the movement of the steering wheel by the _____________.
When the desired rudder angle is attained by a typical  double ram electro-hydraulic steering gear, the ___________.
options: ram relief valves bypass oil to stop rudder movement
six-way valve shifts to the neutral flow position
steering pump electric motor is de-energized by the transfer switch
follow-up gear takes the hydraulic pump off stroke

text: A power failure in the hydraulic system of a compact type steering gear would cause the rudder to _____________.
options: remain locked in its last position
move to the midship position automatically

text: Regulations require that an indicatinglight, located at the propulsion control station, beilluminated if there is an overload that would causeoverheating of the _________.
Dual electro-hydraulic steering units usually operate _____________.
options: with both pumps on line at the same time
only when the rudder is moved amidships

text: When the helm angle position is changed, the series ofcorresponding events of the steering gear will include__________.  I. rate of steering gear ram movement will be proportional to amount of helm angle input  II. degree of tilting plate (box) angle wi
In an emergency, the electro-hydraulic steering units can be directly controlled by the _____________.
In an electro-hydraulic steering system, damage due to  rudder shock is prevented by _____________.
The purging of air from an electro-hydraulic steering gear unit is necessary when _____________.
options: changing over to hand pump operation
the system has been filled with new oil
the rudder angle indicator does not match the helm position

text: Regulations require hydraulic steering gear systems to be equipped with a means of steadying the rudder in an emergency. This may be accomplished with  ____________.
options: a suitable arrangement of stop valves in the main piping
a positive arrangement for stopping the rudder before the rudder stops are reached
a suitable arrangement of block and tackle powered by  winches
buffer arrangements to relieve the gear from shocks to the rudder

text: Regulations make certain requirementsregarding overcurrent protection. Steering gear feeder circuits shall be protected only by _____________.
options: a circuit breaker with instantaneous trip

text: Of the equipment listed below, which equipment must be  tested NOT more than 12 hours prior to getting underway from a  port when the voyage is to be of 48 hours or greater duration?
A vertical shaft having a rudder attached to its lower end and having a yoke, quadrant, or tiller fitted to its upper portion by which it may be turned, is the _________________.
Under normal operating conditions, the rudder is   hydraulically locked unless _____________.
options: the manual trick wheel is engaged for steering
the variable stroke pump is off stroke
a rudder order is given by the control system
an electric power system failure occurs at the steering gear

text: Regulations require that prior to departure  on a three-day voyage, the steering gear, whistle, and  communications system between the bridge and engine room must be tested prior to departure no earlier than _____.
Hydraulic pumps most commonly used in steering systems are of the ____________.
While inspecting the steering gear at sea, you should check for _____________.
options: accuracy of the rudder angle indicator

text: Air trapped in the hydraulic fluid of a steering system  would be indicated by _____________.
options: hammering noises in the equipment or transmission lines

text: According to Regulations ,which of the following statements is correct regarding the steering  apparatus requirements for a vessel over 75 metres in length?
options: Hydraulic structural rudder stops are mandatory.
On hydraulic type steering gears, a suitable arrangement of check valves in the main piping system may be considered as a means of steadying the rudder.
A separate auxiliary means of steering is not required  where the main gear is of the dual power hydraulic type, having two independent pumps and connections.

text: Electric and electro-hydraulic steering gear motors are required by Regulations to be   _____________.
options: protected by a circuit breaker and a thermal overload device
provided with a running motor overcurrent protection device
served by a single two conductor cable

text: Hydraulically, servo-operated, automatic, change over  valves, utilized in a two ram hydraulic steering gear, serve to ____________.
options: allow an alternate main pump to start in the fully loaded condition thus developing immediate full torque
All internal vessel power control communications and alarms

text: Which of the listed systems related to an engineer's signal alarm panel is required to be indicated by a continuously illuminated light while in operation?
options: Port or starboard steering gear motor running
No. 1 diesel generator low lube oil pressure

text: An AC synchronous transmission type remote steering control system consists of a ______________.
options: transmitter at the steering station and a receiver at the steering engine
reversible motor at the steering station geared to the  steering pumps
small induction motor on the bridge which controls the  rudder through pulleys
variable displacement hydraulic pump driven by a reversible DC motor

text: According to Regulations , a power  driven auxiliary steering gear for a vessel capable of a 12 knot service speed, must be able to meet the rudder movement requirements at which of the minimum vessel speeds listed below?
Regulations  require that electric andelectro-hydraulic steering gear motors shall be   _____________.
options: served by two electric power feeder circuits
provided with a motor running overcurrent protection device
protected by a circuit breaker and a thermal overload device
served by a single two conductor cable

text: The follow-up gear on an electro-hydraulic steering gear _____________.
options: takes the pump off stroke when the desired rudder angle is attained
synchronizes wheel position with the rudder position
returns the rudder to midposition when the wheel is released

text: In an electro-hydraulic steering gear system, when will the variable displacement pump be placed on stroke?
options: When the helm is at any angle other than amidships.
When the six-way valve is opened.
When the ram relief valves lift.
When the rudder angle is different from the position of the helm.

text: In a radial piston pump, reversal and control of fluid flow are accomplished by moving the _____________.
Dryseal threads, typically used for tubing to pipe   connectors, and threaded piping in hydraulic systems are ___________.
options: the same as National Pipe threads idle by cross pressure flow
prevent both units from operating simultaneously which could result in doubling the flow of oil and pressure leading to over pressurization of the system

text: Rotation of the steering wheel on the navigation bridge  initiates oil pressure being applied to the steering gear rams by _____________.
options: regulating the oil flow with the six-way valve
moving the receiving telemotor which regulates the two-way valve
varying the angle of a tilting box or eccentricity of a  floating ring

text: The purpose of the six-way valve used in an     electro-hydraulic steering system is to _____________.
options: parallel rudder motion to the steering wheel
take the pump off stroke when desired rudder angle is  attained
redirect hydraulic fluid flow when changing over pumps
ensure positive contact between the Rapson slide and the rudder post

text: A power-driven auxiliary steering gear is required by Regulations to be capable of putting the  rudder over from 15‚ on one side to 15‚ on the other side with the vessel running ahead. For a 20 knot vessel, this test must be carried out at a speed of ____
Rudder position is shown on the bridge by the _____________.
In an electro-hydraulic steering gear, any change in  relative position between the synchronous receiver and the follow-up gear will result in _____________.
options: the pump going to full stroke
driving the rams up against the stops
a corresponding slowing or increasing of the pumped flow rate

text: The usual number of single-acting pistons used in a   variable stroke axial-piston pump is _____________.
The size of flexible hose used in a hydraulic system is  indicated by _____________.
options: the inside diameter of the tube with the trough of the opposing thread
different than NPT as the flanks of the matching threads are in contact with the opposing threads
the same as National Fine threads

text: To charge a bladder type hydraulic accumulator ____________.
options: remove all hydraulic system pressure and bring the pneumatic pressure to the accumulator preload pressure
remove all hydraulic system pressure and bring the pneumatic pressure to the system's design pressure
increase the pneumatic pressure until the hydraulic system reaches its design pressure
allow the accumulator to completely fill with gas charge at atmospheric pressure, shut off the air chamber, and add  hydraulic fluid until proper pressure is reached

text: In application, which of the listed sealing devices is most similar to an O-ring?
The purpose of an accumulator in a hydraulic system is to ______________.
options: collect any dirt in the system
collect fluid from any small leak
preheat the fluid during cold weather
store potential energy in the form of hydraulic fluid under pressure in the system

text: In which of the listed hydraulic system components could an O-ring seal be satisfactorily used in providing a seal?
When installing a hydraulic hose, which of the following precautions should be taken?
options: The hose should not be twisted.
The hose should be protected with a sleeve if it is subjected to rubbing.
There should be some slack in the hose.

text: A reservoir, as used in hydraulic systems aboard ship, is used to store hydraulic oil. Another function is to  _____________.
options: maintain the stored oil under pressure
act as a base or foundation for the power unit
eliminate pressure surges in the system

text: Strainers are commonly used in hydraulic systems to ___________.
options: protect the pump from fine soluble contaminants
prevent solid particles from entering the pump
prevent solid particles from entering the filter

text: An O-ring seal in a hydraulic system will begin to leak when it has lost its interference fit due to ____________.
One function provided by a hydraulic accumulator is to_________.
options: provide an area where air can separate from the oil
provide an area to separate solid contaminants from the oil
a color code on the armor
the thickness of the tube wall

text: Before doing any work on a hydraulic system equipped with accumulators, you should _____________.
options: drain the accumulators and purge with oxygen
bleed off all stored energy from the accumulators
completely charge the accumulators to prevent system energy loss
pump the hydraulic fluid into the accumulators to prevent fluid loss

text: As a rule of thumb, when pressure is applied to hydraulic oil, the oil will _____________.
options: not be compressed, as liquids are not compressible
reduce in volume by 1% for every 7bar increment
increase in volume by 1% for every 70bar increment
reduce in volume by 1/2% for every 70bar increment

text: Obstructed suction passages in the casing or pintle of a radial piston hydraulic pump will cause the _____________.
options: pump return and discharge pressures to equalize
pump discharge flow to drop to zero

text: Before performing any maintenance on a hydraulic system  storing energy in an accumulator, you should _____________.
options: pressurize the system to test for leaks
bleed off all pressure within the system
operate the machine until it reaches normal temperature
disconnect the pump pressure control switch

text: To convert a vane type hydraulic pump to a hydraulic motor, which of the following would have to be done?
options: Install an enlarged control ring around the rotor.
Provide small springs between the vanes and the base of the vane slots.
Provide one additional slot and vane.

text: The amount of the cushioning effect developed within a  hydraulic cylinder is determined by the ____________.
options: position of the directional port in the cushion cavity
adjustment of the cushion cavity check valve
design shape of the cylinder ends
setting of the cushioning adjustment needle valve

text: A hydraulic system directional control valve fitted with "detent" will _____________.
options: have an infinite number of valve positions
usually be shifted into three specific positions
be able to be varied through out the travel of the valve spool
have an offset, directional control only

text: Purging air from a hydraulic system is necessary when _______________.
options: adding small amounts of oil to the system
the system has been drained and then filled with new oil
the system has been idle for a long period of time

text: Energy losses occurring in a hydraulic system are ultimately absorbed by the _____________.
options: absorb shocks occurring in the system

text: The movement of heat within a fluid, caused by the   application of thermal energy, is called ___________.
The delivery rate of an axial-piston hydraulic pump is  controlled by varying the position of the ____________.
If the pump in a hydraulic system produces a low rumbling noise while in operation, this is a probable indication of _______________.
If the relief valve on the discharge side of a hydraulic pump lifts, the cause could be _____________.
options: a low load on the unit
a blockage in the line between the pump and hydraulic motor
the hydraulic motor turning too fast

text: Return lines in a non-pressurized hydraulic system   reservoir should enter the tank well below the fluid surface level to _____________.
Overheating of a hydraulic system may be a result of ______.
options: changing pump discharge pressure in response to normal load variations
continued slow recirculation of the oil

text: An orifice-check valve placed in a hydraulic system is used to ______________.
options: regulate the fluid flow in either direction
restrict movement of hydraulic fluid in one direction, but allow free movement in the other direction
allow free movement of hydraulic fluid in both directions
allow a restricted fluid flow in one direction only

text: In a typical hydraulic system, a baffle is installed in thereservoir to _______.  I. insure proper lubrication of the hydraulic pump  II. assist in the removal of Solid contaminants   entrained in the returning oil
As a general rule of thumb, the reservoir used in a   hydraulic system should have a capacity, when at the normal level, approximately equal to ____________.
options: two to three times the normal flow rate through the system
the flow rate through the system
ten times the flow rate through the system
the pump gpm fluid as friction

text: How can the chance of contaminating hydraulic fluid be  decreased when working on hydraulic systems?
options: Clean the fittings before they are disconnected.
Place drip pans under leaky fittings.
Seal any cracks in lines with Permatex.
Coat all threads with graphite oil.

text: If a hydraulic pump is producing a noisy whine when in  operation, the cause may be ____________.
options: an air leak in the pump suction line above the oil level in the reservoir
low viscosity in the hydraulic fluid
an oil leak across the pump shaft packing
due to the wrong direction of rotation of the hydraulic  motor

text: Which of the listed conditions will occur if the discharge pressure of a rotary pump is increased from the designed 3.50 kg/cm2 to 21.0 kg/cm2, while maintaining the same RPM?
options: The pump capacity will be decreased.
The pump capacity will be increased.
Internal liquid slippage will be reduced.

text: Cavitation in a hydraulic pump is indicated by noisy pump operation and can be caused by a/an _____________.
options: high fluid level in the reservoir
excessive discharge pressure from the pump
clogged suction strainer in the reservoir

text: An internal bypass is provided on some hydraulic system  suction strainers to help reduce the possibility of   _______________.
A reservoir is used in a hydraulic system to _____________.
options: store fluid until required by the system
provide a place for air to separate out of the fluid
provide a place for contaminants to settle out

text: Hydraulic system reservoirs should be maintained at recommended normal levels to reduce ___________.

In a typical hydraulic system, a baffle is installed in thereservoir to _______.  I. provide a critical reduction in free surface   effect hydraulic pump    II. assist in retaining the hydraulic oil to provide heat removal
Which of the following problems occurring in a hydraulic system can be caused by the use of an oil having a viscosity lower than specified?
options: Fast response and hunting Offset, two position valve

text: A hydraulic fluid flow control circuit, controlling linear actuator speed, with the pump operating below maximum  operating pressure is known as the ____________.
The determining factor for the strength of a flexible rubber hydraulic hose is the _____________.
When normal operating pressure is applied to the hydraulic oil in a high-pressure system, the oil ____________.
Which of the listed pressure-control valves would be used in a hydraulic system to temporarily divert some, or all of the pump discharge until the additional flow was required?
Which of the devices listed would be considered a hydraulic system directional control valve?
Winch gears must be maintained in proper alignment to  prevent _____________.
A mooring winch should be equipped with mechanical brakes capable of holding ________________.
options: half the breaking strength of the mooring line
the full breaking strength of the mooring line
the maximum expected tension of the mooring line
50% over the working tension of the mooring line

text: The delivery rate of a variable stroke axial piston   hydraulic pump is controlled by varying the position of the ______________.
Directional control valves used in hydraulic systems may be positioned ____________.
options: manually To regulate actuator speed through the entire stroke length.
mechanically To slow the action of the piston preventing shock   and damage due to hammering effects.
electrically To allow the pump to temporarily operate at a pressure 10% above the relief valve setting without lifting the relief valve.
all of the above All of the above.

text: A hydraulic fluid flow control circuit, controlling   linear actuator speed during extension, with the pump  operating at system pressure, is known as a ____________.
The by-products of oxidation, as a result of water   contamination of hydraulic oil, are generally ____________.
options: harmless and have no effect on system components

text: If a radial piston hydraulic pump fails to deliver rated fluid volume, the cause can be _______________.
An axial piston pump differs from a radial piston pump as the pistons of an axial piston pump are positioned   _____________.
options: parallel to each other and to the shaft
parallel to each other but at a right angle to the shaft
at an angle to each other and to the shaft

text: The speed of a radial piston hydraulic MOTOR is controlled by varying the _________.
options: amount of cylinder block offset with respect to the rotor
fluid flow rate discharged to the motor
length of the motor piston stroke on the power cycle
pintle discharge rate to the suction side of the pump

text: A check run on a hydraulic anchor windlass during long  periods of inactivity should be carried out to ____________.
options: prevent chemical breakdown of hydraulic fluid
remove condensation from the fluid reservoir
prevent the anchor from seizing in the hawsepipe
renew the internal coating of lubrication

text: The torque rating of a fluid power motor is expressed in _______________.
options: RPMs under a given load condition
Newton-metre per rate of input flow

text: When changing to a fire resistant hydraulic fluid in a  system, it is important to check the compatibility of the new fluid with the system's _____________.
The baffle plate used in the reservoir of a hydraulic system serves to _____________.
options: reduce fluid turbulence at the pump suction
minimize the amount of contamination deposited from return lines entering the system
minimize the entrance of entrained air through the pump  inlet line

text: Which of the valves listed is not considered to be a  hydraulic system directional control valve?
In a typical hydraulic system, return lines to thereservoir are ___________.  I. terminated at or just above the normal level  II. placed as far from the pump suction as possible
Which of the following devices is not considered to be a pressure vessel?
If an anchor windlass has been idle for some time, you  should _____________.
A device incorporating a variable orifice placed in series with a check valve in a hydraulic system, is used to  ________________.
options: allow free movement of hydraulic fluid in both directions
allow fluid flow in one direction only
allow throttled fluid flow in one direction only
restrict hydraulic fluid flow in both directions

text: Hydraulic machinery failures are commonly caused by contamination of the hydraulic fluid and _________.
Prolonged operation of a hydraulic pump in a cavitating condition can cause _____________.
options: the hydraulic fluid to become overheated
the fluid motor to become overloaded
a wide variation in fluid pressure surges

text: When renewing sections of pipe in a hydraulic system, the nominal pipe size of the piping always indicates the  ______________.
Return lines to hydraulic systems reservoirs should________.
options: end the return line as far as practicable from the pump suction
end the return line as close to the pump suction as  possible
provide a "P" or "S" trap in the return line as close to the reservoir as possible to trap sediment from entering the tank
connect the return lines directly to the cleanout and inspection plates to limit the number of openings on the reservoir

text: The delivery rate of an axial piston hydraulic pump is  controlled by varying the position of the _____________.
Energy imparted to the hydraulic fluid in an operating  hydraulic system is stored in the ____________.
Proper internal lubrication of a hydraulic anchor windlass left idle for extended periods can be accomplished by  _______________.
options: performing a check run on the unit at regular intervals
testing the hydraulic fluid for proper pH
checking the reservoir for proper level

text: Which of the devices listed is considered to be a hydraulic system directional control valve?
Which of the following is the most common type of valving element used in hydraulic system directional control valves?
Water allowed to accumulate in the hydraulic system oil will cause an oxidation process producing by-products which are normally _______________.
The purpose of a restrictor valve, as it is used in a  hydraulic hatch cover system, is to _____________.
options: prevent oil backflow to the actuators
prevent the hydraulic pump from overheating
control the speed of the hatch cover movement while closing
restrict the oil supply to the hatch covers not in use

text: The output volume of a positive fixed displacement pump can be changed only by _______________.
options: changing the angle of the tilting plate
changing the speed of the pump
moving the slide block and rotor

text: Which of the following problems will occur if the internal drain at either end of a hydraulic, two-way, spool-type  directional control valve cylinder were to become plugged?
options: The reservoir would become vapor bound.
The valve would be placed in hydraulic lock.
The spring loaded relief ports would open.
The buffering chambers would be unable to function.

text: If dirt is allowed to contaminate the sump of a hydraulic deck crane, which of the following problems will occur?
options: All the seals in the hydraulic lines will immediately  blow out.
The sheathing on the hydraulic lines will fracture.
The lifting capacity of the crane will be immediately  reduced by 70%.
The internal parts of the pump and hydraulic motor will wear excessively.

text: A gradual decrease in the discharge pressure of an operating hydraulic pump can be caused by _____________.
options: the four-way control valve failing to shift
the bleeder valve sticking in the open position

text: If you are given the job of adding hydraulic fluid to a  mooring winch, and are not certain as to the type of fluid to use, you should _____________.
options: add fluid that is the same color as the fluid in the  reservoir
add turbine oil because it is always a good substitute
add any oil that has the same viscosity as the hydraulic fluid
check the winch manufacturer's instruction book

text: The oil in a cargo winch gear box should be sampled   periodically to _____________.
options: prevent the gear box from leaking
prevent the oil from becoming inflammable
make sure it has not become contaminated
make sure the motor bearings are lubricated

text: Which of the valves listed is NOT considered to be a  hydraulic system directional control valve?
The pressure of an operating hydraulic system, as indicated by a pressure gauge, is a result of the fluid flow   overcoming _________________.
options: the load applied to the system

text: Which of the listed pressure control valves would be used to permit the completion of one action of a hydraulic system before a second action would be permitted?
Which of the following statements describes the functions of a reservoir used in a hydraulic system?
When a linear actuator (cylinder) is being retracted without an applied load, the pressure on the oil leaving the "cap end" will be __________.
options: inversely proportional to the speed of retraction
inversely proportional to the flow rate at the rod end
increased as the speed of retraction increases

text: Which characteristic or condition will have the greatest effect on increasing a hydraulic oil's viscosity?
The rubber bladder or separator bag installed in a hydraulic accumulator should only be filled with __________.
The component which is used to thoroughly separate small, fine, dust-like particulate contamination from hydraulic fluid is a/an _____________.
Hydraulic system reservoirs are often fitted with a combined filler/breather cap. If the breather element becomes  fouled, the _______________.
options: reservoir will be subjected to a partial vacuum
flow through the return lines will be stopped
actuator response time will be halved

text: A filter used in a multi-operation hydraulic system would most likely be located ______________.
options: between the pump and the directional control valves
between the control valves and the actuators

text: Flexible hose under pressure in a hydraulic system will _________________.
options: tend to twist about its long axis
expand in length and in diameter
contract in length and expand in diameter
flex at right angles to the applied pressure

text: Which of the listed pressure control valves would be used to establish the maximum operating pressure of a hydraulic  system?
Which of the listed pressure-control valves is used in a hydraulic system to prevent the stray movements of a  vertical load until required?
Hydraulic system tubing should be anchored every three or four feet to prevent ____________.
options: expansion and contraction of the tubing
tube fitting leaks from vibration and pressure surges
tube flexing at right angles to the applied fluid pressure

text: If you install a new hydraulic hose in a hydraulic system, the hose must be long enough to allow for contraction to prevent _______________.
options: friction in other areas of the hydraulic system

text: Sluggish response or action of the hydraulic actuators may be a result of _____________.
options: reservoir level being maintained two inches above normal

text: Flanged joints in hydraulic system piping are commonly made leak proof by _______________.
options: seal welding the mating flange surfaces
leather wipers installed with the flange seals
enclosing the flanged connection in a container

text: The clutch band of a constant tensioning mooring winch must be set up tight enough to drive the winch drum and should slip only when _____________.
options: excessive loads are placed on the winch
minimum pull is being exerted by the winch
automatic operation of the winch is desired
wire is being retrieved at the maximum rate

text: New piping and tubing to be installed in a hydraulic system can be safely degreased by using _________.

For the various sizes of tubing and wall thickness used in a hydraulic system, the inside diameter can be determined if it is remembered that the inside diameter equals the outside diameter less _____________.
When the tilting box of a variable stroke axial-piston pump is perpendicular to the pump shaft, which of the following conditions will exist?
The component used in a hydraulic system to store potential energy in the form of hydraulic fluid under pressure, is the _______________.
A bent axis, variable stroke, axial piston pump produces a greater capacity than a comparable tilting box-type axial piston pump, because the ___________.
options: discharge/return line diameter are arranged differently

text: A hydraulic accumulator, used in a hydraulic system , is designed to _________________.
options: act as the main fluid reservoir
provide the only means of overpressure relief
act as the singular source of fluid replenishment to a  system

text: A valve installed in a hydraulic system to manipulate the reversal of fluid flow, is called a _____________.
Compensated flow control, or constant flow valves are used in hydraulic systems to _____________.
options: compensate for major leaks in the system
allow for changes in pressure and temperature within the system

text: Hydraulic machinery failures are commonly caused by misalignment of the system components and by _____________.
One function of a replenishing pump installed in many  pressure- closed hydraulic systems, is to supply fluid flow to ____________.
The portion of a hydraulic hose that determines its overall strength, is the _____________.
An axial piston, variable stroke pump is used in a vessel's hydraulic steering gear. Under pressure, oil continually leaks out from between the valve plate/ cylinder barrel and will ___________.
options: cause loss of hydraulic oil from the system
result in extreme damage to the pump
cause damage to the pump if not continually drained from the pump casing
result in the system high side pressure to substantially drop off

text: Which of the following problems may be encountered by using an oil having a viscosity higher than that specified for an operating hydraulic system?
The hydraulic oil most likely to thin out when hot and thick when cold would have a viscosity index of _________________.
Both the direction of flow and fluid flow rate of a variable displacement radial piston pump are determined by the  relative positions of the ____________.
What is the minimum permissible flashpoint of hydraulic  fluid used in a system which operates at 17.6 kg/cm2(250 psi)?
A valve installed in a hydraulic system to control the  reversal of fluid flow is called a _____________.
Plug valves installed in hydraulic systems are most suitable for use as _____________.
In the design of hydraulic piping and equipment    consideration is given to minimize turbulence in the  hydraulic fluid, as this will cause ______________.
Setting the relief valve opening pressure in a hydraulic system lower than the required operating pressure will  result in ________________.
options: the main system accumulators under all operating conditions

text: Leakage of hydraulic fluid from around the shaft of a  hydraulic motor may be caused by _____________.
options: high level in the oil sump

text: If the pump for a hydraulic anchor windlass is overheating, the cause may be _____________.
options: too low of a tilting box angle

text: Which of the valves listed is NOT considered to be a  hydraulic system directional control valve?
As a general rule of thumb, the reservoir used in a   hydraulic system should have a capacity, when at the normal level, approximately equal to ____________.
options: two to three times the normal flow rate through the system
the flow rate through the system
ten times the flow rate through the system
the number of gallons a pump can circulate in one minute.

text: Pressure in an operating hydraulic system is developed ________________.
options: only by the pump as its primary function
by resistance to the fluid flow through the system
by the thermal input to the system's fluid
solely by the charge applied by the accumulators

text: If you install a hydraulic hose on a unit and fail to leave sufficient room for expansion, which of the following  problems will develop?
options: The hose may pull loose from its fittings.
The components connected by the hose will be damaged.
The hydraulic unit will fail to acquire any power.
The hydraulic fluid will overheat and breakdown.

text: While riding at anchor, the anchor chain should be secured by the ____________.
If a hydraulic pump is overheating, the cause may be ____________.
options: excessive internal slippage in the pump
low discharge pressure and fluid flow
excessive fluid level in the hydraulic reservoir
operation of the pump at 100% efficiency

text: Increased internal leakage, in addition to poor hydraulic system response, and inadequate lubrication, is the result of ____________.
options: excessively worn actuator-piston packing overheating of the system
broken directional control valve centering springs overspeeding of the hydraulic pump
low hydraulic oil viscosity extended system life

text: Which problems can occur if the brake band lining of a  wildcat brake becomes excessively worn?
options: The brake's effectiveness will be reduced.

text: An improperly maintained filter used in a hydraulic system can _____________.
options: reduce or stop the output action of the actuator
allow all metal particles to enter the power unit

text: Overheating of the hydraulic fluid in an electro-hydraulic anchor windlass can result from a/an ____________.
options: low fluid level in the reservoir
low fluid viscosity around the shaft seal
high oil level in the sump

text: Hydraulic systems typically operated for intermittent service do not require the use of oil coolers and would include all of the following except _________?
options: Internal combustion engine hydraulic starter system

text: In which of the listed hydraulic systems will the   installation of an oil cooler be necessary?
options: Internal combustion engine hydraulic starter system

text: When new piping sections have been fabricated for   installation in a hydraulic system, prior to installation the piping should be _____________.
options: descaled by using a pickling solution
hydrostatically tested to 100% of maximum working pressure

text: If the pump for a hydraulic anchor windlass is over heating, the cause may be ______________.
options: too low of a tilting box angle

text: When normal operating pressure is applied to the hydraulic oil used in a high-pressure system, the oil _______________.
At high pressures, an O-ring has a tendency to extrude into the clearance space between the mating parts of a hydraulic actuator. Which of the listed components is used to prevent extrusion?
options: Double seal ring A spring at one spool end has broken and jammed, preventing the spool from shifting.
Lathe cut ring The "extend" solenoid coil has developed an open.
Backup ring The pump coupling is damaged and pump is unable to turn at its required speed.
Static washer Any one of the above will cause the actuator to not move.

text: Air entrained in the hydraulic fluid, or trapped in an  actuator will cause the actuators to move with a jerky  motion. This action is a result of ________________.
options: the trapped air being compressed to a pressure above the maximum pump discharge pressure, then re-expanding and lowering in pressure after the actuator moves
the cyclic expansion and contraction of air due to the  motion of the actuator
air being capable of moving loads more effectively than a liquid
air providing better lubrication of internal components, found in Hydraulic Systems, than hydraulic fluids

text: Which of the following precautions should be observed  concerning the introduction of a fire resistant fluid into a hydraulic system?
options: Deterioration of paints, seals, metals, and electrical  insulation may occur.
Fluid viscosity always increases as a normal result of its use.
Decreased wear rates of components is an advantage of its use.
Only chemically active filters may be used.

text: Which of the listed components of a hydraulic system would enable the pump to be temporarily shutdown, and yet still provide an instantaneous source of hydraulic force?
When the hydraulic control lever for a deck winch is placed in the neutral or off position, the spring set brake on the fluid motor drive shaft is _____________.
options: engaged by spring action and only released by hydraulic  pressure
released by spring action and hydraulically locks the winch when the drum ceases rotating
engaged by spring action and is insured to be locked in  place by hydraulic pressure
