text: A tank or compartment is "gas free" when there is an absence of dangerous concentrations of _____________.
options: flammable or toxic gases all-purpose nozzle attached

text: Small oil spills on deck may be prevented from entering the water by _____________.
options: placing floating booms alongside the ship

text: To effectively treat a person suffering from heat   exhaustion, you should ____________.
options: put him in a tub of ice water
give him sips of cool saline solution

text: During the towing of a survival craft, a lookout should be on station to ______________.
options: release the towline in an emergency
check water level in the bilge

text: When retrieving the survival craft, the winch operator  should stop the winch and check ____________.
options: that all personnel are seated in the craft
that the cable has not jumped any grooves on the drum
which way the wind is blowing
the hydraulic fluid level before lifting

text: Releasing oil from the sea anchor of a survival craft may __________.
options: keep the propeller from being fouled
help calm spray given off from the waves in the vicinity of the craft
increase the holding power of the sea anchor

text: The air cylinder bottles in the survival craft should be refilled with ________________.
Fire hose should always be stowed at the fire station in the hose rack _____________.
options: after the hose has been completely drained
with the nozzle in the solid stream position
so the hose can be connected quickly if a fire occurs
so the nozzle is protected by the hose

text: Which of the listed materials would be considered as a  burning class "C" fire?
For the best extinguishing effect, the discharge from a dry chemical type fire extinguisher should be directed at the _____________.
options: top and sides of a fire

text: Which of the listed procedures should be followed when  individuals are rescued in cold climates and suffer from hypothermia?
options: Get them to a hot room
Immerse them in a warm bath (104°F)

text: The explosive range of benzene is 1.4% to 8% by volume in air. If you sample an empty tank that had contained benzene and obtained a reading of 50% L.E.L. on a combustible gas indicator, then the _____________.
options: vapor/air mixture is too lean to burn
vapor concentration at the sample point is 0.7%
vapor/air mixture is not within the flammable range

text: A fire may be spread by which of the following means?
options: Conduction of heat to adjacent surfaces

text: The designation "Not safe for men - Not safe for fire" means _____________.
options: the gas or oxygen concentrations within a compartment is not within permissible limits
the compartment was not tested due to the presence of  ballast slops or bunkers

text: The flammable limits of kerosene are 0.7% to 6.0% by volume in air. The combustible gas indicator reading is 50% of the lower explosive level. The flammable vapor concentration at the sample point is _____________.
When compared to low-expansion foam, a high-expansion foam will ____________.
Heat exhaustion is caused by excessive _______________.
options: loss of water and salt from the body
intake of water when working or exercising

text: Hose spanner wrenches should be used to connect ___________.
options: hose spreaders for attaching new fittings

text: Which of the following statements defines the purpose of the lifeboat gravity davit limit switch?
options: To cut off power when the davits hit the track safety stops.
To stop the davits from going too fast.
To cut off power when the davits are about 30cms from the track safety stops.

text: Which of the following should NOT be done for a person who has fainted?
options: Get the person in the open air.
Lay the person with the head higher than the feet.
Splash cold water in the face.

text: A fire hose is subject to damage if the swivel on the female coupling is lubricated with ____________.
When compared to a high-expansion foam, a low-expansion foam will ____________.
A crew member suffering from generalized hypothermia should be given ____________.
Which of the conditions listed make it difficult to   indefinitely maintain fire hoses?
options: Gasoline and oils act to dissolve the rubber or cement which holds the inner jacket and lining together. 0.0035
Heat causes the rubber lining to harden and crack. 0.0265
Moisture will cause mildew to form on the jacket which  destroys the cotton fiber.

text: Which of the listed classes of fire would apply to a main switchboard fire?
Lifejackets should be stowed in ____________.
You have abandoned ship and are in charge of a liferaft or lifeboat. How much water per day should you permit each person after the first 24 hours?
An inflatable life raft has inflated on top of you in an upside down position. What should you do next?
options: Wait for others to lift the raft off of you.
Dive down to prevent your lifejacket from fouling as you come out.
Pull yourself out from under the raft in a face up position to keep your lifejacket clear of the raft.
You should remove your lifejacket before attempting to right an inflatable raft.

text: A galley grease fire would be classified as ___________.
options: Class A Fresh water may be obtained from fish.
Class B Lifeboat covers or canopies should be washed with rain  before drinking water is collected.
Class C Fresh water may be collected from condensation inside the liferaft.
Class D Freshwater cannot be produced from standing seawater.

text: Foam is effective in combating which of the listed classes of fire?
When jumping into water upon which there is an oil fire, you should ____________.
options: break the water surface with your hands when diving   head first
use your hands to hold your knees to your chest
cover your eyes with one hand while pinching your nose shut and covering your mouth with the other
enter the water at the bow or stern, on the windward side of the vessel

text: Which one of the following statements is true concerning the gas free status of a compartment?
options: The gas free status applies to the conditions of the  compartment at the time of the inspection.
The gas free status applies for the duration of the work inside the compartment.
The gas free status applies for the 24 hour period following the inspection.
The gas free status applies as long as the air temperature inside the compartment remains at or below 25 degrees  Celsius.

text: For the safety of personnel working with fire hoses, fire pumps are fitted with a/an _____________.
options: butterfly valve on the discharge side
air priming valve on the impeller housing
pressure gage and relief valve on the discharge side

text: Inflatable liferafts are provided with ____________.

One of the common hazards onboard a vessel is represented by the accumulation of harmful gases in tanks, holds, etc.  Which of the following gases is the most dangerous to  personnel safety in confined atmospheres, as it is absorbed by the blood 300 times
Foam is a very effective smothering agent, in addition ____________.
options: it provides cooling as a secondary effect
works well on extinguishing electrical fires
can be used to combat combustible metal fires

text: Which of the following conditions is an important   consideration to remember when using a combustible gas  indicator? ____________.

The flammable limits of Benzene are 1.4% to 8% by volume in air. If a combustible gas indicator is used to sample a tank that contains a Benzene vapor/air mixture of 0.7%  Benzene vapor by volume in air, which of the listed   indications will be observed
options: The meter will deflect to the 70% explosive position.
The meter will deflect to the 50% explosive position.
The meter will deflect full scale and return to zero.
The meter will be unable to detect a concentration of 0.7% Benzene vapor in air.

text: Which of the listed classes of fire would most likely occur in the engine room of a vessel?
One of the methods NOT usually allowed in cleaning up oil spills in the water is ____________.
Handholds or straps on the underside of an inflatable  liferaft are provided ____________.
options: to right the raft if it capsizes
to carry the raft around on deck
for crewmen to hang on to
to hang the raft for drying

text: Fires involving combustible metals are classified as ______.
Which of the following should not be used as a treatment for a person who has received a head injury and is groggy or unconscious?
A fire involving aluminum powder would be a class _________.
If for any reason it is necessary to abandon ship while far at sea, it is important for the crew members to ___________.
options: They do not work properly in oxygen deficient atmospheres.
Any movement of the needle indicates an unsafe condition specifically related to the toxicity of petroleum vapors present.

text: What danger to personnel exists when a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher is discharged in a small enclosed space?
To keep injured survivors warm in the water, after   abandoning ship, they should ____________.
options: be placed in the middle of a small circle formed by the  other survivors in the water
float on their backs with their arms extended for maximum exposure to the air
remove their lifejackets and hold on to the uninjured  survivors
sip water at fifteen minute intervals

text: A burning mattress is considered as which of the following classes of fire?
If a person gets something in his eye and you observe that it is not embedded, you can ____________.
options: get him to rub his eye until the object is gone
remove it with a match or toothpick
remove it with a piece of dry sterilized cotton rag
remove it with a moist cotton-tipped applicator

text: Records of garbage disposal are required to be maintained _________.
options: until the end of each voyage
for a minimum of one year
for a minimum of two years

text: According to the  Pollution Prevention   Regulations , which of the following statements is correct regarding the fuel oil containment around loading manifolds?
options: They may be fixed or portable, depending upon the age of the vessel.
The containment drains must have quick-closing valves to be closed in the event of leakage or failure of any part of the transfer connection.
All containment drains must lead to a common fixed drain tank.
Containment drains are prohibited from leading to a common tank.

text: You are in the mess room when a shipmate suddenly begins to choke, is unable to speak and starts to turn blue. Which of the following actions should you take?
options: Administer abdominal thrust in an effort to clear the  airway.
Make the victim lie down with the feet elevated to get blood to the brain.
Do nothing until the victim becomes unconscious.

text: If someone suffers a heart attack and has ceased breathing, you should ___________. rescued
options: get away from the area because sharks will be attracted to the vessel
immediately head for the nearest land
remain together in the area because rescuers will start  searching at the vessel's last known position

text: Inflatable liferafts are provided with a ____________.
Shipboard oil pollution emergency plans must be reviewed ___________.
options: annually by the owner and a letter sumitted six months prior to expiration
only once every five years and a letter submitted six months prior to expiration
and the entire plan resubmitted for approval once every five years, six months prior to expiration
annually by the owner and submit a letter to the Administration within one month of the anniversary date of the plan  approval

text: The external flotation bladder of an immersion suit should be inflated ____________.
options: only after two hours in the water
only after four hours in the water

text: You have just extinguished an oil fire on the floor   plates of the engine room with a  CO2  extinguisher. Which of the dangers listed should be prepared for next?
options: Complete lack of oxygen in the engine room.
Chemical reaction of the CO2 and oil forming carbonic acid.
Sudden stoppage of the main engine.

text: When using the hand crank to retrieve and stow a lifeboat on gravity davits, which of the listed precaution should always be observed?
options: Make certain the hand brake is disengaged.
Put the emergency disconnect switch in the "off" position.
Make certain the crank is in the locked position.

text: Fires occurring in combustible metals, such as sodium or magnesium, are classified as a _________.
In which of the circumstances listed would a carbon dioxide fire extinguishing agent be most effective?
After a liferaft is launched, the operating cord __________.
options: make the victim comfortable in a bunk

text: Which of the following statements concerning immersion suits is correct?
options: All models will automatically turn an unconscious person face-up in the water.
The immersion suit seals in all body heat and provides  protection against hypothermia for weeks.
The suit is flameproof and provides protection to the wearer while swimming through burning oil.
The suits provide for limited body movement such as walking, climbing a ladder, and picking up small objects like a  pencil.

text: The temperature at which a liquid, as listed on a Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS), will change to a gas is called its __________.
Why is it necessary to warn engine room personnel before activating the fixed CO2 system?
options: To make them aware there is a fire.
To let them know they should leave the engine room.
To prevent possible injury from frostbite.
To make them aware that ventilation will automatically stop.

text: Which of the following statements concerning immersion suits is correct?
options: All models will automatically turn an unconscious person face-up in the water.
The immersion suit reduces the rate of body cooling and  greatly increases the survival time in cold water.
The suit is flameproof and provides protection to a wearer swimming in burning oil.
The suit provides a full range of body movement and is  suitable for routine wear on deck.

text: When taking samples of a tank atmosphere with an   explosimeter, you should _____________.
options: only sample around the deck longitudinals as gases are  lighter than air
sample only near the ullage openings as all vapors   accumulate there
avoid sampling in the vicinity of deep webs to prevent false readings
sample as much of the tank as possible, especially at the bottom

text: In cleaning up an oil spill, the use of straw or reclaimed paper fibers would be an example of which type of oil  removal?
What information can be obtained from a marine chemist's certificate on a tank barge?
options: The number of fire extinguishers required onboard.
The quality of the barge's cargo.
The tanks which are safe to enter when the certificate was issued.

text: Hydrocarbon vapors are usually detected by the use of the _____________.
options: is used to rig the boarding ladder
is cut immediately as it is of no further use

text: Pressure within a firemain system will vary from point to point due to ________.
options: friction in the piping and valves

text: The lowest temperature at which a liquid, listed on a  Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS), gives off enough vapor to ingnite in the presence of an ignition source is called its _____________.
Hydrogen sulfide gives crude oil the sour odor of rotten eggs. A hazard to personnel regarding hydrogen sulfide is _____________.
To reduce mild fever the MOST useful drug is ____________.
When whistle signals are used as commands for launching the lifeboats, one short blast means ____________.
options: drill is over, secure all boats

text: The most important thing to remember when launching an inflatable liferaft by hand is to ____________.
options: ensure that the operating cord is secured to the vessel
inflate the raft on the vessel, then lower it over the side

text: Symptoms of sugar diabetes include ____________.
What statement about immersion suits is FALSE?
options: The suit should be worn only when abandoning ship.
The suit is received from the manufacturer in an outer  storage bag that should be used aboard ship for storing the suit.

text: An ullage reading is the distance from a given point at the _________________.
options: sounding tube to either side of the tank
top of the tank down to the bottom of the tank
top of the liquid down to the tank bottom
top of the sounding tube down to the surface of the liquid

text: If a person with diabetes has been injured, the symptoms of the onset of a diabetic coma would include ____________.
options: slurred speech and loss of coordination

text: A chemical material, as listed on a Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) that can undergo a sudden and violent release of pressure and heat is called _________________.
The oil transfer procedures aboard a tanker transferring oil are NOT required to contain _________________.
options: the name of the person designated as the person in charge of transfer
a line diagram of the vessel's oil transfer piping
special procedures for topping off tanks
a description of the deck discharge containment system

text: Material listed on a Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) that violently self-reacts under commonly occurring conditions is called _______________.
What is the purpose of an explosimeter?
options: To indicate the absence of oxygen.

text: What term is listed on a Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) to describe a chemical that can produce life-threatening or seriously disabling health hazard ?
What term is listed on a Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) to describe a chemical that produces only minor health  effects that usually heal with or without medical attention when exposure stops?

A written document identifying chemical material, listing its physical properties, describing health hazards,   required controls, correct procedures for firefighting,  spill or leak cleanup, waste disposal, and the safe handling and storage of the materi
options: Immersion suits provide protection against hypothermia even if there is a small leak or tear.

text: An inflatable liferaft is hand-launched by ____________.
The most effective method in the use of a CO2 fire   extinguisher is for the discharge to be directed at the  _____________.
options: top and sides of a fire
base or nearest edge of a fire

text: When firing a pyrotechnic distress signal, it should be  aimed ____________.
options: at the vessel whose attention you want to attract

text: Which of the following operations should be carried out when launching an inflatable liferaft by hand?
options: Turn the valve on the CO2 cylinder to start inflation
Make sure the operating cord is secured to the vessel before throwing it over the side
After inflation, detach operating cord from liferaft

text: The most beneficial assistance for a person having an  epileptic convulsion is to __________________.
options: give the victim one 30mg tablet of phenobarbitol

text: The bayonet catch on an all purpose nozzle is used to latch in place the ____________.
Your vessel is taking on bunkers from a shore side facility. If oil begins flowing from one tank vent, which of the  following actions should be taken FIRST?
options: Open the intake valve to an adjacent tank.
Set out drip pans and sawdust and begin to mop up the spill.
Signal the shore control point to shutdown.
Close the valve on the tank vent line.

text: Which health hazard term is listed on a Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) to indicate that contact with the substance will freeze body tissue on contact?
The instrument shown in the illustration has not been used for several weeks. Prior to its use for testing a   compartment, you should _______.
Which of the following symptoms would be indicated if a  person's appendix ruptured?
options: Muscle tenseness in almost the entire abdomen

text: The all-purpose, or combination nozzle is capable by itself of producing a solid stream of water __________.
options: and high and low velocity water fog

text: If you wish to remove an inflatable liferaft from its  cradle, the best way to free it is by ____________.
options: unscrewing the turnbuckle on the back of the cradle
lifting one end of the raft
pushing the plunger on the center of the hydrostatic release

text: When a patient is suspected of having appendicitis, the pain should be relieved by ____________.
options: keeping a ice bag over the location of the appendix
giving the patient aspirin with a glass of water

text: According to Regulations , a person who discharges plastic or garbage mixed with plastic is liable for ______________.
options: imprisonment for up to five years for each violation

text: A chemical that causes a substantial proportion of exposed people or animals to develop an allergic reaction in normal tissue after repeated exposure is listed on a Material  Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) as a ____________.
Using a sea anchor will ____________.
options: reduce the drift rate of the liferaft
keep the liferaft from turning over

text: The primary concern in aiding a back injury patient is _______________.
options: relieving the patient's pain by giving aspirin or stronger medication
avoiding possible injury to the spinal cord by incorrect handling
preventing convulsions and muscle spasms caused by the  injury
providing enough fluids to prevent dehydration

text: In order to produce a high velocity water fog using an  all-purpose nozzle, you must _____________.
options: pull the nozzle handle halfway back
push the nozzle handle completely forward

text: The physical data term on a Material Safety Data Sheet  (MSDS) describing whether a liquid is lighter or heavier than water is ____________.
Which of the following conditions is NOT a function of the covered lifeboat pressurized air supply?
options: Provides combustion air for the diesel engine.
Prevents smoke and other noxious fumes from entering craft.

text: When you notice oil on the water near your vessel while  taking on fuel, you should FIRST ____________.
options: determine whether your vessel is the source

text: Ethylene oxide has a lower explosive limit of 2.0% and an upper explosive limit of 100% by volume in air. This means _____________.
options: there is no possible ethylene oxide concentration in air which is too lean to burn
there is no possible ethylene oxide concentration in air which is too rich to burn
an ethylene oxide spill always results in an explosion
a 2.0% concentration of ethylene oxide in air would give a reading of 2.0% on a combustible gas indicator

text: Seasickness is caused by rolling or rocking motions which affect fluids in the ____________.
Progressive flooding on a ship may be indicated by ________.
options: a continual worsening of list or trim

text: While taking on fuel, you notice oil on the water around the vessel. Which of the following actions should be taken  FIRST?
options: Determine if your vessel is the source

text: The most practical method of extinguishing a class "A" fire, involving burning dunnage, is to use _____________.
options: Purple K powder and light water

text: Which of the following should NOT be done for a person who has fainted?
If an incorrect entry were made in the Oil Record Book, youshould _____________.
options: erase the entry and rewrite it
completely black it out and make the correct entry alongside
draw a single line through the wrong entry and initial it

text: A crack, located in the shell plating or deck plating of a ship, may be temporarily prevented from increasing in length by ____________.
options: drilling a hole at each end of the crack
installing welded brackets across both ends
V-grooving and welding from both sides of the crack
cutting a square notch at each end of the crack

text: First aid treatment for battery acid or alkali burns,  especially in the eyes, includes _____________.
options: flushing with large amounts of fresh water and seeking  medical attention ashore or by radio
wiping the affected area with a clean dry cloth and resting quietly for several hours
drying the acid or alkali with a rag followed by applying a light cream
all of the above may be acceptable treatments depending on the severity of the burn

text: When off loading garbage to another ship, your records must identify that ship by name and _________.
In order to find out which lifeboat you are assigned, you should look at the _____________.
What is the required gas supply capacity of an inert gas system?
Firehose can be used for purposes other than firefighting service when ____________.
A vessel sailing through the specific special areas may  discharge or dispose of __________.
options: fairly dense material that will sink, i.e. metal cans or glass bottles at anytime
victual waste when at least 12 nautical miles from shore

text: Were there are multiple accident victims, which type of  injury should be the first to receive emergency treatment?
Generally, when lifting an inflatable liferaft back aboard ship, you would use the ____________.
Which of the following procedures can be used for checking for spinal cord damage to an unconscious patient?
options: Beginning at the back of the neck, and proceeding to the buttocks, press the spine to find where it hurts.
Prick the skin of the hands and the soles of the feet with a sharp object to check for reaction.
Selectively raise each arm and each leg and watch patient's face to see if he registers pain.
Roll patient onto his stomach and prick along the length of his spine to check reaction.

text: Where would you look to find out what your duties are during a fire?
The best means of combating an oil fire on the surface of the water surrounding a vessel tied to the pier, is to use _________.
options: solid water streams directly into the fire
foam directed against the vessel's side

text: After launching, an inflatable raft should be kept dry  inside by ____________.
options: using the bailers and cellulose sponge

text: An inert gas system is designed to reduce the possibility of tank explosions by _____________.
options: eliminating sparks and fire in the vicinity of cargo tanks
removing all hydrocarbon gases from the cargo tanks
blanketing cargo tanks with inert foam
reducing the oxygen concentration below levels necessary for combustion

text: The three positions of an all-purpose fire nozzle are ____________.
options: forward: off, center: fog, back: solid stream
forward: solid stream, center: fog, back: off
forward: fog, center: solid stream, back: off
forward: off, center: solid stream, back: fog

text: Where there are multiple accident victims, which injuries should be the FIRST to receive emergency treatment?
options: Back injuries with spinal cord damage

text: The purpose of the four water pockets, located on the  underside at each corner of the raft, is to ____________.
options: stow rainwater; these four spaces will not take up valuable space

text: In managing a situation involving multiple injuries, the rescuer must be able to ____________.
options: rapidly evaluate the seriousness of obvious injuries
accurately diagnose the ailment or injury

text: After an injury, which of the following can be determined by examining the condition of a victim's pupils?
options: The degree of pain being suffered.
Whether or not the brain is functioning properly.
Whether or not the victim's blood pressure is normal.

text: What is the primary purpose of a spint applied in first aid?
On most makes of inflatable liferafts, the batteries to  operate the light on the inside of the rafts can be made to last longer by ____________.
options: unscrewing the bulb during the daylight
operating the switch for the light
taking no action as there is no way of preserving power
taking no action as they shut off automatically in daylight

text: Immediately after a class "B" fire has been extinguished by the use of foam, crewmen should _____________.
options: carefully apply a low velocity fog over the foam blanket
wade through the foam blanket to evenly distribute the foam
be careful not to disturb the foam blanket
sweep the foam blanket overboard and secure firefighting equipment

text: A person has suffered a larceration of the arm. Severe bleeding has been controlled by using a sterile dressing and direct pressure. What should you do next?
options: Apply a torniqiuet to prevent the bleeding from restarting.
Apply a pressure bandage over the dressing.
Remove any small foreign matter and apply antiseptic
Administer fluids to assist the body in replacing the lost blood.

text: Spontaneous combustion can occur in _____________.
options: paint thinner in an open can
oily rags stored in an unvented compartment

text: Which of the listed fire extinguishing agents would be  incorrect for the direct use on a gasoline or oil fire?
Which statement is FALSE regarding Halon as a fire extinguising agent?
options: It is more effective than CO2.
It is noncorrosive. and in an upright position hold the freshwater required by regulation to be provided in the raft when packed

text: When an oil fire has been extinguished, the surface of the oil should be kept covered with foam to prevent ___________.
options: air from contacting the oil vapors permitting reignition
spontaneous combustion below the oil surface
toxic fumes from escaping to the surface

text: When fighting an oil or gasoline fire, which of the listed fire extinguishing agents should NEVER be sprayed directly into the fire?
Bleeding from a vein may be ordinarily controlled by __________.
options: applying direct pressure to the wound
pouring ice water directly into the wound

text: Fires resulting from spontaneous combustion are usually  caused by the improper disposal of _____________.
In all but the most severe cases, bleeding from a wound should be controlled by ___________.
options: submerging the wound in lukewarm water
applying direct pressure to the wound

text: According to Regulations , a pressurer-demand, open cicuit, self contained breathing appratus shall have a minimum _______ minutes air supply.
The rescuer can best provide an airtight seal during mouth-to- mouth resuscitation by pinching the vitim's nostrils and ____________.
options: cupping a hand around the patient's mouth
applying his mouth tightly over the victim's mouth

text: Which of the following is classified as a grade "E"   combustible liquid?
Which of the circumstances listed is an exception to the garbage discharge requirements in Annex V to MARPOL 73/78?
options: The garbage to be discharged will sink.
Garbage accumulation onboard has exceeded storage space.
A person falls overboard, and a plastic ice chest is thrown for flotation.

text: The most likely place for a fire to start through the  process of spontaneous combustion is in _____________.
You are fighting a class "B" fire with a potable dry chemical extinguisher. The discharge should be directed ___________.
options: at the seat of the fire, starting near the edge
over the top of the fire
at the main body of the fire
to bank off a bulkhead onto the fire

text: Which extinguising agent is most likely to allow reflash as a result of not cooling the fuel below its ignition temperature?
Liferafts are less maneuverable than lifeboats due to their ____________.
A tourniquet should be used to control bleeding ONLY _________.
options: when all other means have failed
to prevent bleeding from from minor wounds

text: The date of each fire and boat drill conducted on a ship must be recorded _____________.
Which of the practices listed should be avoided since it represents a fire hazard?
options: Stowing oily rags in a paint locker
Stowing portable acetylene bottles in the vertical position

text: When taking on fuel it is important to remember that  petroleum vapors are _____________.
options: not volatile unless the ambient air temperature exceeds  32oC
lighter than air and dissipate rapidly
unlikely to ignite except in the presence of an open flame
heavier than air and may enter the bilges and/or deckhouse

text: The only portable electrical equipment permitted in a compartment which is not gas free is a lamp that is ______________.
When welding or burning aboard a vessel, you must be certain that the space _____________.
options: has no oil in the bilges

text: If a small fire initially breaks out in the engine room  bilges, in which of the following sequences should the fire extinguishing agents or systems be used?
options: Fixed CO2, semi-portable CO2, then hand portable CO2.
Semi-portable CO2, fixed CO2, then hand portable CO2.
Hand portable, semi-portable CO2, then as a last resort  the fixed CO2 system.
Hand portable, fixed CO2, then as a last resort the   semi-portable CO2.

text: You are about to enter a compartment to investigate a  suspected smoldering fire. If you're not wearing an self-contained breathing apparatus, you should test the compartment's  atmosphere to determine if there is/are _____________.
options: sufficient oxygen to sustain human life
an explosive mixture in the compartment
a toxic atmosphere in the compartment

text: All of the following are part of the fire triangle EXCEPT ___________.
The best way to combat an engine room bilge fire is through the use of a _____________.
options: dry chemical extinguisher and solid stream water
foam extinguisher and low velocity water fog
foam extinguisher and solid stream water

text: To produce a low velocity water fog spray with an   all-purpose fire hose nozzle, you must _____________.
options: push the nozzle handle completely forward
change the fire pump discharge pressure
pull the nozzle handle completely back
insert a low velocity fog applicator

text: When administering mouth to mouth resucitation to an adult, you should breathe at the rate of how many breaths per minute?
If you hear the sound of a gas escaping from the liferaft just after it has inflated, you should ____________.
options: quickly hunt for the hole before the raft deflates
check the sea anchor line for a tear if the seas are rough
check the painter line attachment for a tear caused by the initial opening
not panic as the relief valves allow excess pressure to  escape

text: The fixed CO2 fire extinguishing system has been activated to extinguish a large engine room bilge fire. When is the best time to vent the combustible products from the engine room?
options: Immediately after the fire is extinguished. Possible suffocation from the CO2 gas.
One half-hour after the fire is extinguished. Forgetting to secure the engine room ventilation system.
After the metal surfaces have cooled down. CO2 gas being drawn into nearby engine or air compressor intakes.
Before any personnel in fireman outfits reenter the engine room. Rekindling of the fire.

text: Which portable fire extinguiser is normally recharged in a shore facilty?
You have abandoned ship in tropical waters. Which of the following procedures should be used during a prolonged  period in a liferaft?
options: Wet clothes during the day to decrease perspiration.

text: Which of the listed extinguishing agents is NOT suitable for fighting a liquid paint fire?
If you reach shore in a liferaft, the first thing to do is ____________.
options: drag the raft ashore and lash it down for a shelter
find some wood for a fire
get the provisions out of the raft
set the raft back out to sea so someone may spot it

text: The spreading of fire as a result of heat being carried  through a vessel's ventilation system, is an example of heat transfer by _____________.
Which of the listed type of fires would the fire   extinguisher, shown in the illustration, be suitable for extinguishing?
options: Small oil fire on the engine room floor plates

text: If a fire ignites in the engine room as a result of a high pressure fuel oil leak, you should FIRST _____________.
options: shut off the fuel oil supply

text: The four basic components of a fire are fuel, heat, oxygen and a chain reaction. Which of the following statements best describes what must be eliminated to extinguish a fire?
options: Any one component can be eliminated to extinguish a fire. will normally dissipate rapidly
Any two components must be eliminated to properly extinguish a fire. will always form explosive vapors below 0oC ambient
Any three components must be eliminated to properly   extinguish a fire. may collect in ship's bilges
All four components must be eliminated to extinguish any fire. are of no concern if the wind is blowing

text: Which extinguishing agent is the best for use on electrical fires?
It is necessary to cool the bulkheads and decks surrounding a compartment where there is a fire in order to ___________.
options: cool the metal below its ignition temperature
form a dense coating of smothering steam
prevent oxygen from reaching the flames
prevent the fire from spreading by the conduction of heat

text: As its temperature rises, the volume of fuel oil in the  tanks will _____________.
Combustible material stored in a compartment adjacent to a compartment where there is a fire may be ignited. This  ignition can occur by ____________.
options: heat convection through the adjacent bulkheads
combustion gases carried through the ventilation system
direct convection of the heat emitted by the adjacent fire
heat radiated from the deck or bulkhead by the adjacent fire

text: If the water level is rising in the bilge of a survival  craft, you should first ____________.
options: check for cracks in the hull
shift all personnel to the stern

text: The most important reason for taking anti-seasickness pills as soon as possible after entering a liferaft is to   ____________.
options: prevent loss of body moisture by vomiting
prevent impaired judgement due to motion-induced   deliriousness

text: When abandoning ship, after launching the motor lifeboat you should ____________.
options: go only with the direction of the sea
go one direction until fuel runs out

text: Which of the following statements holds true for both CO2 and Halon 1301 fixed extinguishing systems?
options: A cylinder is considered satisfactory if its weight is  within 10% of the stamped full weight of the charge. Sprinkler heads installed in the living spaces would have a higher melting point fusible link than those installed in the galley.

If a protected space is ventilated mechanically, the  ventilation system must be automatically shutdown by the release of the agent. Sprinkler heads installed in the galley would have a higher melting point fusible link than those installed in the  living spaces.

To avoid confusion during an emergency situation, there  should be only one action necessary (such as a single pull box) to activate the system. Sprinkler heads installed in the galley and living spaces would have the same fusible link melting point.
All of the above. None of the above; sprinkler heads with fusible links are prohibited aboard vessels

text: A fire cannot exist if which of the listed elements is  removed from the fire?
Breathing petroleum vapors is hazardous and may cause _____________.
When combating an oil fire in a deep tank aboard ship, water fog can be used to extinguish the fire and ____________.
options: prevent any deformation of the tank top

text: On a vessel with lifeboats on both sides, the No. 2 lifeboat would be located ____________.
options: forward of lifeboat No. 4 on the starboard side
forward of lifeboat No. 4 on the port side
aft of lifeboat No. 1 on the starboard side

text: The No. 2 lifeboat on a tanker would be found __________.
options: aft of No. 1 lifeboat port side
aft of No. 1 lifeboat starboard side

text: While working in the engine room, you hear seven short  blasts followed by one long blast on the ship's whistle, supplemented by the same signal on the general alarm bells. You should _____________.

text: Before attempting to put out a class "C" fire involving an electric- driven centrifugal pump, you should FIRST ________.
To extinguish an electrical fire, your FIRST action should be to ____________.

secure the power isolate and secure the piping
Completely discharge a 15 pound (6.8 kg) CO2 extinguisher throw sand on the fire
apply mechanical or chemical foam you should FIRST _____________. extinguish the fire using a combination nozzle with   applicator

text: Which of the following hazards is associated with the  handling of petroleum products?

options: recorded as cubic meters with a conversion to barrels
recorded directly from the oil discharge monitor
consistently recorded through the Oil Record Book in one specified unit (gallons, barrels, cubic meters)

text: Which of the listed tanks presents the greatest potential for an explosion?
options: A full tank of diesel oil
A partially filled tank of diesel oil
A full tank of lubricating oil
A partially filled tank of hydraulic oil

text: Which of the following conditions is true concerning a  flammable liquid with a concentration above the upper  explosive limit?

discharge an initial charge of CO2 with a hand portable extinguisher
direct a fire hose into the tank and energize the fire main
spray the tank boundaries with a fire hose to promote  cooling

text: Which of the following steps should normally be taken first by those who have boarded a lifeboat in an emergency  situation?
options: Determine position and closest point of land

text: Gasoline is a flammable liquid whose vapors are ___________.

If the buoyant force on a ship's hull is equal to or greater than the displacement tonnage, the ship will _____________.
options: require ballast added to only the port side tanks

text: When a vessel is inclined due to an external force, such as the action of seas in which no cargo shifts, the tendency of the vessel to return to its original position is caused by the shift in ______.

Continuous blast of the ship's whistle for a period of not less than 10 seconds supplemented by the same signal on the general alarm.
Three short blasts of the whistle supplemented by the same signal on the general alarm.
More than six short blasts and one long blast on the whistle supplemented by the same signal on the general alarm.

text: Which of the fire extinguishing agents listed is the safest to use when combating a class C fire?

A continuous blast of the whistle for a period of not less than 10 seconds, supplemented by the continuous ringing of the general alarm bells for not less than 10 seconds, would indicate a _____________.
The principal effect of liquid free surface is dependent upon the volume of displacement of the vessel and the  ____________.
options: height of liquid in the tank
amount of liquid in the tank
weight of liquid in the tank

text: Vessel stability is greatly affected when water or fuel  tanks are partially filled as a result of the _____________.

To prevent an explosion when the pumproom has not been certified gas free and repairs are to be carried out, the only type of portable electric equipment allowed in the  pumproom would be an approved _____________.
options: explosion proof self-contained battery powered lamp
marine drop lamp and extension cords

text: Diesel fuel is relatively safe to handle due to its low  volatility, but the fumes will self-ignite and continue  to burn steadily if the fuel is heated to the _____________.
Class C fires may be combatted using a _____________.
Which of the listed fire extinguishers should be used on an electrical fire aboard ship?

If you hear a continuous blast of the whistle for a period of not less than 10 seconds; supplemented by a continuous ringing of the general alarm bells for the same time period, you should _____________.
options: proceed to your man overboard muster station

text: Before making any welded repairs to the internals of a  vessel's fuel tank in a  port, the tank must be examined by _____________.
A fuel tank is considered to be gas free when the tank is _____________.
options: inerted with carbon dioxide for 24 hours
free of most flammable gas concentrations
thoroughly ventilated for at least 24 hours
free of dangerous concentrations of flammable or toxic gases

text: Which of the following precautions are necessary when using any electrical equipment in a hazardous location, such as a cargo pumproom?
options: The pumproom is to be gas free.
The adjacent compartments are to be gas free.
Each compartment where flammable gas is expected to accumulate is to be closed and secured.

text: Which of the following procedures should be used to examine a cartridge-operated dry chemical fire extinguisher?
options: Make certain that the pressure cartridge has not been  punctured. adding water to the powder
Check to see that hose and nozzle are clear. painting the CO2 cartridge red
Ensure that dry chemical is not become caked. puncturing the CO2 cartridge
All of the above. shaking the extinguisher to loosen the caked-up portions of the powder

text: When reentering an engine room that has been flooded with CO2, the investigating team should initially _____________.

static electricity could be generated by fuel falling  through vapor- laden air
spillage of fuel could occur with an open hatch
water contamination of the fuel could occur

text: The state of charge of a stored pressure type dry chemical fire extinguisher can be readily determined by ____________.

removing the lid and checking the level of dry chemical

have its electrical equipment insulated from its structure

text: Which of the following statements describes the advantage of using a Halon fire extinguisher versus a CO2 fire   extinguisher?
options: Halon cools rather than smothers a fire.
Halon is more effective than CO2.
Halon can extinguish combustible metal fires.
Halon is not toxic and safe to breathe under all conditions.

text: The inclining experiment conducted on a merchant ship is the method for determining the exact location of the __________.

text: If a vessel rolls to the starboard side, and there are no movable or moving weights onboard, the center of gravity will ____________.

text: Liquid petroleum products will become electrically charged as a result of the _____________.

movement of water droplets in an oil filled tank
oil moving through the pipe lines
All of the above are correct.

bonding cable only if sparks are seen

connect ground cable, open switch, and connect cargo hose
close switch, connect cargo hose, open switch, and connect ground cable
connect ground cable, connect cargo hose, open switch, and then close switch

text: Which statement is true concerning Halon 1301 fire extinguishing equipment?

The agent is less effective than CO2.
Halon extinguishes the fire by smothering.
The agent is a colorless odorless gas.

text: To operate a CO2 fire extinguisher equipped with a squeeze grip handle, you are to remove the locking pin and ________.

Loosely set up two dogs adjacent to the hinges, snugly set up one dog on the opposite side from the hinges, then evenly set up all the remaining dogs.
Snugly set up two dogs on the opposite side from the hinges, then evenly set up all the remaining dogs.
Loosely set up one dog on the hinge side, snugly set up two dogs on the opposite side from the hinges, then evenly set up all the remaining dogs.

text: Portable carbon dioxide fire extinguishers must be recharged _____________.

when the remaining charge weight is 10% less than the  required weight

Replace the air cylinder with a full spare cylinder.

more freeboard is required in the North Atlantic in   winter

National Standard fire hose coupling threads shall be used having 9 threads per inch for 2 1/2 inch hose and 7 1/2  threads per inch for 1 1/2 inch hose.

Steel wire trigger lines provided to all bottles in the main bank.

text: Which of the following statements is true concerning a,Halon 1301 fixed extinguishing system?

text: The most critical time for preventing an accidental oil  spill during bunkering, is when the _____________.

options: The instrument is capable of providing an immediate accurate reading of any space with no delay.

text: Which of the following liquids can ordinarily be discharged overboard without being processed through an oily water separator?

text: Where would you look to find out what your duties are during a fire?

fixed foam systems to be ineffective

text: Dry chemical fire extinguishers may be effectively used on which of the listed classes of fires?

text: As a precaution against oil spills when topping off fuel tanks, you should _____________.

text: When administering chest compression during CPR, at what part of the victim's body should the pressure be applied?

options: at each alarm bell survivors in the water
at each alarm actuator food and water

make sure the fire is out with CO2

text: Which of the listed devices is required to be located on the discharge side of the pump in a fire main system?
