text: Pollution Prevention Regulations (MARPOL) state that when a tank vessel is discharging cargo, each sea suction valve connected to the vessel's oil transfer, ballast, or cargo tank systems must be ____________.
Why should passageway fires be fought from one end only?
options: This action will cause the passageway to become slippery and difficult for fire fighters to stand.
So that one hose team does not push the flames heat and  smoke directly at the other hose team.
It is impossible for the fire main to handle two fully  pressurized hoses.
So that the extinguishing water can be trapped at the end of the passageway.

text: Keeping the draft of a vessel at, or below the load line mark, insures the unit will have adequate _______________.
Mechanical foam used for firefighting is produced by  mechanically mixing and agitating _____________.
options: foam chemical with air and water
bicarbonate soda with air and water

text: When a vessel is floating upright, the distance from the keel to the metacenter is called the ______________.
Artificial respiration may be necessary in cases of _______.
The discharge side of every fire pump must be provided with a ______________. 0.8
Which of the devices listed must be used in conjunction with the portable in-line foam proportioner to produce foam?
options: A plain or common fire hose nozzle 3.5kg/cm2

text: The amount of Halon remaining in a fire extinguisher  cylinder is determined by ______________.
The use of flexible hose pipes to connect the crude oil  washing machines is permitted only on ________________.
Fire hydrant valves on a crude oil tanker shall be isolated from the crude oil washing system by ____________.
Antiseptics are used principally to ___________.
Which type of firefighting foam listed is supplied in both 3% and 6% concentrations?
The water in which a vessel floats provides vertical upward support. The point through which this support is assumed to act is known as the center of ______________.
If you have to abandon ship, and enter a liferaft, your main course of action should be to ________________.
options: remain in the vicinity of the sinking vessel

text: No outlet on a fire hydrant may point above the horizontal, in order to _______________.
When water fog is used as an extinguishing agent, the fire is extinguished principally by the ____________.
options: isolation of the fuel righting arm
displacement of gases of combustion gravity and the buoyant forces is called the ___________. metacentric radius
height of the center of buoyancy

text: The order of importance in addressing damage control on a floating ship is ________________.
options: control flooding, control fire, repair structural damage
restore vital services, control fire, control flooding
control fire, restore vital services, control flooding
control fire, control flooding, repair structural damage

text: Which of the following is NOT a recommended safe practice?
options: Securing equipment against slipping or drifting.
Operating machinery at its recommended speed.
Repairing loose handles on tools before using.
Using tools for purposes for which they are not designed.

text: If accidents are considered as an "unexpected contact", you will be able to perform a better job by observing which of the following practices?
Low velocity water fog is very effective in _____________.
options: removing toxic fumes from the air
controlling the movement of burning oil
removing combustible vapors from the air
shielding fire fighters from the fire

text: What information can be obtained from a marine chemist'scertificate issued to a fishing vessel?
options: The number of fire extinguishers required onboard.
The quality of fuel in the vessel's tanks
The tanks which are safe to enter when the certificate was issued

text: Sufficient portable drive heads for a crude oil washing  system must be provided in order to avoid one head being used during the tank washing procedure more than __________.

A seaman has sustained a small, gaping laceration of the arm requiring sutures to close if medical facilities were  available. Which of the following remedies can be done  to temporarily close the wound?
options: Wrap a tight bandage around the wound.
Gently close the wound and while holding it closed apply a compression bandage.
Use temporary stitches of sail twine.
Apply butterfly strips and cover with a sterile dressing.

text: Aqueous film forming firefighting foam is also known as _____________.
A person reports to you with a fishhook in their thumb. The accepted procedure for removing it should be to ___________.
options: cut the skin from around the hook
push the barb through, cut it off, and then remove the hook

text: A vessel is subjected to "hogging" when it __________.
options: has its main deck under compressive stress
has its bottom plating under tensile stress
is supported on a wave whose crests are at the bow and stern
is supported on a wave whose crest is amidships

text: At which of the listed tank locations should you obtain  oxygen content readings prior to tank washing?
options: At the hatch coaming and tank bottom.
At the hatch coaming and middle of the tank.
At the middle and bottom of the tank.
At the center of the ullage and one meter below deck.

text: Which of the following fire extinguishing agents has the greatest capacity for heat absorption?
Overhauling a fire in the living quarters on a vessel must include _______________.
options: opening dead spaces to check for fire or lingering   sources of heat

text: Which of the documents listed will describe lifesaving  equipment located aboard your vessel?
A fire main system must have enough fire hydrants for each accessible space to be reached with ______________.
options: a water spray or solid stream
at least two spray patterns of water

text: With damaged floating vessels, the most important   consideration is the preservation of ________________.
Water applied as a "fog" can be more effective than water applied as a "solid stream", because _____________.
options: a given amount of water can absorb more heat when it is in the form of fog
it reduces the total amount of water that must be pumped into the ship to fight a given fire
it does not have to hit the seat of fire to be effective

text: On a cargo ship, firefighting equipment must be inspected once every _______________.
After extinguishing a paint locker fire using the fixed CO2 system, the next immediate action is for the space to be ____________.
Which of the actions listed and instituted on your part  will have the greatest lasting effect on the crew with  respect to safety?
options: Showing video tapes of actual accidents
Incorporating safety practices in daily routine

text: When a floating vessel inclines to an angle slightly greater than the angle of loll, it will ____________.
options: return to the angle of loll

text: Initial stability refers to the vessel's stability ________.
options: when loaded with minimum deck load

text: Which of the following must be carried out in order to  launch and inflate an inflatable liferaft?
options: Pull on the hydrostatic release, pull on the sea painter.
Push on the hydrostatic release, pull on the sea painter.
Push on the hydrostatic release, push on the sea painter.
Pull on the hydrostatic release, push on the sea painter.

text: The maximum draft to which a cargo ship may be safely loaded is called __________.
The preferred method of controlling external bleeding is by _____________.
options: applying direct pressure on the wound
applying pressure on a pressure point
applying a tourniquet above the wound

text: You have found a person laying prone and not breathing. An electric wire is touching the victim. Which of the actions listed is the FIRST thing you should do?
options: Shut off the power or remove the wire from the person.
Leave the accident scene and summon medical assistance.

text: The stability term for the distance between the center of gravity (G) to the Metacenter (M), when small angle   stability applies is known as the _________________.
Bleeding from a vein can be characterized as _____________.
Control valves for a CO2 fire extinguishing system may be located within the protected space when _____________.
options: it is impractical to locate them outside
checked for the proper oxygen level
opened and doused with water to prevent reflash

text: If the cause of severe list, or trim is due to off-center ballast, counterflooding into empty tanks will ____________.
The important stability parameter "KG" is defined as the _______________.
options: height of the metacenter above the keel
height of the center of buoyancy above the keel
height of the center of gravity above the keel

text: A person suffering from possible broken bones and internal injuries should __________.
options: be examined then walked to a bunk
not be moved, but made comfortable until medical assistance arrives, provided he is in a safe environment
not be allowed to lie down where injured but moved to a  chair or bunk

text: What is the function of the small gas accumulator cylinder attached to the discharge piping of the engine room fixed CO2 system?
options: It absorbs the pressure shock to the piping resulting from the initial release of carbon dioxide.
It delays the release of carbon dioxide into the engine  room.
It directly activates the warning siren in the engine room.
It controls the rate of discharge of carbon dioxide into the engine room.

text: Your assigned emergency stations aboard your vessel can be found on the ship's ______________.
Operation of the release control valve on a fixed CO2 fire extinguishing system, must automatically ______________.
options: release CO2 to the protected space
secure all mechanical ventilation in the protected space
sound the general alarm signal for a fire
sound an alarm in the ballast control room

text: How often must fixed CO2 fire extinguishing systems be  inspected to confirm the cylinders are within 10% of the stamped full charge weight?
Unless there is danger of further injury, a person with a broken bone should not be moved until bleeding is controlled and _______________.
The usual method for effectively applying foam on a fire is by ___________.
options: spraying directly on the base of the fire
flowing the foam down a vertical surface
sweeping the fire before you with the foam the CO2 cylinders are also in the space
spraying directly on the surface of the fire an automatic heat sensing trip is installed

text: As a firefighting medium, CO2 can be dangerous under certain conditions as it can cause _____________.
A quick and rapid motion of a floating vessel in a seaway is an indication of ____________.
The object of plugging holes below the waterline on a ship should be to ____________.
options: eliminate all water entering through the hole
only plug holes in machinery or other vital spaces
reduce the entry of water as much as possible

text: A CO2 fire extinguisher is checked by __________.
options: discharging a small amount of CO2

text: You should operate a fixed CO2 system by opening the  control valve to the space protected and ___________.
options: visually checking to see that CO2 is discharging through the piping
operating a separate control to release CO2
listening for the sound of the warning siren
modulating the proper amount of CO2 from individual bottles

text: In order to release CO2 to the machinery spaces, you must physically open the engine room ____________.
options: control valve and actuate the alarm
CO2 releasing valve and actuate the alarm
control valve, then open the CO2 releasing valve
control valve, then shut off the engine room ventilation

text: Portable Halon fire extinguishers used  may be  charged with _______________.
The most effective means for combating an oil fire on the water surrounding a vessel is to use _____________.
options: high velocity fog over the fire
solid streams of water directed at the edge of the fire
foam directed against the side of the vessel

text: At all angles of inclination, the true measure of a vessel's stability is the _____________.
options: metacentric height weight required to sink the vessel
displacement total weight of the vessel

text: When the height of the metacenter is less than the height of the center of gravity, a vessel has what type of stability?
Foam extinguishes a fire by _____________.
options: releasing large amounts of carbon dioxide

text: Which of the following conditions is a symptom of a person in shock?
Which of the following conditions is NOT a symptom of  traumatic shock?
Which of the following procedures is always recommended in the rescue of an unconscious person from a compartment containing an unsafe atmosphere?
options: Have the rescuers wear an approved self-contained breathing apparatus.
Have the rescuers carry in a stokes basket for the victim.
Have the rescuer carry an emergency first aid kit for the victim.
Have the rescuers carry a combustible gas analyzer.

text: A person who observes an individual fall overboard from a ship should ______________.
options: immediately jump into the water to assist the individual
call for help and keep the individual in sight
run to the radio room to send an emergency message
go to the control room for the distress flares

text: Increasing the free surface of a confined liquid has the effect of raising the ____________.
options: virtual height of the center of gravity

text: Which of the following statements is true concerning the actions of a properly organized rescue party for removing an unconscious victim from a tank containing toxic vapors?
options: The victim should not be moved until the extent of his  injuries is determined. accomplished by an authorized servicing representative
The victim should be fitted with a canister type OBA as soon as possible. recorded by the person-in-charge
The rescue party should provide the victim with oxygen or fresh air immediately. _______________. completed every six months

text: On a cargo ship equipped with a combination detection-carbon dioxide system, the presence of fire is indicated at the cabinet simultaneously in two spaces: #3 hold upper between decks, containing bagged coffee beans; and #4 lower hold containing fertilize
options: open the three-way valves to each space simultaneously
open the applicable three-way valve for #3 hold and fight the fire in #4 hold with water
open the applicable three-way valve for #4 hold. After  discharge, secure the valve; then open the applicable valve for #3 hold.
activate the pilot CO2 cylinders, directing the discharge to #3 hold, then to #4 hold through the three-way valve

text: When the height of the metacenter is the same as the height of the center of gravity, the upright equilibrium position is ________________.
Foam extinguishes a fire by ____________.
options: chemical reaction with the burning material

text: Extinguishing an oil fire when using foam is a result of _____________.
When treating a person for shock, you should wrap the victim in warm coverings to ____________.
options: avoid self-inflicted wounds caused by spastic movement
protect the person from injury during transportation

text: The reserve buoyancy of a vessel varies directly with  changes in the vessel's _____________.
High concentrations of hydrogen sulfide gas are most  dangerous to personnel because they can _____________.
After an accident, the victim may go into shock and die. Which of the following actions should be taken to help  prevent shock?
options: Slightly elevate the head and feet.
Keep the person lying down and at a comfortable temperature. personnel enter.

text: Why is an upper limit switch used when raising the lifeboat?
options: To prevent the davits from pulling up against the stops.
To assist in cranking in the lifeboat.
To keep the tricing lines from releasing or getting tangled.
To stop the lifeboat from being lowered.

text: The maximum concentration of hydrogen sulfide to which  workers may be regularly exposed without adverse effects is _____________.
Firefighting foam is only effective when the foam ________.
options: penetrates to the bottom of the fire
is kept saturated with low velocity water fog
mixes with the burning fuel oil
completely covers the top of the burning liquid

text: When fighting a fire on deck with foam, you should ________.
options: play the foam stream directly on the burning oil
play the foam stream off nearby vertical vessel structures
only use the foam on flowing liquids
play the foam stream on the base of the fire with whirling motion

text: Which of the following machinery space operations is  required to be logged in the Oil Record Book?
options: Ballasting or cleaning of fuel oil tanks.
Shifting suction of main fuel pump to reserve fuel oil tank.
Changing out sprayer plates to adjust for steam demand.
Daily inspection of engine room bilges.

text: Chemical foam is most suitable for use on a fire involving _____________.
First, second, and third degree burns are classified  according to ____________.
options: source of heat causing the burn

text: The approval period for a shipboard oil pollution emergency plan expires after __________.
The hand brake of a lifeboat winch is _____________.
options: manually disengaged when hoisting a boat
applied by dropping the counterweighted lever
controlled by the centrifugal brake mechanism
automatically engaged if lowering speed is excessive

text: The empty weight of a "100 pound" (45.4 kg) cylinder in a fixed CO2 system is 130 pounds (58.9 kg). What is the  minimum acceptable total weight of the cylinder before  recharging is required?
When the engine room fixed CO2 system is activated, which of the following actions should be taken FIRST if the automatic fuel oil and ventilation shutoffs have failed to operate?
options: Manually operate the remote shutdown controls.
Activate an additional CO2 bottle to increase pressure on the pressure switch plunger.
Send someone down to manually secure the fuel oil pumps and ventilation fans.
Discharge extra carbon dioxide into the engine room.

text: The difference between the initial trim of a vessel and the trim after a change in load has occurred is known as  _____________.
A weathertight door on a ocean going ship must not allow water to penetrate into the unit _____________.
In firefighting, the term "protecting exposures" means _____________.
options: protecting fire fighters from direct exposure to the heat of the fire
keeping flames from burning at the tank vents
protecting exposed areas of the superstructure from flames
taking measures to prevent the spread of fire from the  involved compartment to an adjacent compartment

text: For an upright vessel, draft is the vertical distance  between the keel and the _____________.
Aqueous Film Forming Foam (AFFF), commonly known as   "light water", is especially suitable for fighting   _____________.
options: oil fires in the engine room bilges
class B fires in paint lockers

text: When treating a person for third degree burns, you should ___________.
options: submerge the burn area in cold water
make the person stand up and walk to increase circulation
cover the burns with thick sterile dressing
break blisters and remove dead tissue

text: The centrifugal brake mechanism incorporated in a lifeboat handling winch is intended to _____________.
options: automatically stop the boat if electric power fails
mechanically stop the boat when the hand brake fails
hold the load suspended during lowering operations
automatically control the speed of lowering by gravity

text: A device for preventing sparks or flames from entering a tank, yet permitting the free passage of gases is _____.
A fine mesh wire screen is fitted to tank vent pipes to  prevent _____________.
options: passage of flames into the tank
entry of rodents into the tank
debris stopping up the vent pipe
embers from a fire entering the tank

text: Each fireman's outfit and its spare equipment on a ship must be stowed _____________.
options: in a locked cabinet in the machinery space
in an unlocked cabinet in the machinery space
in a separate and accessible location

text: If a crewman suffers a second degree burn on the arm, you should ____________________.
options: scrub the arm thoroughly to prevent infection
immerse the arm in cold water

text: On a ship, the locker or space containing the self-contained breathing apparatus must be _____________.
options: located in close proximity to the main control station
equipped with battery powered emergency lighting

text: In the Oil Record Book, a comprehensive list of operational items are grouped into operational sections. Each section is codified by a/an ____________.
Severe airway burns will cause _____________________.
Which is the best way to ensure adequate oxygen is present for safe entry into a seldomly used cargo tank?
options: Test tank atmosphere with a combustible gas indicator.
Observe the amount of rust deposited on the tank's surface.
Ventilate the tank with fresh air and test with an oxygen indicator.
Open the manhole cover for approximately ten minutes.

text: A vessel would most likely develop a list if you were _____________.
options: burning fuel from a centerline tank
using water from two equally sized tanks on either side of the centerline simultaneously
using fuel from tanks on the port side only
using water from the fore peak tank

text: A shipboard oil pollution emergency plan is required of  _________.
options: all vessels, regardless of size and commercial application
any barge or other ship which is constructed or operated in such a manner that no oil in any form can be carried aboard
an oil tanker of 150 gross tons or above, or other ship of 400 gross tons or above
an oil tanker of 400 gross tons and above, or other ships of 150 gross tons and above

text: Which of the toxic gases listed is a product of incomplete combustion, and is often present when a fire burns in a  closed compartment?
Why are flame screens an important safety device?
options: They provide a "dead air space" within a fuel tank.
They accelerate the dispersal of fuel vapors.
They prevent the passage of flames from or into closed  spaces.
They smother flames once a fire has been started.

text: On a ship, a cabinet or space containing the controls or valves for the fixed firefighting system must be __________.
options: posted with instructions on the operation of the system
ventilated and equipped with explosion-proof switches
painted with red and black diagonal stripes
equipped with a battery powered source of emergency lighting

text: Each hand portable fire extinguisher carried on a ship must be marked with ____________.
options: the name of the unit on which it is located
the date that it was installed on the unit
the names of the individuals qualified to use it
an identification number different from other extinguishers on the unit

text: A minor heat burn of the eye should be treated by _________.
options: warming the eye with moist warm packs
laying the person flat on his back
mineral oil drops directly on the eye

text: Which of the listed substances is susceptible to spontaneous combustion?
The FIRST action in the treatment of a person suspected of having airway burns is to ____________.
options: move him to a cool location
confirm and maintain an open airway
apply cool damp dressing to his neck

text: It shall be insured that each lifeboat on a cargo ship is lowered to the water, launched, and operated at  least once every _____________.
options: month Apply by bouncing agent off nearby bulkheads.
two months Use a quick sweeping motion towards the base of the fire.
three months Always direct discharge at the source of the fire.
six months It shouldn't be used on an electrical fire.

text: Chemical burns are caused by the skin coming in contact  with ___________.
Which of the fire extinguishing agents listed uses a chain breaking action to put out a fire?
Compliance with the terms of the load line certificate on a ship is the responsibility of the ______________.
Since electrical burn victims have been subjected to  electric shock, the FIRST medical response is to check for ____________.
What should you do with your emergency position indicating radio beacon if you are in a liferaft during storm   conditions?
options: Bring it inside the liferaft and leave it on.
Bring it inside the liferaft and turn it off until the storm passes.
Leave it outside the liferaft and leave it on.
Leave it outside the liferaft but turn it off.

text: After being launched from a ship, a totally enclosed  survival craft which has been afloat over a long period of time, requires _______________.
options: frequent opening of hatches to permit entry of fresh air
use of ear plugs to dampen engine noise
frequent flushing of the water spray system with fresh water

text: The purpose for the bag or box on top of some survival craft is to ______________.
options: right it in case of capsize
act as a sail in case of a power loss
steady the craft in heavy seas

text: Normally fire hose should be stowed with the ___________.
options: female end connected to the fire hydrant Rigidly mounted reduction gear bearing
male end attached to the fire hydrant Segmental pivoted-shoe thrust bearing
female end connected to the nozzle Self-aligning radial bearing
female end and nozzle arranged to be quickly run out to the fire Spherically-seated radial bearing

text: Which of the following statements is true concerning the Halon 1301 fire extinguishing agent?
options: The agent is highly toxic as stored in pressurized   cylinders.
The agent is less effective than carbon dioxide.
Halon extinguishes the fire by a chain breaking reaction.
The agent can not be used on electrical fires because it leaves a residue.

text: The person on a ship who is responsible for maintaining the engineering spaces in a clean and sanitary condition is the _____________.
options: senior mechanic, or mechanic on duty if no senior mechanic designated
senior electrician, or electrician on duty if no senior  electrician designated

text: Periodic surveys to renew the load line assignment for a ship must be made at intervals not exceeding ____________.
Halogenated fire extinguishing agents such as Halon 1301 extinguish fires by _____________.
When a rescuer discovers a burn victim in the vicinity of electrical equipment or wiring, his first step is to  ________________.
options: flush water over any burned area of the patient
apply ointment to the burned areas on the patient
shutdown electrical power in the area
remove the patient from the vicinity of the live electrical equipment or wiring

text: Basic emergency care for an electrical burn is to _________.
options: flood the burn with water for two minutes
brush away the charred skin and wrap the burned area
cover the burned area with a clean cloth and transport the patient to a medical facility
apply ointment or spray to the burned area and wrap with a clean cloth

text: A patient has an electrical burn, after checking breathing and pulse, ____________.
options: look for a second burn, which may have been caused by the current exiting the body
locate the nearest water source and flood the burn with  water for five minutes
remove any dirt or charred skin from the area of the burn
apply ointment to the burn area and wrap with clean cloth

text: To prevent the spread of fire by convection ____________.
options: cool the bulkhead around the fire cover the area with foam
remove combustibles from direct exposure cover the area with absorbent material
close all openings to the area wash down immediately with a fire hose
shut off all electrical power wash down immediately with an oil dispersing solvent

text: Dry chemical extinguishing agents extinguish a fire by _____________.
options: cooling the fuel below ignition temperature
smothering and removing the oxygen from the fuel
breaking up the molecular chain reaction
removing the fuel by absorbing the heated vapors

text: With the air supply on, the air pressure in the survival craft will be _____________.
options: changing in relation to the speed of the craft

text: Aboard a survival craft, ether can be used to _____________.
options: start the engine in cold weather

text: If the survival craft is not loaded to full capacity, the personnel should be ______________.
options: loaded more on port side to forward
loaded equally on both sides with more forward
loaded equally on both sides with more aft

text: When an electrical fire has been extinguished with a dry chemical agent, there is always a possibility of the  _________.
options: fire being rekindled by spontaneous combustion
electrical contacts or relays being rendered inoperative
chemical crystals suffocating the fire fighter

text: During an abandonment or drill, the first person to arrive at the survival craft should _____________.
options: pass out food and water to personnel
open the doors and start the sprinkler system
open the doors and prepare the craft for boarding

text: While retrieving the survival craft, the engine should be stopped ____________.
options: when the craft clears the water
when the cable has been attached

text: Small oil spills on deck can be kept from contaminating the surrounding waters by _____________.
options: driving wooden plugs into the vents Acetylene
closing the lids on the vents Lube oil
keeping plugs in the sounding pipes All of the above

text: At a speed of six knots the fuel aboard a survival craft should last ________.
Which of the listed conditions is a symptom of sun stroke?
options: Temperature falls below normal, pulse is rapid and feeble, and skin is cold and clammy.
Temperature is high, pulse is strong and rapid, and skin is hot and dry.
Temperature is high, pulse is slow and feeble, and skin is clammy.
Temperature falls below normal, pulse is rapid, and skin is clammy.

text: The backup system on an electric start survival craft is a _____________.
When a rescue vessel approaches a survival craft in heavy seas, the person in charge of the survival craft should  ______________.
options: tie up to the rescue vessel
transfer only those personnel who are not seasick
wait for calmer weather before transferring personnel

text: The survival craft engine is fueled with _____________.
Which of the following methods should be used to prevent a class "A" fire in a topside compartment, with steel  bulkheads, from spreading to the adjacent compartments?
options: Sweep all exterior surfaces with water water fog until the fire is reportedly extingished.
Sweep all exterior surfaces with a solid stream of water until the fire is reportedly extinguished.
Sweep all exterior surfaces with a solid stream of water until steam is no longer produced, secure the nozzel, and repeat as necessary.
Sweep all exterior surfaces with water fog until steam is no longer produced, secure the nozzle, and repeat as necessary.

text: When retrieving the survival craft, the helmsman should  instruct the crewman to __________.
options: check that hooks are fully locked in place

text: A class "C" fire would occur in _____________.
options: bedding immerse the feet in warm water
pipe insulation warm the feet with a heat lamp
paint warm the feet at room temperature
a generator rub the feet to restore circulation

text: When transferring survivors from a survival craft to a  rescue vessel, personnel onboard the craft should   ____________.
options: remove their lifejackets to make it easier to climb onboard the rescue vessel
climb on top of the survival craft while waiting for their turn to transfer to the rescue vessel
remain seated inside the survival craft and make the  transfer one person at a time
enter the water and swim over to the rescue vessel

text: The ventilation system aboard your vessel has fire dampers restrained by fusible links. Which of the following  statements concerning the fusible links is true?
options: A fusible link will automatically open after a fire is  extinguished and reset the damper.
Fusible links must be replaced at every inspection for  certification.
Fusible links are tested by applying a source of heat to them.
Fusible links must be replaced if a damper is closed.

text: A health hazard, listed on a Material Safety Data Sheet  (MSDS), that can cause cancer in exposed individuals is  called a ________.
Which of the listed methods, is the most effective to fight a fire on the open deck of a vessel if using a dry chemical type fire extinguisher?
options: Approach the fire from the windward side.
Direct the extinguisher discharge at the base of the fire.
Move the discharge stream back and forth in a rapid sweeping motion.

text: Which of the following is least likely to cause the ignition of fuel vapors?
A fire in an electrical generator is considered to be  ___________.
Which of the following statements is NOT true concerning life- jackets?
options: They come in two sizes, child and adult. discharge a stream horizontally and allow it to flow down evenly on all burning surfaces
They are required to be of a highly visible color. discharge a stream at the base of the fire, starting at the near edge, and use side-to-side sweeping motions
The life jackect will always support an unconscious wearer in an upright position. play the stream off adjacent vertical surfaces until the area is blanketed
They must be able to support the wearer in an upright  position. use concentrated amounts in small locations and put the fire out in sections

text: Except in rare cases, it is impossible to extinguish a  shipboard fire by ____________.
If a fire hose is left unattended and under pressure with the nozzle shut off, the fire hose will ____________.
A fire, occurring in the windings, of an overloaded   electrical motor, is considered a ______________.
Fire dampers prevent the fire spreading through the process of ____________.
If emergency welding repairs must be made to the upper areaof a fuel tank, the tank and/or adjacent compartments mayneed to be ____________.
options: all of the above as necessary

text: The flammable limits of methane by volume is 5% to 15%. If the combustible gas indicator reading is 50% when sampling a compartment containing methane, the flammable vapor concentration at the sample point is ____________.
Besides heat, fuel, and oxygen, which of the following is necessary for a fire to exist?

The vapor explosive range of gasoline by volume is 1.0% to 6.0% in air. If a gasoline tank is sampled with an   explosimeter, and the reading obtained is 30% L.E.L., the vapor concentration at the sample point is ____________.
The class of fire on which a blanketing effect is essential to extinguish the fire is ________.
If you have to jump in the water when abandoning ship, your legs should be ____________.
options: spread apart as far as possible
held as tightly against your chest as possible
extended straight down and crossed at the ankles

text: Which of the tools listed is designed for connecting and disconnecting fire hose couplings?
With no alternative but to jump from a vessel, the correct posture should include ____________.
options: holding down the lifejacket against the chest with one arm crossing the other, covering the mouth and nose with a hand, and feet together
knees bent and held close to the body with both arms around the legs
body straight and arms tightly at the sides for feet first entry into the water
both hands holding the lifejacket below the chin with knees bent and legs crossed

text: Which of the following conditions must be met before a  marine chemist will issue a certificate for a compartment to be "safe for workers, safe for hot work"?

standing on the CO2 bottle, holding the bottom straps, and throwing your weight backwards flushed and dry skin

text: A small fire is reported in the forward cargo hold on a  vessel equipped with a combination smoke detection/carbon dioxide fixed fire extinguishing system. After ensuring the space is unoccupied and properly sealed off, you should NEXT ____________.

a man may work safely without breathing apparatus in cold weather as vapors are less volatile

text: The piece of equipment shown in the illustration is used with a fire hose to produce _____________.

To turn over a liferaft that is floating upside down, you should pull on the _____________.
Class "B" EPIRB's transmit on frequencies that are monitored by ______________.
The master or person in charge of a cargoship must record the date of each test of emergency lighting systems, power  systems, the condition of each and the performance of the equipment ____________.
A vessel which is subjected to "hogging" _____________.
options: has its main deck under compressive stress bridge log
has its main deck plating under tensile stress Master's log
has its bottom plate under tensile stress engine room log
has its bottom plating under ductile stress oil record book

text: In order to initiate CPR on a drowning victim, ____________.
options: start chest compressions before the victim is removed from the water
drain water from the lungs before ventilating
do not tilt the head back since it may cause vomiting

text: Which of the following is a correct statement with respect to inserting an airway tube?
options: Only a trained person should attempt to insert an airway tube.
A size #2 airway tube is the correct size for an adult.
The airway tube will not damage the victim's throat.
Inserting the airway tube will prevent vomiting.

text: After using a portable Halon fire extinguisher, it shouldbe ____________.
options: put back in service if more than 50% of the charge remains

text: In a crude oil washing system, according to regulations, each cargo tank shall be fitted with a sufficient number of machines, such that those areas  unwashed by direct impingement shall not exceed 10% for  horizontal areas, and ____
Which of the following conditions can contribute to   accidents?
Which of the following procedures is the generally accepted method of determining whether the atmosphere within a cargo tank is over rich, explosive, or too lean to support  combustion?
options: Use the open flame test on a small sample that has been  taken from the tank.
Send a gas sample ashore for laboratory analysis.
Enter the tank with a teledyne oxygen analyzer.

text: In the absence of external forces, the center of gravity of a floating vessel is located directly above the ___________.
options: geometric center of the displaced volume

text: The sea painter of an inflatable liferaft should be   ____________.
options: free running on deck clean ballast
faked out next to the case dirty ballast
secured to a permanent object on deck via a weak link segregated ballast
stowed near the raft crude oil

text: According to MARPOL Regulations , all ships are required to prepare, submit, and maintain a(an) __________.
Paints and solvents used aboard a vessel should be   __________.
options: stowed safely at the work site until work is completed
returned to the paint locker after each use
covered with a fine mesh screen to protect from ignition sources
continuously kept in closed containers at all times

text: The term "arrival ballast" refers to ___________.
options: any form of sea water ballast

text: When fire pumps are used for purposes other than   firefighting service, each pipe connection to the other  service (except for branch lines used for deck washing) must have a ___________.
options: check valve installed in the line
shut off valve at a manifold near the pump
quick disconnect union within ten feet of the pump
regulator in the line set at 8.8kg/cm2

text: The stability existing after the unintentional flooding of a compartment on a cargo ship is called ______________.
When compared to other fire extinguishing agents, water fog _____________.
options: has the greatest ability to produce foam
will completely remove toxic fumes from the air
will completely remove combustible vapors from the air

text: An initial attempt to extinguish a galley grease fire may be made by using ____________.
The wooden plug fitted tightly in the vent of a damaged tank may prevent the tank from _____________.
options: filling completely vertically above the center of buoyancy
developing free surfaces vertically above the center of gravity
developing free surface moments at the intersection of the upright vertical centerline and the line of action of the buoyant force
collapsing geometric center of the underwater volume

text: A person has suffered a laceration of the arm. Severe  bleeding has been controlled by using a sterile dressing and direct pressure. Which of the following actions should be taken next?
options: Apply a tourniquet to prevent the bleeding from restarting.
Apply a pressure bandage over the dressing.
Remove any small foreign matter and apply antiseptic.
Administer fluids to assist the body in replacing the lost blood.

text: The primary function of an automatic sprinkler system is to ____________.
options: instantaneously extinguish the fire which triggered it
limit the spread of the fire and control the amount of heat produced
protect people in the areas which have had sprinkler heads installed
alert the crew to the fire

text: The volume of a floating vessel above the waterline that can be made watertight is its __________.
Bleeding from a vein may ordinarily be controlled by  ___________.
options: applying direct pressure to the wound
pouring ice water directly onto the wound

text: According to MARPOL regulations,  the shipboard oil pollution emergency plan must include __________.
options: all information ordinarily provided in the oil record book
an explanation and purpose of this plan
a one-line schematic of the plan to be implemented
the operating instructions for any and all oily-water  separators installed aboard the vessel

text: The quality of initial stability is indicated by __________.
Which of the following is the correct procedure to follow when launching an inflatable liferaft by hand from an ocean going vessel?
options: Connect the stainless steel link (float free link) to the vessel.
Pull the line (painter) from the container and make it fast to the cleat provided.
Remove the raft from the container to permit complete  inflation.

text: The angle to which a floating vessel, with a negative initial metacentric height, lies while at rest in still water is the angle of __________.
Blood flowing from a cut artery would appear _____________.
options: dark red with a steady flow
bright red with a steady flow

text: Actuating the fixed CO2 system should cause the automatic shutdown of the ________________.
During counterflooding to correct a severe list or trim  aggravated by an off center load, a cargo ship suddenly takes a list or trim to the opposite side. You should ____________.
options: continue counterflooding in the same direction
continue counterflooding, but in the opposite direction

text: Which abbreviation represents the height of the center of buoyancy?
What is the primary purpose for applying a splint in first aid?
A CO2 fire extinguisher provided aboard  has lost 10% of its charge. It now must be _______________.
A CO2 fire extinguisher must be weighed  ____________.
Which of the listed of fractures would be considered as the most serious?
When positive stability exists, "GZ" represents the _______.
One of the main concerns when fighting a galley fire is__________.
options: spreading of fire through the engineering space
the igniting of a grease fire in the range hood ventilation system

text: The stamped full weight of a 45kgs CO2 cylinder is 142kgs. What is the total minimum weight of the cylinder before it must be recharged?
If the alarm provided in the fixed CO2 system sounds in the engine room, you should _____________.
options: make certain that the CO2 starts flowing into the space
immediately assist the fixed system by discharging all  portable units as well

text: How often are fire hoses required to be tested ?
A Crude Oil Wash (COW) system is considered as ____________.
options: a mechanism which filters and purifies crude oil
a Butterworth system using crude oil instead of water as the cleaning medium
a cleanup for pump room bilges

text: Before releasing CO2 into the machinery space, the alarm for a fixed CO2 fire extinguishing system must sound for at  least ______________.
A compound fracture is a fracture _____________.
options: in which more than one bone is broken
in which the same bone is broken in more than one place
which is accompanied by internal bleeding
which causes external bleeding at the site of the fracture

text: Which of the following statements concerning chemical foam is true?
options: It is formed as a result of the reaction between dry powder and water.
It is recommended for use on fires in main propulsion  electric motors.
It is formed as a result of the rapid mixing of foam  liquid, water, and air.
It is useful in fighting only chemical fires.

text: In a fixed CO2 system, the "pilot" cylinders are used to ____________.
options: direct CO2 to the pilot house

text: To keep injured survivors warm in the water after abandoning a ship, they should _____________.
options: be placed in the middle of a small circle formed by the  other survivors in the water
float on their backs with their arms extended for maximum exposure to the air
remove their lifejackets and hold on to the uninjured  survivors
sip seawater at intervals of fifteen minutes

text: When discharging the main bank of fixed CO2 into a major engine room fire, CO2 pressure is used to _____________.
Flooding of any compartment in a ship, resulting in a  serious loss of reserve buoyancy, will always _____________.
Smoking in bed on a ship is prohibited _______________.
When the height of metacenter is greater than the height of the center of gravity, a vessel has what type of stability?
The lowest temperature at which the vapor formed from a  liquid ignites and continues to burn steadily in the  presence of an ignition source is called the ___________.
When using mechanical foam to fight a bilge fire, the stream of foam is most effective when directed _________.
The sense of smell cannot be depended upon to detect  hydrogen sulfide because _______________.
options: it has a smell similar to petroleum products
the gas is odorless in any concentration
of paralysis to the olfactory nerves
it is better to rely on eye irritation as a detector

text: The effects of free surface on initial stability depend upon the surface area dimensions of the free liquids andthe __________.
options: volume of liquid in the tank
volume of displacement of the ship
location of the tank in the ship
height of the center of gravity of the ship

text: Which of the following statements is true concerningAqueous Film Forming Foam (AFFF)? delay sequence
options: activate the main bank all at once
automatically activate the main bank if an abnormal heat rise is detected

text: If you have to jump into the water when abandoning a ship, your legs should be ____________.
options: spread apart as far as possible
held as tightly against your chest as possible
extended straight down and crossed at the ankles

text: When should the emergency position-indicating radio beacon be activated after abandoning a ship?
options: Only when another vessel is in sight

text: Which of the equipment listed is required to be included in the fireman's (emergency) outfit?
The order to abandon a ship should only be given by the ___________.
If you wear extra clothing when entering the water after abandoning a ship it will ____________.
options: make it more difficult to breathe

text: Fire and lifeboat stations are required to be listed on the _____________.
Reducing the free surface of a liquid within a tank has the effect of lowering the _______________.
options: virtual height of the center of gravity

text: Which of the following is NOT a mandatory requirement part of the shipboard oil pollution emergency plan?
When the wave period and the apparent rolling period of the ship are the same, __________.
Reducing the free surfaces within a vessel, reduces the ____________.
options: AFFF can only be produced from fresh water.
Because AFFF works through surface tension, it cannot be broken up if it is agitated.
AFFF controls the vaporization of flammable liquids by means of a water film.

text: The minimum concentration of hydrogen sulfide that can  result in death if a person is exposed for even an instant is _____________.
Which of the following is a mandatory section of the  shipboard oil pollution emergency plan?
options: list of individuals required to respond

text: Which of the following is NOT required to be provided as part of the appendixes of the Shipboard Oil Pollution  Emergency Plan?
options: a list of agencies or officials of Coastal State    administrations responsible for receiving and processing incident reports
a list of agencies or officials in regularly visited ports.
a list which specifies who will be responsible for informing the parties listed and the priority in which they must be notified.
a list of personnel duty assignments

text: Individuals who have consumed alcohol within 24 hours of exposure to H2S can tolerate ___________.
options: smaller than normal concentrations of H2S
moderate concentrations of H2S without the usual reactions

text: Which of the devices listed below is designed to prevent flames from entering through fuel tank vents?
Which of the following operations aboard a tanker must be recorded in the Oil Record Book on a tank-to-tank basis  according to MARPOL Regulations?
options: Any internal transfer of oil cargo during a voyage.
The discharge overboard in port or at sea of any bilge water accumulated in machinery spaces.
The loading or unloading of any or all oil cargo.

text: After transferring a weight forward on a vessel, the draft at the center of flotation will ____________.
options: change, depending on the location of the LCG

text: According to Regulations , a bulkhead capable of preventing the passage of smoke and flame for one hour would be classified as a/an ____________.
options: D-60 uncorrected height of the center of gravity

text: While you are fighting a fire in a smoke-filled compartment one of your shipmates falls sustaining a severe laceration and ceases breathing. Your FIRST response should be to  __________.
The lethal concentration of hydrogen sulfide that will  result in death with short-term exposure is a minimum of _____________.
Which of the listed fixed fire extinguishing systems is  most effective for use in the cargo holds?
A man has suffered a burn on his arm characterized by  reddening of the skin, blistering, and swelling. This is an example of what kind of burn?
Part of the required equipment for a lifeboat includes _____________.
An explosive mixture containing air with a maximum   concentration of vapor, is capable of ignition when it is at the ____________.
The Oil Record Book must be maintained onboard the vessel for ______________.
options: not less than 3 years and be readily available for   inspection
6 months and then submitted to the nearest Marine Safety Office for review
the duration of the ship's active employment

text: The average of the observed drafts is known as ____________.
options: mean draft immerse the burn in cold water
mean of the calculated drafts apply petroleum jelly
draft at the center of flotation apply a bandage to exclude air

text: An inflatable liferaft should be lifted back aboard the ship by using ____________.
options: the single hook at the top of the raft
two lines passed under the raft

text: On a ship, how many ring buoys are required to have a  buoyant line attached?
options: One ring life buoy on each side of the MODU.
Two ring life buoys on each side of the MODU.

text: The spread of fire can be prevented by ____________.
options: cooling surfaces adjacent to the fire
removing combustibles from the endangered area

text: In the absence of external forces, adding weight to one side of a floating vessel, will cause the vessel to ____________.
options: heel until the angle of loll is reached
list until the center of buoyancy is aligned vertically with the center of gravity
trim to the side opposite TCG until all moments are equal
decrease draft at the center of flotation

text: Each emergency light on a ship must be marked with ________.
options: an arrow pointing to the nearest exit

text: With no environmental forces acting on the vessel, the  center of gravity of an inclined vessel is vertically  aligned with the ______________.
With no environmental forces present, the center of gravity of an inclined vessel is vertically aligned with the  ____________.
Halon 1301 enters the fire area as a _____________.
The most effective first aid treatment for chemical burns is to immediately ____________.
options: apply ointment to burned area decreases
flood the affected area with water ______________. remains the same

text: A victim has suffered a second degree burn to a small area of the lower arm. Which of the following statements  represents the proper treatment for this injury?
options: Immerse the arm in cold water for 1 to 2 hours, apply burn ointment, and bandage.
Open any blisters with a sterile needle, apply burn   ointment, and bandage.
Apply burn ointment, remove any foreign material, and insure that nothing is in contact with the burn.
Immerse the arm in cold water for 1 to 2 hours, open any blister, and apply burn ointment.

text: Spontaneous combustion is most likely to occur in _________.
A branch line valve of a fixed fire extinguishing system on a ship must be marked with the ____________.
options: maximum pressure allowed at that branch
name of the space or spaces which it serves
date of the last maintenance inspection
pressure needed to maintain an effective stream at that  point

text: Each completed page of the Oil Record Book must be signed by the ____________.
In treating a person for extensive first or second degree thermal burns it is important to prevent or reduce   _____________.
For each passenger vessel, the minimum number of immersion suits to be carried for each lifeboat is at least ____________.
How are line shaft bearings usually lubricated?
If the metacentric height is large, a floating vessel will ______________.
options: have a slow and easy motion

text: Which of the following statements is true concerning the Halon 1301 fire extinguishing agent?
options: The agent is highly toxic at normal room temperature.
The agent by weight is more effective than CO2.
apply an ice pack to the burned area

text: A fire in a fuel oil settling tank is a ____________.
Each distress signal and self-activated smoke signal must be replaced not later than the marked date of expiration, or from the date of manufacture, not later than ___________.
Which of the following would be considered a Class "B" fire?
options: Waste rag fire in the engine room
Oil fire in the engine room bilges

text: If a person is unconscious as a result of an electric shock, you should first remove the victim from the electrical  source and then ____________.
options: check for serious burns on the body

text: The master or person-in-charge of a MODU is required to  submit a casualty report of an intentional grounding when ______________.
options: it creates a hazard to the vessel
it will last longer than 24 hours
it will last longer than 48 hours

text: Which of the listed precautions should be taken when testing a line throwing gun?
options: Never remove the line from the rocket.
Fire it at an angle of approximately 90 degrees to the  horizon.

text: Who is charged with appointing persons to be in command of the lifeboats and/or liferafts on a mobile offshore drilling units?
When abandoning a ship, following the launching of the  survival craft you should _____________.
options: plot a course for the nearest land
take a vote on which direction you should go
go one direction until fuel runs out

text: If help has not arrived in 10-12 hours after having   abandoned a ship in a survival craft, you should __________.
options: go in one direction until the fuel runs out
plot course for the nearest land
take a vote on which direction you should go
shutdown the engines and set the sea anchor The agent can not be used on electrical fires as it leaves a residue.

text: If fuel oil is burned from the port side tanks alone, the ship will _____________.
If a line shaft bearing begins to overheat the shaft speed should be reduced. If overheating persists, you should then _____________.
options: increase lube oil pressure to the bearing
decrease lube oil pressure to the bearing
apply emergency cooling water externally to the bearing
flood the bearing with a higher viscosity oil to provide emergency lubrication and cooling

text: Convection spreads a fire as a result of ____________.
options: transmitting the heat of a fire through the ship's metal
burning liquids flowing into another space
heated gases flowing through ventilation systems
the transfer of heat across an unobstructed space

text: Fire fighting agents, such as Halon and Purple K, suppress the fire by ______________.
Which of the following substances is to be considered as fuel for a class "B" fire?
You are in a survival craft boadcasting a distress message. What information would be essential to your  rescuers?
options: Your position by latitude and longitude.

text: A self-righting survival craft will return to an upright position provided that all personnel ____________.
options: are seated with seatbelts on and doors shut
are seated with seatbelts on and doors open
are to shift to one side to right it

text: When the scuppers are plugged and an oil spill occurs on deck, you should _____________.
options: remove scuppers and wash fuel overboard with water
use absorbent material, such as sawdust, to clean up the spill
remove scuppers to allow the spill to run overboard and wipe the area dry with rags
remove scuppers and wash the fuel overboard with a solvent

text: Treatment of heat exhaustion should consist of ____________.
options: moving to a shaded area and laying down in parallel
bathing with rubbing alcohol in series
placing patient in a tub of cold water to the detecting cabinet
All the above to the trouble alarm supervising resistor

text: In the event of a small bunker oil spill on deck occurring while fueling, you should _____________.
options: wash down the area immediately with a fire hose
wash down the area with kerosene
cover the area with absorbent material

text: An "on-load" release system on a survival craft means the cable can be released ______________.
options: only when the load is taken off the cable
only there is a load on the cable
only when activated by the controls at the lowering station

text: Treatment of sunstroke consists principally of ____________.
options: cooling, removing to shaded area and having the victim lay down

text: Fire hoses located at protected fire stations, must always be _____________.
options: capped on the ends for protection
supplied with a smooth bore nozzle
open to the air to prevent rot

text: Which of the conditions listed is necessary for a substance to burn?
options: The temperature of the substance must be equal to or above its fire point.
The air must contain oxygen in sufficient quantity.
The mixture of vapors with air must be within the "explosive range."

text: The MAIN objection to the use of a dry chemical fire  extinguisher on an electrical fire is that the __________.
options: extinguishing action is not as good as with soda acid
powder conducts electricity back to the fire fighter
dry chemical leaves a powder residue which may render  electrical equipment inoperative
extinguisher will need to be recharged

text: Limit switches are located on the survival craft winch  systems  to ____________.
options: stop the winch just before the craft reaches final stowage position
limit the amount of cable on the drum
stop the winch in case the craft weighs too much

text: All ship's personnel should be familiar with the survival  craft __________.
With the sprinkler system and air system on, and all hatches shut, the survival craft will be protected from ___________. the _____________.
options: male and female ends connected together to prevent damage
female end available to be quickly connected to the hydrant
male end attached to the adjacent fire hydrant
nozzle end arranged to be easily run out to the fire

text: The source of power for the CO2 alarm is obtained from ___________.
options: the emergency power and lighting bus
the general alarm 24 volt DC bus
the flow of CO2 under pressure

text: Small oil spills on deck can be prevented from contaminating any waters by _____________.
options: placing floating booms around the ship

text: In a cartridge-operated dry chemical type fire extinguisher, when the CO2 cartridge is activated, the dry chemical is released from the extinguisher _____________.
options: with the squeeze grip trigger on top of the container
by squeezing the control valve carrying handle
by turning the activating handle on the bottom of the  container
with the squeeze-grip on/off nozzle at the end of the hose

text: When a helicopter is lifting personnel from a survival  craft, the other individuals in the craft should __________.
options: enter the water in case the person being lifted slips from the sling
stand on the outside of the craft to assist the person being lifted
remove their lifejackets to prepare for their transfer to the helicopter
remain seated inside the craft to provide body weight for stability

text: The sprinkler system of a survival craft is used to   ____________.
options: cool the craft in a fire
spray oil on the sea to calm it

text: The scuppers should be plugged ____________.
options: only if fixed containment is not used
only if portable containment is not used
only if fixed containment drains are open
whenever the vessel is being fueled

text: Physical exertion on the part of a person who has fallen into cold water can ___________.
options: be the best thing to try if there were no rescue in sight
increase survival time in the water
increase the rate of heat loss from the body
not affect the heat loss from the body

text: Which of the following statements concerning fire hose  couplings is true?
options: Fire hose couplings are strong and not easily damaged.
Fire hose couplings must be lubricated with oil.
Fire hose couplings can be easily damaged by dropping.
Fire hose couplings should be painted red.

text: A class "C" fire is burning _____________.
options: a drop greater than ten feet

text: Treatment of frostbite is to _________.
On a cargo vessel, fire and boat drills must be held within24 hours of leaving port if the percentage of the crew  replaced is more than ____________.
When the cotton cover of a fire hose becomes oily or greasy, it should be washed with a solution of mild soapy   freshwater and _____________.
When compared to low-expansion foam, a high-expansion foam will ____________.
The most effective warming treatment for a crew member  suffering from hypothermia is ____________.
options: running or jumping to create heat

text: If the threads and gasket of a fire hose coupling are in good condition, the minimum mechanical advantage necessary for making a watertight connection to another hose coupling, can be developed with ______________.
When compared to a high-expansion foam, a low-expansion foam will ____________.
Which of the following statements is TRUE concerning lifejackets?
options: Buoyant vests may be substituted for lifejackets.
Lifejackets are designed to turn an unconscious person's face clear of the water. magnesium
Lifejackets must always be worn with the same side  facing outwards to float properly. dunnage
Lightly stained or faded lifejackets will fail in the water and should not be used. electrical insulation

text: When using a dry chemical fire extinguisher to fight a fuel oil fire, you should ___________.
options: attack the fire from the leeward side
direct the chemical discharge into the center of the burning fuel surface
sweep the chemical discharge back and forth across the  burning fuel surface
invert the dry chemical extinguisher before starting to  fight the fire

text: The most likely location for a liquid cargo fire to occur on a tanker would be ____________. 0.05
Fusible link fire dampers are operated by ____________.
options: a mechanical arm outside the vent duct
the heat of a fire melting the link

text: A burning AC motor would be considered what class of fire?
When using a dry chemical type fire extinguisher in a windy location, you should direct the stream from _____________.
A fire and boat drill on a tank vessel shall, by regulation, include ____________.
options: bringing all rescue and safety equipment from the emergency equipment lockers
demonstrations of rescue and safety equipment by designated persons

text: Which of the listed classes of fire is considered as one involving electrical equipment?
Fires are grouped into which of the listed categories?
options: Class A, B, C, and D All watertight doors which are in use while the vessel is underway shall be operated.
Type 1, 2, 3, and 4 All lifeboat equipment shall be examined.
Combustible solids, liquids, and gases Fire pumps shall be started and all exterior outlets opened.
Flammable solids, liquids, and gases All of the above.

text: If the liferaft you are in should capsize, all personnel should leave the raft and ____________.
options: right the raft using the righting straps

text: Lifesaving equipment shall be stowed so that it will be ____________.
options: on the topmost deck of the vessel at all times

text: A reproductive health hazard, listed on a Material Safety Date Sheet (MSDS), that can cause genetic changes in sperm or egg cells is called a ___________.

The flammable limits of benzene are 1.4% to 8.0% by volume in air. If a reading of 50% of the lower explosive limit isobtained, using the meter shown in the illustration, thevapor concentration at the sample point is __________.
Which of the following procedures is NOT recommended when it is necessary to swim through an oil fire?
options: Wear as much clothing as possible.
Enter the water feet first and legs together.
Swim in the same direction as the wind is blowing.
Cover your eyes with one hand when entering the water.

text: The function of the lifeboat davit limit switch is to  ____________.
options: control the descent rate of a lifeboat
control the ascent rate of a lifeboat
cut off power to the winch when the lifeboat nears the final stowed position
cut off power to the winch when the lifeboat reaches the davit bumpers

text: Provided every effort is used to produce, as well as  preserve body moisture content by avoiding perspiration, how long is it normally possible to survive without stored  quantities of water?
A class "D" fire would be considered as burning __________.
options: wood within permissible concentration, and dangerous gases are either present, or may be produced by residues, what would be the safety designation listed on a marine chemist's certificate? Safe for Men - Safe for Fire
Safe for Men - Not Safe for Fire
Not Safe for Men - Safe for Fire
Not Safe for Men - Not Safe for Fire

text: When using the combustible gas indicator to test a cargo tank, and the hydrocarbon content of the atmosphere exceeds the upper explosive limit (UEL), _____________.
options: the needle will move to zero and stay there
the needle will move to the maximum reading and stay there
the needle will move to the maximum reading and return to zero immediately
an audible beeping will be heard

text: An oil fire would be classified as a _________.

A compartment is tested and found to contain 20.8% oxygen by volume, permissible concentrations of toxic materials, and concentrations of flammable gas just below the lower  flammable limit. If the residues are not capable of  producing toxic materials or
options: Safe for men - Safe for Fire
Safe for men - Not Safe for Fire
Not safe for men - Safe for Fire
Not safe for men - Not Safe for Fire

text: After the lifeboat has reached the top of the davit heads, the davit arms begin moving up the tracks until the movement is stopped by the ____________.
If ignited, which of the listed materials would be a class "B" fire?
To determine whether or not an adult victim has a pulse, the rescuer should check for the pulse at the ___________.
You will find a limit switch on a ____________.

The flammable limits of kerosene is 0.7% to 6.0% by volume in air. If a tank having contained kerosene is tested with the combustible gas indicator, shown in the illustration, and a reading of 50% is obtained, this means _____________.
options: insufficient oxygen exists in the space to support life

text: An unconscious person should NOT be _____________.
options: placed in a position with the head lower than the body

text: When a helicopter is lifting personnel from an enclosed  lifeboat, the other individuals in the boat should   ____________.
options: enter the water in case the person being lifted slips from the sling
stand on the outside of the boat to assist the person being lifted
remove their lifejackets to prepare for their transfer to the helicopter
remain seated inside to provide body weight for stability

text: The reading of a combustible gas indicator indicates the percentage of the _____________.
options: concentration of flammable gas in a compartment
lower explosive limit of a flammable gas concentration
upper explosive limit of a flammable gas concentration
concentration by weight of nonflammable gas in a compartment

text: One of the disadvantages of using carbon dioxide to   extinguish a fire in an enclosed space is _____________.
options: the "snow" which is sometimes discharged along with the gas is toxic
prolonged exposure to high concentrations of CO2 gas causes suffocation
rapid dissipation of the CO2 vapor
the CO2 gas is lighter than air and a large amount is  required to extinguish a fire near the deck

text: A good approach to personnel safety is to assume that all tanks and pumprooms are potentially dangerous. You should immediately leave an area whenever _______________.
options: you have irritation of the eyes, nose, or throat
you experience an abnormal bodily sensation
any of the above conditions occur

text: When testing the atmosphere of a compartment with a combustible gas indicator, the operator must remember that _____________.
options: most petroleum vapors are lighter than air and accumulate around deck longitudinals
the indicator guarantees a safe atmosphere for a maximum of two hours
the meter scale indicates only the upper explosive range
the indicator tests only the atmosphere at the end of the suction tube

text: When any fire has been extinguished with carbon dioxide, there is always the danger of the _____________.
options: fire being reignited by residual heat
dry ice crystals clogging the machinery
atmosphere containing a dangerous charge of static   electricity

text: When operating gravity davits, ____________.
options: the gripes should be released after the boat is moving
the sample is below the lower explosive limit

options: smoke density has been decreased sufficiently to see  the bulkhead opposite the compartment's entrance
a lifeline and explosion proof flash light are    used
all smoke and toxic fumes are removed and an adequate  oxygen supply is present

text: According to Regulations , pumproom  ventilation on a tanker may be accomplished by _____________.
options: steam or air actuated gas ejectors

text: The most important characteristic of a fire extinguishing agent to be used on electrical fires is for the agent to be _____________.
To extinguish an electrical fire, you should use __________.
A continuous sounding of the vessel's whistle, supplemented by a continuous ringing of the general alarm bells for a period of not less than 10 seconds, is the signal for  ____________.

If you hear a continuous blast of the whistle for a period of not less than 10 seconds, supplemented by a continuous ringing of the general alarm bells for not less than 10  seconds, you should go to your _____________.

While in a foreign port, burning and welding repairs are to be carried out on a section of heating coil located in a tank having last contained a grade "D" product. Which of the following procedures should be followed when a certified marine chemist is no
options: It is solely the chief engineer's responsibility to ensure all safety precautions are observed, and all entries are to be made in the engine room log.
The repair work cannot be done at this time due to the lack of the chemist's certificate.
Prior to any hot work, an inspection must be made by the senior officer present, and an entry made in the official logbook.
Repairs should proceed as scheduled since a chemist's  certificate is not required for that type of work.

text: Servicing of a cartridge-operated dry chemical fire   extinguisher aboard ship would include ensuring that the ____________.
options: pressure gage is within the operating range
powder is not caked and there is a full charge
cartridge weight is not less than 1/4 ounce of weight  stamped on cartridge
recharge of weight loss exceeds 10% of the weight of charge

text: Ships are less stable when water or fuel tanks are   partially filled because of the ____________.
Regulations require electric hand  lamps, approved for use in fuel tanks, to be battery powered and _____________.
Vessel stability can be adversely affected by ________.
When you hear three short blasts on the ship's whistle and the same signal on the general alarm bells, you ___________.
options: are required to be at your liferaft
should point to the man overboard

text: To initially combat a fire the ventilation to a compartmentshould be secured to ____________.
options: allow the exhaust fans to remove the smoke
lower the carbon monoxide smothering capabilities
reduce the amount of available oxygen to the fire
protect the fire fighters from smoke

text: The advantage of using a dry chemical fire extinguishing agent is _____________.
options: permanent extinguishment regardless of the reignition  sources

text: Any liquid with a flash point above 26.7°C is a __________.
What is the usual effect of moving weight from the main deck to a position lower in the vessel?
options: The stability of the vessel is increased.
The trim of the vessel is changed substantially.
The stability of the vessel is significantly decreased.
The vessel will list in proportion to the volume of the  cargo shifted.

text: Which of the listed conditions will occur to the ship's  center of gravity if 200 tons of steel is transferred to the ships cargo hold from shore side?
options: The center of gravity will remain in the same position.
The center of gravity will be lowered.
The reserve buoyancy will remain the same.

text: A vessel's center of gravity is lowered when the __________.
If deck cargo is carried, it should be stowed so that it _____________.
Regulations stipulate that in addition to emergency signals, muster lists must specify_____________.
options: actions to be taken by persons on board when each signal is sounded
the order to abandon the vessel
the duties assigned to different members of the crew

text: Following an engine room bilge fire, believed to be   extinguished, and properly fought using a fixed CO2 fire extinguishing system; the investigating team should reenter the closed space ____________.
options: no sooner than 15 minutes after the fire is believed  extinguished
via the lowest access door and equipped with SCBA's no  sooner than 30 minutes after the fire is believed   extinguished
via the highest access door and equipped with SCBA's

text: An insulating flange should be used in a cargo hose   connection instead of a bonding wire _____________.
options: when static electricity may be generated
when the terminal is equipped with a cathodic protection system

text: Bonding cables are used to reduce the possibility of  accidental spark discharge when _____________.
options: transferring flammable liquids to or from a vessel
transferring dry mud to or from a vessel

text: Any liquid which gives off flammable vapors at or below 26.7°C, as determined by flash point from an open cup tester, describes a _____________.
Which of the following statements is true concerning Halon 1301 fire extinguishing equipment?
options: The agent is highly toxic at normal room temperature.
For portable extinguishers, the agent is only available in a 2½ pound unit.
Halon extinguishes the fire by smothering action.
The agent can not be used on electrical fires because it leaves a residue.

text: Which of the listed effects will occur when an operating diesel engine ingests Halon 1301?
options: The high engine combustion temperatures liquefy the Halon.
The engine absorbs the Halon into its cooling system.
The engine decomposes the ingested Halon.
